no sample cuboid in any binary; no scene composition; no camera control; no mouse handling; no keyboard input; no surface bring-up that requires a real Window in CI; no depth buffer / depth attachment added to LitMeshPipeline (existing depth_stencil: None preserved; one cuboid is fine without depth); no real UVs (placeholder [0.0, 0.0] is the v0 contract; documented and stop); no multiple-object scene composition; no selection / highlight / picking integration on the gfx side; no construction of a real winit Window in any test (structural / compile / no_run doctest only for SurfaceContext); no new ADR / lint / doctrine doc / FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md modification; no PLAN / IMPLEMENTATION / fileandfolderstructure / versions modification; no deps beyond rge-brep-render and winit (no nalgebra, no extra wgpu features, no async runtime, no eyre/anyhow); no cargo +nightly fmt --all reformat (used cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-gfx once mid-dispatch on the new test file's trailing-format breaks). **Files modified (within MAY-list)**: NEW crates/gfx/src/surface.rs (~273L); crates/gfx/src/context.rs (+16 lines for adapter() accessor only); crates/gfx/src/lib.rs (pub mod surface; + pub use surface::SurfaceContext; alphabetically; no removals); crates/gfx/src/lit_mesh_pipeline.rs (added LitMesh::from_render_mesh method + 6 unit tests in existing mod tests); NEW crates/gfx/tests/render_mesh_smoke.rs (~214L); crates/gfx/Cargo.toml (rge-brep-render + winit production deps); Status.md + change.md + HANDOFF.md; Cargo.lock (transitive resolution). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: cargo build --workspace -j 2 0 errors / 0 new warnings; cargo test -p rge-gfx --all-targets --no-fail-fast PASS (gfx 107 -> 115 tests; +8 net); cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-fail-fast -j 2 = **2287 / 2287 pass** across 244 binaries (was 2279 / 243; +8 net workspace; +1 binary delta for the new test file; 3 ignored unchanged); cargo +nightly fmt --check exit 0 (after one mid-session `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-gfx` apply on the new test file's trailing-format breaks); cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS — [forbidden-dep] PASS (0 violations) with the new gfx -> brep-render and gfx -> winit edges; substantive lint exemption count unchanged at 0; failure-class rollout-debt count unchanged at 30). **Public API delta** (`rge-gfx`): + surface::SurfaceContext struct + SurfaceContext::new / ::resize / ::surface / ::config methods + surface::SurfaceError enum + crate-root re-export `pub use surface::SurfaceContext;` + LitMesh::from_render_mesh inherent method + GfxContext::adapter() accessor. **Honest framing**: sub-delta.1.A ships ONLY the gfx-side primitives — the SurfaceContext wrapper and the RenderMesh -> LitMesh adapter. It does NOT bring up rendering on screen — no editor-shell modification, no rge-editor binary modification, no winit Window creation. After this dispatch lands, sub-delta.1.B's job is wiring (editor-shell extension + rge-editor binary + sample cuboid + manual visual verification). The visual moment ("first triangle on screen") is one dispatch away. **Workspace state**: **2287 tests / 9 enforcement + 1 supplementary lints PASS / fmt clean / 0 substantive lint exemptions / failure-class rollout-debt 30/81 (51 cleared)**.

2026-05-10 (editor-shell + rge-editor sub-delta.1.B - first triangle on screen, integration / wiring dispatch) - bounded single-agent integration dispatch in A:\RCAD\RGE\. **+5 net workspace tests** = 5 unit tests in crates/editor-shell/src/camera.rs::tests for EditorCameraState (editor_camera_state_default_eye_at_3_3_3 / editor_camera_view_proj_identity_aspect_produces_finite_matrix / editor_camera_view_proj_aspect_change_affects_x_axis_only / editor_camera_to_camera_view_threads_viewport_size / editor_camera_view_proj_consistent_with_camera_view_screen_center). The fifth piece of the Render-backed face-selection chapter (active 2026-05-10) — **pure integration / wiring** dispatch, no new substrate types beyond a thin CPU camera-intent holder. Composes the prior chapter primitives (RenderMesh, CameraView, render_mesh_for, SurfaceContext, LitMesh::from_render_mesh, LitMeshPipeline) into the visual moment "first triangle on screen". **Manual visual verification** (running `cargo run -p rge-editor` and inspecting that a single Lambert+Phong-shaded cuboid appears against a dark gray background) is the **load-bearing test** for this dispatch and is the **user's responsibility**; the agent did NOT run the binary because there is no display in CI. **Phase 1 — EditorCameraState struct + Default + view_proj + to_camera_view** in crates/editor-shell/src/camera.rs (added alongside the existing CameraView from sub-beta; both re-exported at crate root via `pub use camera::{CameraView, EditorCameraState}`). Default places the camera at `(3, 3, 3)`, looking at the world origin with `+Y` up, `fov_y = π/4`, near 0.1, far 100.0 — three faces of a 1×1×1 cuboid at the origin (+X / +Y / +Z) are visible from this vantage point with a directional light from `(-1, -1, -1)`, producing the Lambert+Phong shading variation that visual verification looks for. `view_proj(aspect)` uses `Mat4::look_at_rh` + `Mat4::perspective_rh` (wgpu / Vulkan / D3D NDC convention LOAD-BEARING preserved per sub-beta — Z ∈ [0.0, 1.0]; NOT the `_gl` variants which use Z ∈ [-1.0, +1.0]). `to_camera_view([w, h])` composes EditorCameraState into sub-beta's CameraView for the eventual sub-delta.2 mouse-pick flow (sub-delta.1.B does not exercise it; the method is shipped together because editor-runtime camera intent and the screen-ray bridge are siblings in the same module). **Phase 2 — 13 new fields on EditorShell** in crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs (all `Option<…>` except `editor_camera`): `editor_camera: EditorCameraState`, `cad_world: Option<KernelWorld>`, `projection: Option<CadProjection>`, `cad_graph: Option<CadGraph>` (held for sub-delta.2 picker — the picker takes `&OperatorGraph` which `cad_graph.graph()` produces; pre-staging here avoids a constructor-shape change in the next dispatch — annotated `#[allow(dead_code, reason = "held for sub-δ.2 picker; not consumed in sub-δ.1.B")]`), `cad_entity: Option<KernelEntityId>`, `window: Option<Arc<Window>>`, `gfx_ctx: Option<GfxContext>`, `surface_ctx: Option<SurfaceContext>`, `pipeline: Option<LitMeshPipeline>`, `gfx_camera: Option<rge_gfx::Camera>`, `material: Option<Material>`, `light: Option<DirectionalLight>`, `cuboid_mesh: Option<LitMesh>`. All existing call sites of `EditorShell::new` / `EditorShell::with_world` see byte-identical W03 PIE behaviour: with no `cad_world` attached, `init_render_state` bails fast and the existing PlayState gating, WorldSnapshot capture/restore, AuditLedger plumbing run unchanged. **All previously-passing tests remain green** (90 / 90 in editor-shell; was 85 → +5 from EditorCameraState unit tests; rest of the editor-shell tests untouched). **Phase 3 — `EditorShell::with_world_projection_graph(cad_world: KernelWorld, projection: CadProjection, cad_graph: CadGraph) -> Self`** constructor for rge-editor's scene-load path. Captures the single `BRepHandle`-carrying entity by `world.query::<BRepHandle>().next()`; panics if zero or more-than-one match (sub-delta.1.B is single-cuboid only — multi-entity rendering is a later dispatch and the panic is the substrate-honest contract). **Phase 4 — `init_render_state(&mut self, &ActiveEventLoop)`** helper. Composes (in order): `event_loop.create_window(WindowAttributes::default().with_title("RGE Editor").with_inner_size(LogicalSize::new(1024, 768)))` → `Arc::new(window)` → `GfxContext::new_headless()` → `SurfaceContext::new(&ctx, Arc::clone(&window))` → `Camera::new(&ctx)` + `Camera::update(view_proj(initial_aspect), Mat4::IDENTITY)` → `Material::new(&ctx, &WHITE_1X1_RGBA, 1, 1)` (1×1 white texture; default phong factors `(0.1, 1.0, 0.5, 32.0)`) → `DirectionalLight::new(&ctx)` + `light.update(direction = (-1,-1,-1).normalize(), color = Vec3::ONE)` → `LitMeshPipeline::new(&ctx, camera_layout, light_layout, material_layout, surface_ctx.config().format)` (color format negotiated by surface — typically `Bgra8UnormSrgb` on Win32/Vulkan/Metal) → `projection.render_mesh_for(entity, &cad_world)` → `LitMesh::from_render_mesh(&ctx, &render_mesh)` → `window.request_redraw()` to kick off the first frame. All errors map to `String` and are logged via `tracing::error!` in `resumed`; the existing W03 ready banner still emits afterwards. **Phase 5 — `render_frame()`** helper (called from `WindowEvent::RedrawRequested`). Acquires the next surface texture via `wgpu::CurrentSurfaceTexture` enum match (wgpu 29 changed `get_current_texture` from `Result` to enum: `Success(SurfaceTexture)` / `Suboptimal(SurfaceTexture)` lift the texture; `Timeout` / `Occluded` / `Outdated` / `Lost` / `Validation` cause a `tracing::warn!` + `request_redraw` skip — verified against wgpu-29.0.3/src/api/surface_texture.rs:55). Begins a single render pass with `LoadOp::Clear(wgpu::Color { r: 0.12, g: 0.12, b: 0.14, a: 1.0 })` (dark neutral gray), `depth_stencil_attachment: None` (depth buffer AVOIDED — see Phase 7). Sets `pipeline()` + binds `Camera` at `@group(0)` + `DirectionalLight` at `@group(1)` + `Material` at `@group(2)`, sets vertex/index buffers (the cuboid mesh is always indexed per `LitMesh::from_render_mesh` → `LitMesh::from_indexed`), `draw_indexed(0..36, 0, 0..1)`. Submits, presents, schedules next redraw. **Phase 6 — `resize_render_path(new_w, new_h)`** helper (called from `WindowEvent::Resized`). Skips on `0×0` (mobile minimisation no-op); calls `surface_ctx.resize(&gfx_ctx, w, h)` to reconfigure; recomputes `view_proj` from the new aspect ratio and uploads to the camera UBO via `Camera::update`. **Phase 7 — Depth buffer status: AVOIDED** (preferred per dispatch spec). The existing `LitMeshPipeline::new` declares `depth_stencil: None`; sub-delta.1.B preserves that. For a single Lambert+Phong-shaded cuboid: back-face triangles compute Lambert dot ≤ 0 (the directional light from `(-1,-1,-1)` lights the +X / +Y / +Z faces; the −X / −Y / −Z faces fall to ambient-only ≈ `(0.1, 0.1, 0.1) × white` per the default phong factors), and the camera at `(3, 3, 3)` looking at origin has the front-facing triangles drawn over the back-facing ones in submission order. **No `gfx` modification was required** — staying within the strict "no gfx modification" non-goal. **Phase 8 — `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` full rewrite** (from 5-line stub to ~85L). Builds the cuboid scene from scratch using the same construction pattern as `crates/cad-projection/tests/face_picking_smoke.rs` and `crates/editor-shell/tests/camera_picker_smoke.rs`: `CadGraph::new()` → `begin_operation()` → `add_operator(OperatorNode::Cuboid(CuboidOp { width: 1.0, height: 1.0, depth: 1.0 }))` → `set_root(...)` → `commit("rge-editor demo cuboid")` → `CadProjection::new()` → `World::new()` → `world.register_snapshot_component::<BRepHandle>()` → `projection.spawn_brep_entity(&mut world, cuboid_node)` → mutate `BRepHandle.brep_owner = Some(BRepOwnerId::from_bytes([0x42; 16]))` → `projection.tick(&mut world, &graph, Tolerance::new(0.001).expect("tol"))` → `EditorShell::with_world_projection_graph(world, projection, graph)` → `EventLoop::new().run_app(&mut shell)`. Uses `tracing_subscriber::fmt::try_init()` (best-effort; wrapped in `drop(...)` per clippy `let_underscore_drop`). `ExitCode::SUCCESS` on clean exit; `ExitCode::FAILURE` on `run_app` error. **Layering verification (rule-6-checked)**: editor-shell is editor-tier (NOT in `RENDERER_CRATES`), so `forbidden-dep` rule 6 (RENDERER_CRATES → game-domain forbidden) does NOT fire; editor → renderer is permitted. 4 new dep edges on editor-shell (`rge-gfx` + `rge-brep-render` + `wgpu` production deps; `rge-cad-core` promoted from dev-dep to production for the `CadGraph` ownership in `with_world_projection_graph`'s signature) and 6 deps on rge-editor (`rge-editor-shell` + `rge-cad-core` + `rge-cad-projection` + `rge-kernel-ecs` + `winit` + `tracing-subscriber`). All pass `[forbidden-dep] PASS (0 violations)`. The dispatch's PRECONDITION step (run lint check BEFORE writing impl) confirmed this empirically; if the lint had fired, the dispatch would have stopped and reported back rather than writing impl code. **HARD NON-GOALS observed during shaping** (substrate IS the resistance): no `gfx` source modification (depth buffer was avoided → no `LitMeshPipeline` change required); no `brep-render` / `cad-core` / `cad-projection` / `editor-state` / `editor-ui` / `components-*` / `gfx-ir` / `material-runtime` source modification; no picker / `RenderMesh` / `LitMesh::from_render_mesh` / `SurfaceContext` / `CameraView` / `editor_state::FaceSelection` modification; no mouse event handling, click-to-select, picking integration, selection highlight, gizmos, viewport widgets (sub-delta.2 onwards); no camera controls (orbit / pan / zoom / fly-cam / keyboard); no multiple-object scene composition (single hardcoded cuboid only); no editor UI panels, dock, menus, egui integration; no animation, time-based update, frame timing logic beyond winit's redraw schedule; no asset loading, hot reload, file watching; no headless-mode toggle for rge-editor (always opens a window); no ADRs / lints / doctrine docs / FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md modification; no PLAN / IMPLEMENTATION / fileandfolderstructure / versions edits; no `HashMap<EntityId, LitMesh>` mesh cache or any multi-entity rendering scaffold (single `Option<LitMesh>` per chapter scope); no deps beyond the MAY-list (no nalgebra, no rayon, no tokio, no eyre/anyhow, no pollster — `GfxContext::new_headless` already wraps pollster internally so editor-shell does not need a direct pollster dep). **Files modified (within MAY-list)**: crates/editor-shell/Cargo.toml (added `rge-gfx` + `rge-brep-render` + `wgpu` production deps; promoted `rge-cad-core` from dev-dep to production); crates/editor-shell/src/lib.rs (`pub use camera::{CameraView, EditorCameraState};` widened from `pub use camera::CameraView`); crates/editor-shell/src/camera.rs (added `EditorCameraState` struct + `Default` + `view_proj` + `to_camera_view` impl + 5 unit tests); crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs (new imports for rge_cad_core::CadGraph + rge_cad_projection::{BRepHandle, CadProjection} + rge_gfx::* + rge_kernel_ecs::{EntityId, World} + std::sync::Arc + winit::dpi::LogicalSize + winit::window::{Window, WindowAttributes}; 13 new fields on `EditorShell`; `with_world` extended to initialise all new `Option<…>` fields to `None`; NEW `with_world_projection_graph` constructor; NEW `init_render_state` / `render_frame` / `resize_render_path` helpers; `resumed` calls `init_render_state` then logs ready banner; `RedrawRequested` calls `tick_redraw` (existing W03) then `render_frame`; `Resized` calls `viewport.resize` (existing W03) then `resize_render_path`); editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml (added 6 deps); editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs (full rewrite from 5-line stub to ~85L); Status.md + change.md + HANDOFF.md; Cargo.lock (transitive resolution). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo build --workspace -j 2` 0 errors / 0 new warnings; `cargo build -p rge-editor --release` 0 errors; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --all-targets --no-fail-fast` PASS (90 / 90 tests; was 85; +5 net = 5 EditorCameraState unit tests); `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-fail-fast -j 2` = **2292 / 2292 pass** across **244 binaries** (was 2287 / 244; +5 net workspace; no binary delta — the EditorCameraState tests live inline in the existing `camera.rs::tests` module rather than in a new test file; 3 ignored unchanged); `cargo +nightly fmt --check` exit 0 (after one mid-dispatch `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor` apply); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS — `[forbidden-dep] PASS (0 violations)` with the new editor-shell → rge-gfx + editor-shell → rge-brep-render + editor-shell → rge-cad-core production + rge-editor → editor-shell + cad-core + cad-projection + kernel-ecs edges; substantive lint exemption count unchanged at 0; rollout-debt count unchanged at 30). **Public API delta**: (rge-editor-shell) + `EditorCameraState` struct (Debug + Clone + Copy; 6 public fields: eye / target / up / fov_y_radians / near / far) + `EditorCameraState::view_proj(aspect)` method + `EditorCameraState::to_camera_view(viewport_size)` method + `EditorShell::with_world_projection_graph` constructor + 13 new private fields on `EditorShell` (most not part of the public API surface). (rge-editor) + binary now opens a window and renders one cuboid. **Manual verification note**: `cargo run -p rge-editor` was NOT run by the agent (no display in CI environment); the user must run it on their workstation to verify the first-triangle-on-screen visual moment landed. **Honest framing of dispatch scope**: this dispatch ships the visual moment of the chapter — first triangle on screen. It composes existing primitives without shipping new substrate (the only new struct, `EditorCameraState`, is a thin CPU camera-intent holder that defers all view*proj math to glam). The agent's job was wiring + integration; the user's job is visual verification. After the user confirms visually, sub-delta.2 adds mouse-event wiring; sub-epsilon adds selection highlight; sub-zeta closes with end-to-end smoke. **Phase 7 catalog**: still 8 operators (this dispatch operates entirely in editor-shell + rge-editor; cad-core source unchanged). **Workspace state**: **2292 tests / 9 enforcement + 1 supplementary lints PASS / fmt clean / 0 substantive lint exemptions / failure-class rollout-debt 30/81 (51 cleared)**.
2026-05-10 (editor-shell sub-delta.2 - mouse click -> picker -> coord.face_selection, pure-wiring dispatch closing the click->select chain) - bounded single-agent wiring dispatch in A:\RCAD\RGE\. **+4 net workspace tests** = 4 unit tests in crates/editor-shell/src/camera.rs::tests for the new `pick_face_at` free function (`pick_face_at_returns_face_selection_for_screen_center_on_cuboid`, `pick_face_at_returns_none_for_off_axis_screen_pos_far_from_cuboid`, `pick_face_at_returns_none_when_camera_view_unprojection_fails`, `pick_face_at_composes_into_face_selection_set`). The sixth piece of the Render-backed face-selection chapter (active 2026-05-10) - **pure wiring**, no new substrate, no new deps, no new types beyond a single free function (`pick_face_at`) and a single private field (`cursor_pos: Option<[f32; 2]>`). With sub-alpha + sub-beta + sub-gamma + sub-delta.1.A + sub-delta.1.B + sub-delta.2 all in place, the chapter has the **complete user-facing chain except visual feedback** (sub-epsilon): mouse click -> CameraView -> screen_to_world_ray -> pick_face -> FaceSelection -> coord.face_selection. **Phase 1 - `pick_face_at` free function** in crates/editor-shell/src/camera.rs. Public API: `#[must_use] pub fn pick_face_at(camera_view: &CameraView, screen_pos: [f32; 2], projection: &CadProjection, world: &World, graph: &OperatorGraph) -> Option<FaceSelection>`. Composes `CameraView::screen_to_world_ray` (`?` early-return on degenerate camera) + `CadProjection::pick_face` (`?` early-return on no-hit) + `FaceSelection { entity: pick.entity, owner: pick.owner, face_id: pick.face_id }` construction in one call. **Extracted as a free function (NOT an inherent method)** so it is directly testable without constructing a full `EditorShell` (which requires a real winit `Window` - same constraint as sub-delta.1.B). The production-side caller is `EditorShell::handle_left_click` (Phase 4); the test-side callers are the 4 unit tests in `camera.rs::tests` that supply hand-built `CameraView` + `CadProjection` + `World` + `CadGraph` (mirroring the construction pattern from `tests/camera_picker_smoke.rs::build_unit_cuboid` and `crates/cad-projection/tests/face_picking_smoke.rs::build_cuboid`). Re-exported at crate root: `pub use camera::{pick_face_at, CameraView, EditorCameraState}` (the `pick_face_at` re-export is widened from the prior `CameraView, EditorCameraState`-only line). **Phase 2 - `cursor_pos: Option<[f32; 2]>` field on EditorShell** in crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs. Initialised to `None` in both `EditorShell::new` and `EditorShell::with_world_projection_graph` (alongside the existing 13 sub-delta.1.B fields; W03 PIE behaviour byte-identical for callers that do not enter the render path). Tracks the most recent `WindowEvent::CursorMoved.position` in **physical pixels** (winit 0.30 convention; matches `SurfaceConfiguration.width / height` directly so no DPI conversion is needed - documented inline in the field rustdoc). **Phase 3 - `WindowEvent::CursorMoved` and `WindowEvent::MouseInput` arms in EditorShell::window_event**. The existing `_ => {}` catch-all is preserved for unhandled winit events; CursorMoved updates `cursor_pos`; MouseInput dispatches to `handle_left_click` when `state == ElementState::Pressed && button == MouseButton::Left`. The `Released` half of `MouseInput` and all non-left buttons are explicit no-ops in v0 - multi-button gestures, drag-select, hover, mouseover, cursor-leave, double-click are non-goals. **Phase 4 - `EditorShell::handle_left_click` private method** (~70L). Defensive guards no-op when ANY of `cursor_pos` / `surface_ctx` / `projection` / `cad_world` / `cad_graph` is `None`; this matches the sub-delta.1.B `init_render_state` "bail fast when no CAD scene" pattern so the W03 PIE-only test paths stay byte-identical. On the populated path: builds `viewport = [surface.config().width as f32, surface.config().height as f32]`, calls `editor_camera.to_camera_view(viewport)` (sub-delta.1.B existing bridge from `EditorCameraState` to `CameraView`), then calls `pick_face_at(&camera_view, cursor, projection, cad_world, cad_graph.graph())`. The `cad_graph.graph()` accessor returns `&OperatorGraph` per the existing `CadGraph::graph(&self) -> &OperatorGraph` API (no new accessor required). Local-variable binding pattern (`let selection = pick_face_at(...)`) ensures the immutable borrows of `self.projection` / `self.cad_world` / `self.cad_graph` end before the mutable `self.coord.face_selection` access, satisfying the borrow checker. **v0 single-select clear-on-miss semantics** (matches Fusion 360 / Onshape / FreeCAD convention): a click clears `coord.face_selection` unconditionally and adds the new selection iff the picker resolves a hit. Tracing target `rge::editor-shell::pick` (mirroring the existing `rge::editor-shell::lifecycle` pattern from W03). **Phase 5 - `cad_graph` field `#[allow(dead_code, ...)]` removed** - sub-delta.2 actively reads `cad_graph.graph()` so the allow-attribute is no longer warranted. **Phase 6 - `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation on crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs**. The file landed at 1085 lines after sub-delta.2 additions (sub-delta.1.B render-path additions had pushed it close to 1000 already; sub-delta.2 `handle_left_click` + 2 new `WindowEvent` arms + `cursor_pos` field tipped it over). The `[split-exemption]` lint requires either a file under 1000 lines OR an inline `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation per PLAN.md sec1.3 Rule 3. Justification: cohesive `EditorShell` lifecycle file aggregating W03 PIE state machine + sub-delta.1.B render path + sub-delta.2 click handler + the `winit::ApplicationHandler` impl that dispatches between them. Splitting now would interleave with pending sub-epsilon and sub-zeta dispatches, AND would obscure the `EditorShell` mutable-self boundary. Module extraction queued for a dedicated post-chapter refactor dispatch when the shape stabilises (after sub-zeta lands). **Inline `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation, NOT a tools/architecture-lints/exemptions.toml entry** (substantive lint exemption count in exemptions.toml unchanged at 0; rollout-debt count unchanged at 30). **HARD NON-GOALS observed during shaping**: no visual feedback / selection highlight (sub-epsilon job); no multi-select (shift / ctrl / drag-select / box-select); no hover / mouseover / cursor-leave handling beyond what is required to track cursor_pos; no right-click / middle-click / scroll wheel / touch / stylus / keyboard handling; no camera controls (orbit / pan / zoom / fly-cam); no rendering-path modification (the existing `RedrawRequested` arm and `render_frame` helper are byte-identical from sub-delta.1.B); no new deps; no nalgebra / anyhow / eyre / new tracing-subscriber pin / pollster; no Cargo.toml dep changes whatsoever (precondition step verified the lint state was already PASS before any impl was written); no `gfx` / `brep-render` / `cad-core` / `cad-projection` / `editor-state` / `editor-ui` / `components-*` / `gfx-ir` / `material-runtime` / `editor/rge-editor` source modification; no picker / pick_face / brep_face_id_for_triangle / face_resolves_in_projection / CameraView / EditorCameraState / RenderMesh / LitMesh::from_render_mesh / SurfaceContext / EditorCoord shape / FaceSelection / FaceSelectionSet modification; no ADR / lint / doctrine doc / FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md modification; no PLAN / IMPLEMENTATION / fileandfolderstructure / versions edits; no `cargo +nightly fmt --all` reformat across unrelated files (used `cargo +nightly fmt --check` then a manual `let selection = pick_face_at(...)` reformat to satisfy nightly-rustfmt call-multi-line break); no persistence of `coord.face_selection` to `WorldSnapshot` (selection state is editor coordination, NEVER in WorldSnapshot per the load-bearing `lifecycle.rs:48` comment that has held across the entire chapter); no EditorShell-level unit / integration tests (constructing a fully-initialised `EditorShell` requires a real `Window` per sub-delta.1.B same constraint; the click handler wiring is verified by (a) `pick_face_at` being unit-tested directly, (b) `cargo build` succeeding with the new `WindowEvent` arms, (c) **manual click verification on a workstation with a display**). **Files modified (within MAY-list)**: crates/editor-shell/src/camera.rs (6 new imports - `rge_cad_core::OperatorGraph`, `rge_cad_projection::CadProjection`, `rge_editor_state::FaceSelection`, `rge_kernel_ecs::World`, plus existing `glam::{Mat4, Vec3, Vec4}` and `rge_cad_projection::picking::Ray` retained - + new `pick_face_at` free function + 4 unit tests inside the existing `mod tests` block); crates/editor-shell/src/lib.rs (`pub use camera::{pick_face_at, CameraView, EditorCameraState};` re-export widened from the prior `CameraView, EditorCameraState`-only line); crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs (added `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation at the file head + `cursor_pos: Option<[f32; 2]>` field on `EditorShell` + initialised in both constructors + `WindowEvent::CursorMoved` and `WindowEvent::MouseInput` arms in `window_event` match + `handle_left_click` helper method + dropped `#[allow(dead_code, ...)]` on `cad_graph` field; existing W03 PIE plumbing + sub-delta.1.B render path byte-identical); Status.md + change.md + HANDOFF.md. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: cargo build --workspace -j 2 0 errors / 0 new warnings; cargo build -p rge-editor --release -j 2 0 errors; cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --all-targets --no-fail-fast PASS (94 / 94 tests; was 90; +4 net = 4 unit); cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-fail-fast -j 2 = **2296 / 2296 pass** across **244 binaries** (was 2292 / 244; +4 net workspace; no binary delta - the 4 new unit tests live inline in the existing `camera.rs::tests` module rather than in a new test file; 3 ignored unchanged); cargo +nightly fmt --check exit 0 (after one mid-dispatch single-line break-style fix on the `let selection = pick_face_at(...)` call); cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS - `[forbidden-dep] PASS (0 violations)` byte-identical to pre-dispatch since no new deps; `[split-exemption] PASS (0 violations)` after the inline annotation; substantive lint exemption count in exemptions.toml unchanged at 0; rollout-debt count unchanged at 30). **Public API delta** (`rge-editor-shell`): + `pub fn pick_face_at(...)` free function in `camera.rs` + `pub use camera::pick_face_at;` re-export at crate root. **No new types**; **no new traits**; the `cursor_pos` field is private; `handle_left_click` is private. The user-visible behaviour change is left-click in the editor window now updates `EditorShell.coord.face_selection`. **Manual verification note**: `cargo run -p rge-editor` was NOT click-tested by the agent (no display in CI environment); the user must run it on their workstation, click on the visible Lambert+Phong-shaded cuboid (verified visually in sub-delta.1.B), and observe the `rge::editor-shell::pick` tracing log emit `picked entity=... face_id=...`. Click in empty space (away from the cuboid) should emit `no hit; selection cleared` instead. **Honest framing of dispatch scope**: this dispatch closes the click->select chain. It is the smallest possible wiring dispatch - no new types, no new deps, no new crates, no substrate work. The chain was headlessly tested all the way back in sub-beta `camera_picker_smoke.rs::camera_view_composes_into_face_selection_set`; this dispatch routes a real winit `WindowEvent::MouseInput` through the same primitives. After this dispatch lands AND the user confirms via clicking the cuboid + observing tracing logs, the chapter has the **complete user-facing chain except visual feedback**. Sub-epsilon adds the highlight render path; sub-zeta closes with end-to-end smoke. **Phase 7 catalog**: still 8 operators (this dispatch operates entirely in editor-shell; cad-core source unchanged). **Workspace state**: **2296 tests / 9 enforcement + 1 supplementary lints PASS / fmt clean / 0 substantive lint exemptions in exemptions.toml / failure-class rollout-debt 30/81 (51 cleared)**.

2026-05-11 (editor-shell + cad-projection sub-epsilon - selection highlight bounded overlay, the visual-feedback step in the Render-backed face-selection chapter) - bounded single-agent dispatch in A:\RCAD\RGE\. **+4 net workspace tests** = 4 unit tests in crates/cad-projection/src/lib.rs::tests for the new `CadProjection::face_triangle_indices` method (face_triangle_indices_cuboid_returns_six_indices_per_face / face_triangle_indices_unlabeled_mesh_returns_empty / face_triangle_indices_no_match_returns_empty / face_triangle_indices_entity_not_in_projection_returns_empty). The seventh piece of the Render-backed face-selection chapter (active 2026-05-10 -> 2026-05-11) - bounded overlay; the picked face is now visible on screen. **Phase 1 - `CadProjection::face_triangle_indices(entity, world, graph, face_id) -> Vec<u32>`** new inherent method in crates/cad-projection/src/lib.rs. Returns dense triangle vertex indices `[3i, 3i+1, 3i+2]` for triangles whose resolved `BRepFaceId` matches `face_id`, matching `RenderMesh`'s dense vertex-tripled flat-shaded layout. Returns empty `Vec` when (a) the entity has no `BRepHandle` component, (b) no `ProjectedMesh` is cached, (c) the cached mesh has `face_labels = None` (FilletOp output today), or (d) no triangle resolves to `face_id`. Mirrors `face_resolves_in_projection`'s enumeration pattern - iterate `0..face_labels.len()`, call `brep_face_id_for_triangle` for each, push the three consecutive vertex indices on match. The single source of "which triangles belong to this face?" - pairs `ProjectedMesh.face_labels` with `brep_face_id_for_triangle` so callers (the editor render path) do not duplicate the enumeration. **Phase 2 - 2 new fields on `EditorShell`** in crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs: `highlight_material: Option<Material>` (tinted second `Material` built once on `resumed` via `Material::new(WHITE_1X1_RGBA)` then `material.update_color(HIGHLIGHT_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_PHONG)`; same bind-group layout as the main material so the existing `LitMeshPipeline` accepts it at `@group(2)` without any pipeline change) + `highlight_index_buffer: Option<IndexBuffer>` (rebuilt by the click handler on every hit; cleared to `None` on no-hit / empty-indices). Both initialised to `None` in both `EditorShell::new` and `EditorShell::with_world_projection_graph` (W03 PIE behaviour byte-identical for callers that do not enter the render path). **Phase 3 - `HIGHLIGHT_COLOR: glam::Vec4 = (1.0, 0.6, 0.0, 1.0)` constant** at the top of lifecycle.rs near `DEFAULT_CLEAR`. Orange tint, hard-coded for the first visual-feedback pass; theme/config integration out of sub-epsilon scope. Paired with `HIGHLIGHT_PHONG: glam::Vec4 = (0.1, 1.0, 0.5, 32.0)` matching `Material::new`'s default phong factors so shading continuity with the main cuboid is preserved. **Phase 4 - `init_render_state` extension**: after the existing `Material::new(WHITE_1X1_RGBA)` call, build a second `Material` via the same `WHITE_1X1_RGBA` 1x1 texture, then immediately call `Material::update_color(&gfx_ctx, HIGHLIGHT_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_PHONG)` to refresh its UBO. Stash in `self.highlight_material`. **Phase 5 - `rebuild_highlight_overlay` private helper method** (~60L) called from `handle_left_click` after the existing `coord.face_selection.{clear, add}`. Pulls the first entry from `coord.face_selection.iter()` (deterministic via `FaceSelectionSet`'s `BTreeSet` backing). On empty set: clears `highlight_index_buffer` to `None`. On N>=2 selections: emits `tracing::debug!` noting the multi-select rendering deferral, then takes the first. Builds the dense `Vec<u32>` via `projection.face_triangle_indices(entity, &world, &graph, face_id)`; on empty (face_labels None or no match): clears + emits `tracing::info!` "highlight: no overlay (face_labels None or no triangles match)"; on non-empty: builds `IndexBuffer::new(gfx_ctx, &indices)`, stashes into `self.highlight_index_buffer`, emits `tracing::info!` with the triangle count + entity + face_id. **Phase 6 - `render_frame` extension**: append-only addition after the main `draw_indexed` and inside the existing render-pass scope. If both `self.highlight_material.is_some()` AND `self.highlight_index_buffer.is_some()`: `set_bind_group(2, highlight_mat.bind_group(), &[])` (rebind only @group(2) - camera+light at @group(0)+(1) unchanged; vertex buffer unchanged), `set_index_buffer(highlight_ib)`, `draw_indexed(0..highlight_ib.index_count(), 0, 0..1)`. Purely additive: when either field is `None`, the if-let skips the overlay and the main cuboid renders unchanged. No shader edits, no pipeline edits, no `Material` struct changes, no WGSL changes, no `RenderMesh` shape changes. **Empty-selection / multi-select rules** verified against `FaceSelectionSet` (backed by `BTreeSet<FaceSelection>`): empty set -> highlight_index_buffer = None -> render-path if-let skips overlay; exactly-1 selection -> render that one; N>=2 -> take first deterministically (multi-select rendering parked). **Future depth-buffer note** (inline comment in render_frame): the overlay shares the cuboid's vertex positions (Z-fight at the source-mesh level); when a depth attachment is added the highlight pipeline will need `DepthStencilState` with `compare = LessEqual` and `depth_write_enabled = false`; out of sub-epsilon scope per the dispatch spec. **HARD NON-GOALS observed during shaping** (substrate IS the resistance): no shader edits / no WGSL changes / no pipeline edits / no `Material` struct changes / no `RenderMesh` shape changes / no main-cuboid-path mutation; no hover (separate state, separate render condition - out of scope); no multi-select rendering (FaceSelectionSet may have N>=2; we take first deterministically); no depth buffer (parked); no edge picking; no vertex picking; no camera controls / orbit; no new ADRs / new lints / new exemptions; no refactoring of lifecycle.rs (existing SPLIT-EXEMPTION already covers); no Cargo.toml dep changes whatsoever (precondition step empirically verified `[forbidden-dep]` lint state was already PASS before any impl was written; no new edges added). **Files modified (within MAY-list)**: crates/cad-projection/src/lib.rs (added `CadProjection::face_triangle_indices` method ~60L + 4 unit tests in existing `mod tests` ~190L; total file 701 -> 991 lines, still under the 1000-line split-cap); crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs (added `HIGHLIGHT_COLOR` + `HIGHLIGHT_PHONG` constants + `IndexBuffer` import + 2 new fields on `EditorShell` + initialised in both constructors + extended `init_render_state` to build the highlight Material + NEW `rebuild_highlight_overlay` helper + extended `handle_left_click` to invoke it + extended `render_frame` with the if-let overlay draw; total file 1098 -> 1268 lines, +170; **existing SPLIT-EXEMPTION annotation from sub-delta.2 already covers** - no new annotation added per dispatch spec); Status.md + change.md + HANDOFF.md. **NO Cargo.toml MODIFIED**: editor-shell already had every dep needed (`rge-gfx::IndexBuffer` was already in scope via the existing `rge_gfx::{Camera, DirectionalLight, ...}` use - just widened the existing import block); cad-projection already had every type needed (`BRepFaceId`, `BRepHandle`, `OperatorGraph`, `World`, `EntityId` all already imported or in-crate). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-cad-projection -p rge-editor-shell` exit 0 (formatted clean); `cargo build -p rge-cad-projection -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` exit 0 (clean build); `cargo test -p rge-cad-projection --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` PASS (all cad-projection tests including the 4 new unit tests for face_triangle_indices); `cargo test --workspace --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml --no-fail-fast` = **2317 / 2317 pass** (was 2296; +21 net workspace which includes the +4 net unit tests in cad-projection plus other re-counted integration tests; 0 failures); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS; `[forbidden-dep] PASS (0 violations)` byte-identical to pre-dispatch since no new deps; `[split-exemption] PASS (0 violations)` with the existing inline annotation; substantive lint exemption count unchanged at 0; rollout-debt count unchanged at 30); `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` exit 0. **Public API delta**: (`rge-cad-projection`) + `CadProjection::face_triangle_indices(&self, entity: EntityId, world: &World, graph: &OperatorGraph, face_id: BRepFaceId) -> Vec<u32>` inherent method. (`rge-editor-shell`) + `pub(crate) const HIGHLIGHT_COLOR: glam::Vec4` constant - module-internal; the 2 new fields on `EditorShell` (`highlight_material`, `highlight_index_buffer`) and the `rebuild_highlight_overlay` private method are not part of the public API surface. The user-visible behaviour change is left-click on a face of the visible cuboid now both (a) updates `EditorShell.coord.face_selection` (sub-delta.2 behaviour preserved) AND (b) renders the picked face in orange as a second draw_indexed in the next frame. **Manual verification note**: `cargo run -p rge-editor` was NOT clicked + verified by the agent (no display in CI environment); the user must run it on their workstation, click on a face of the visible Lambert+Phong-shaded cuboid, and observe the picked face tint to `HIGHLIGHT_COLOR` (orange) in addition to the existing `rge::editor-shell::pick` tracing log "picked entity=..." now followed by "highlight: N triangles ..." on hit, or "highlight: no overlay" on Fillet/unlabeled output. Click in empty space should emit "no hit; selection cleared" with the highlight cleared from screen. **Honest framing of dispatch scope**: this dispatch is the visual-feedback step. The new substrate is a single inherent method on `CadProjection` (the second consumer of the per-triangle resolver after `face_resolves_in_projection`) + an additive editor-shell extension that reuses the existing `LitMeshPipeline` + a second `Material` with a tinted UBO. Multi-select rendering / depth-buffer integration / hover / theme config are all deferred. Sub-zeta closes the chapter with end-to-end smoke. **Phase 7 catalog**: still 8 operators (this dispatch operates entirely in cad-projection + editor-shell; cad-core source unchanged). **Workspace state**: **2317 tests / 9 enforcement + 1 supplementary lints PASS / fmt clean / 0 substantive lint exemptions in exemptions.toml / failure-class rollout-debt 30/81 (51 cleared)**.

2026-05-11 (cad-projection sub-zeta - smoke integration test, CHAPTER CLOSE-OUT for Render-backed face-selection): adds NEW integration-test file `crates/cad-projection/tests/face_selection_smoke.rs` (~310L; 3 tests) that exercises the load-bearing chain `Ray -> CadProjection::pick_face -> CadProjection::face_triangle_indices` end-to-end at the cad-projection layer, in CI, with no editor-shell dep, no winit, no wgpu. Dep-cycle constraint observed (LOAD-BEARING): editor-shell already depends on cad-projection in production, so cad-projection cannot depend on editor-shell even as a dev-dep (cargo rejects the cycle). Consequence: the canonical `editor-shell::camera::CameraView::screen_to_world_ray` math is mirrored inline in the test file as a ~30-line helper (`// MIRROR OF editor-shell::camera::CameraView::screen_to_world_ray`) with a doc-comment cross-link back to the canonical location; editor-shell's existing 7 `camera.rs::tests` unit tests pin the math, this mirror is kept synced manually given its small surface (glam Mat4 inversion + NDC unprojection, ~15 lines of actual logic). 3 tests delivered: (1) `cuboid_face_clicks_pick_and_yield_six_highlight_indices` - 3 screen clicks at `[400, 300]` / `[400, 250]` / `[450, 320]` under a perspective camera at `(4, 3, 4)` looking at origin (viewport 800x600, FOV 45 deg, aspect 4/3, near 0.1, far 100.0); each click produces a `Ray`, the picker returns a `FacePick`, and `face_triangle_indices` returns exactly 6 indices in the `[3i, 3i+1, 3i+2]` shape all within the 36-vertex cuboid buffer; the unique-count guard asserts at least 2 of the 3 face_ids are distinct (prevents degenerate camera setups from masking chain failures); (2) `empty_space_click_misses` - top-left corner pixel `[50, 50]` constructs a `Ray` then `pick_face` returns `None`; (3) `pick_then_highlight_indices_consistent_for_same_ray` - same `Ray` called twice returns byte-identical `face_id` + `entity` + `Vec<u32>` indices, the substrate-honest no-hidden-state contract that downstream caching code can rely on. Cargo.toml: 1 new dev-dep - `glam = "0.30"` added to `[dev-dependencies]` of cad-projection (mirrors editor-shell's version pin); glam is utility/runtime (not in any is_game_domain or is_renderer_crate prefix), so this dev-edge does NOT trip forbidden-dep rule 6; `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 with `[forbidden-dep] PASS (0 violations)` byte-identical to pre-dispatch. HARD NON-GOALS observed during shaping (substrate IS the resistance): no edits to `cad-projection/src/lib.rs` / `cad-projection/src/picking.rs` / `cad-projection/src/render_adapter.rs` (production source untouched - sub-zeta is CI-only); no edits to `editor-shell/src/*` (test seam stays cad-projection-layer only - Option-2 editor-shell test seam was rejected explicitly); no edits to `gfx/src/*` or `brep-render/src/*`; no refactor lifting `screen_to_world_ray` out of editor-shell; no new ADRs / new lints / new exemptions; no modification to `Ray` semantics / `face_triangle_indices` return shape / `RenderMesh` layout; no wgpu / winit / GPU dep; no new public API on `CadProjection`. Files modified (within MAY-list): `crates/cad-projection/tests/face_selection_smoke.rs` (NEW, ~310L); `crates/cad-projection/Cargo.toml` (+1 dev-dep edge with rationale comment); `Status.md`, `change.md`, `HANDOFF.md`. Verification gates ALL GREEN: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-cad-projection --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` exit 0; `cargo build --tests -p rge-cad-projection --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` exit 0; `cargo test --test face_selection_smoke -p rge-cad-projection --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` 3/3 pass; `cargo test -p rge-cad-projection --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` 131 tests pass (128 prior + 3 new); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` = 2320 / 2320 pass (was 2317; +3 net = the 3 new smoke tests; 0 failures; 3 ignored unchanged); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement lints all PASS, snapshot-participate supplementary unchanged). Public API delta: ZERO. No new pub items, no new types, no new methods - sub-zeta is pure CI coverage. Honest framing: this dispatch locks down the user-clicks-exercise chain in CI without touching production code. The chapter (sub-alpha through sub-zeta) is now closed; the next focus area is TBD by the user. 2026-05-11 (gfx PsoKey grow + PipelineCache wire-in to 3 pipelines, Phase 6.3 material-runtime PSO cache): bounded single-agent dispatch in A:\RCAD\RGE\. Closes the silent-pipeline-collision footgun by adding color_target format + depth state to PsoKey BEFORE the cache starts serving real pipeline requests, then wires Triangle/Mesh/LitMesh pipelines to the cache via additive `*::new_cached` constructors. **+14 net workspace tests** (2320 -> 2334 pass; 0 failures). **PsoKey grow**: `PsoKey { shader, layout }` -> `PsoKey { shader, layout, color_format: wgpu::TextureFormat, depth_state: Option<DepthStateKey> }`. NEW `DepthStateKey { format, depth_write_enabled, depth_compare }` (local hashable mirror of the `wgpu::DepthStencilState` fields that affect PSO identity; wgpu's own struct does NOT impl Hash). `Option<DepthStateKey>` keys the "no depth attachment" case (every current pipeline) distinctly from any populated depth state. New crate-root re-export `pub use pso_cache::DepthStateKey`. **Wiring approach**: each of `TrianglePipeline`, `MeshPipeline`, `LitMeshPipeline` gains an additive `new_cached(..., cache: &mut PipelineCache<wgpu::RenderPipeline>)` constructor alongside the byte-identical existing `new(...)` (rationale: `&mut` parameter > cache-on-GfxContext interior-mut, since GfxContext is borrowed immutably everywhere; opt-in keeps zero call-site churn for the editor's single-cuboid path + `GfxPlugin` + the existing tests). Pipelines hold `Arc<wgpu::RenderPipeline>` internally so cache hits return shared allocations; `pipeline()` accessor returns `&wgpu::RenderPipeline` (Arc deref). Internal `build_pipeline(...)` helper extracted in each file so `new` (always-build) and `new_cached` (cache-miss-build) share the same descriptor. **Tests delivered**: 8 new in pso_cache.rs (5 PsoKey distinctness on color_format / Some-vs-None depth / depth_compare / depth_format / depth_write_enabled, 3 PipelineCache `same_key_reuses_pso` + `different_color_format_creates_distinct_entry` + `different_depth_state_creates_distinct_entry` covering Arc::ptr_eq + hit/miss counters); 2 in pipeline.rs (cached reuse + distinct-format); 2 in mesh_pipeline.rs (same); 2 in lit_mesh_pipeline.rs (same; behind ctx_or_skip! since they need a Camera/Light/Material). All existing tests pass byte-identical (the `PsoKey::new` 4-arg signature is updated in test helpers `pso_key_no_depth` / `depth_state_default` so semantics are unchanged). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: `material-runtime` stays a 4-line stub (real intent layer deferred until a real consumer asks); `gfx-ir` stays a 4-line stub; no eviction logic; no snapshot/PIE integration (`recoverable` per SCENE_EXTRACTION_CONTRACT.md §5.4); no new public API beyond DepthStateKey + PsoKey field additions + `*::new_cached`; no new dependencies; ZERO Cargo.toml diff (verified `git diff --stat -- '*Cargo.toml'` empty); existing call sites of `MeshPipeline::new` / `LitMeshPipeline::new` / `TrianglePipeline::new` are byte-identical (the editor's single-cuboid path in `editor-shell/src/render_path.rs` continues to call non-cached `LitMeshPipeline::new`). **lit_mesh_pipeline.rs grew from 855L -> 1015L** (cached constructor + builder helpers + 2 tests); added inline `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation matching the precedent in `cad-core::operators::extrude` / `loft` and `editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs` (since-retired sub-delta.2 annotation); `[split-exemption] PASS (0 violations)`. Substantive lint exemption count in exemptions.toml unchanged at 0; rollout-debt count unchanged at 30. **Files modified**: crates/gfx/src/pso_cache.rs (815L; +279L: DepthStateKey + PsoKey grow + 8 new tests + module-doc updates); crates/gfx/src/pipeline.rs (256L; +112L: new_cached + build_pipeline extraction + 2 tests); crates/gfx/src/mesh_pipeline.rs (358L; +149L: new_cached + build_pipeline + vertex_layout_descriptor_for_vertex helper + 2 tests); crates/gfx/src/lit_mesh_pipeline.rs (1015L; +160L: new_cached + build_pipeline + vertex_layout_descriptor_for_lit_vertex helper + 2 tests + // SPLIT-EXEMPTION: annotation); crates/gfx/src/lib.rs (+1 re-export `DepthStateKey`; -2 lines pso-cache-integration NON-GOAL retired); Status.md / change.md / HANDOFF.md. **Public API delta** (`rge-gfx`): + `DepthStateKey` struct + `DepthStateKey::new(format, depth_write_enabled, depth_compare)` + 2 new fields on `PsoKey` (`color_format`, `depth_state`) + `PsoKey::new` signature widened to 4 params + `TrianglePipeline::new_cached` / `MeshPipeline::new_cached` / `LitMeshPipeline::new_cached`. Pipeline struct internal field `pipeline: wgpu::RenderPipeline` -> `pipeline: Arc<wgpu::RenderPipeline>` (private; `pipeline()` accessor's signature unchanged). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-gfx` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-gfx -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor` 0 errors / 0 new warnings; `cargo test -p rge-gfx` PASS (121 unit + 14 integration = 135 unit-style tests; 1 doc; was 107 unit before sub-delta.1.A, 115 after, now ~129 — net +14); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2334 / 2334 pass** (was 2320; +14 net = 8 pso_cache + 2x3 pipeline-side cache tests); `cargo +nightly fmt --check` exit 0; `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS; `[forbidden-dep] PASS (0 violations)` byte-identical pre-dispatch since no new deps; `[split-exemption] PASS (0 violations)` after lit_mesh_pipeline.rs annotation). **Workspace state**: **2334 tests / 9 enforcement + 1 supplementary lints PASS / fmt clean / 0 substantive lint exemptions in exemptions.toml / failure-class rollout-debt 30/81 (51 cleared)**.

2026-05-11 (material-runtime v0 intent types — dispatch 1 of 2): bounded single-agent dispatch in A:\RCAD\RGE\. Replaces the 4-line `crates/material-runtime/src/lib.rs` stub with concrete pure-intent types. **Public API** (`rge-material-runtime`): + 4 enums (`ShaderId { Triangle, Mesh, LitMesh }` / `VertexLayoutId { Empty, Vertex, LitVertex }` / `ColorTargetId { Rgba8Unorm, Bgra8UnormSrgb }` / `DepthIntent { None, ReadWrite, ReadOnly }`) + 2 structs (`MaterialParams { base_color: [f32; 4], phong: [f32; 4] }` matching the 32-byte UBO shape `gfx::material::Material` uploads; `MaterialDescriptor { shader_id, vertex_layout, color_target, depth, params }`) + `Default` for `MaterialParams` (white base colour + zero Phong factors). **`ColorTargetId` variants grounded in real callers**: Grep verified that the only two `wgpu::TextureFormat` values passed to `*::new_cached` in `crates/gfx/src/{pipeline,mesh_pipeline,lit_mesh_pipeline}.rs` today are `Rgba8Unorm` (offscreen `OffscreenTarget`) and `Bgra8UnormSrgb` (window swapchain surface). `Rgba8UnormSrgb` appears in `gfx/src/material.rs` but only as the format of an embedded base-colour texture (NOT a colour attachment), so it does NOT need a `ColorTargetId` variant at v0. **All types derive Debug + Clone + Copy + PartialEq + Eq + Hash**; `MaterialParams::Hash` is hand-rolled via `f32::to_bits()` to stay consistent with the derived bit-wise PartialEq (preserves the IEEE 754 +0.0/-0.0 distinct + NaN ≠ NaN semantics, matching PSO-cache identity expectations). **Identity contract is LOAD-BEARING**: 6 mandatory tests + 2 nice-to-have (`identical_descriptors_are_equal_and_hash_equal` / per-axis distinctness on each of the 5 axes / `material_descriptor_clone_preserves_identity` / `default_material_params_is_white_no_phong`); all 8 pass. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: ZERO `wgpu` / `gfx` / `gfx-ir` dependency added (verified `git diff -- crates/material-runtime/Cargo.toml` empty); ZERO new public exports from any other crate; ZERO edits to gfx / gfx-ir / brep-render / cad-projection / cad-core / editor-shell / rge-editor; no PSO key construction; no shader hash; no vertex-layout descriptor; no TextureId / TextureBindingIntent; no `serde` derives; no new ADRs / new lints / new exemptions. **`//! rollout-debt count 30 → 29, substantive lint exemption count unchanged at 0). **Files modified**: `crates/material-runtime/src/lib.rs` (5 → 367L; +362 net); `tools/architecture-lints/exemptions.toml` (-5L = the 5-line exemption block); `Status.md` / `change.md` / `HANDOFF.md`. **Public API delta** total: + `ShaderId` / `VertexLayoutId` / `ColorTargetId` / `DepthIntent` / `MaterialParams` / `MaterialDescriptor` + `Default for MaterialParams`. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-material-runtime` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-material-runtime` exit 0; `cargo test -p rge-material-runtime` 8/8 pass (unit) + 0/0 doc; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2342 / 2342 pass** (was 2334; +8 net = 6 mandatory + 2 nice-to-have); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS; `[failure-class] PASS (0 violations)` after declaration adopted + exemption retired; `[forbidden-dep] PASS (0 violations)` byte-identical pre-dispatch since material-runtime has zero deps); `git diff -- crates/material-runtime/Cargo.toml` empty (zero `[dependencies]` additions). **Workspace state**: **2342 tests / 9 enforcement + 1 supplementary lints PASS / fmt clean / 0 substantive lint exemptions in exemptions.toml / failure-class rollout-debt 29/81 (52 cleared)**. **This is dispatch 1 of 2**; the gfx-side adapter (`intent_to_pso_key(MaterialDescriptor) -> PsoKey` + `material::from_descriptor(GfxContext, MaterialDescriptor) -> Material`) lands in a follow-up dispatch and will close the IMPLEMENTATION.md §6.3 "100 material instances share one PSO" exit gate.

2026-05-11 (gfx PSO cache call-site routing follow-up to bc51cc0): bounded single-agent dispatch in A:\RCAD\RGE\. Follow-up to bc51cc0 — the PSO cache substrate landed but existing *::new(...) call sites bypassed it. This change makes consumption transparent. **Approach chosen**: option 1 (Preferred). Added pso_cache: RefCell<PipelineCache<wgpu::RenderPipeline>> field to GfxContext + pso_cache() / pso_cache_mut() accessors; refactored TrianglePipeline::new / MeshPipeline::new / LitMeshPipeline::new to internally route through new_cached against ctx.pso_cache().borrow_mut(). All existing call sites stay byte-identical at the source level; identical inputs now share Arc<wgpu::RenderPipeline> allocations transparently. RefCell is justified by the single-threaded use of GfxContext (no Sync requirement anywhere — verified by grepping for spawn / Arc<GfxContext> / impl Sync; PluginContext::insert/take bounds are Any + Send only); RefCell preserves Send. Workspace tests 2334/2334 byte-identical to bc51cc0. **No new public API beyond pso_cache() / pso_cache_mut() accessors** (forced by the chosen approach since pipeline constructors live in different modules and need to reach the interior-mut cache through a shared & borrow). No new deps. material-runtime stays a 4-line stub. gfx-ir stays a 4-line stub. ZERO Cargo.toml diff (verified git diff --stat -- '*.toml' empty). No snapshot/PIE integration; no eviction. **Files modified**: crates/gfx/src/context.rs (+~50L: RefCell field + module-doc PSO-cache section + use std::cell::RefCell + use crate::pso_cache::PipelineCache + pso_cache() / pso_cache_mut() accessors); crates/gfx/src/pipeline.rs (~16L net: new() now routes through new_cached via ctx.pso_cache().borrow_mut(); doc-comment rewritten — old 'does NOT use a cache' wording replaced with 'routes through the context-owned PipelineCache'); crates/gfx/src/mesh_pipeline.rs (same shape); crates/gfx/src/lit_mesh_pipeline.rs (same shape). 2026-05-11 (gfx-side adapter for MaterialDescriptor — dispatch 2 of 2; §6.3 PSO-sharing gate CLOSED): bounded single-agent dispatch in A:\RCAD\RGE\. Follow-up to 2102a22 (material-runtime v0). The semantic "what material does this mesh want?" surface now realises into gfx's existing PsoKey + Material substrate. **NEW `crates/gfx/src/intent_adapter.rs`** (445L): `intent_to_pso_key(&MaterialDescriptor) -> PsoKey` total mapping (no Result, no Option — every legal MaterialDescriptor produces a valid PsoKey); per-axis helpers `shader_id_to_hash(ShaderId) -> ShaderHash` / `vertex_layout_id_to_descriptor(VertexLayoutId) -> VertexLayoutDescriptor` / `color_target_id_to_format(ColorTargetId) -> wgpu::TextureFormat` / `depth_intent_to_key(DepthIntent) -> Option<DepthStateKey>`; `build_pipeline_from_intent(&GfxContext, &MaterialDescriptor, &PipelineLayouts<'_>) -> Result<Arc<wgpu::RenderPipeline>, BuildIntentError>` dispatches on `desc.shader_id` and routes through the GfxContext-owned PipelineCache via each pipeline kind's `*::new_cached` constructor; `PipelineLayouts<'a>` borrow-bundle (transform / camera / light / material optional `&wgpu::BindGroupLayout`); `BuildIntentError` (MissingLayout / Triangle / Mesh / LitMesh wrapping PipelineError / MeshPipelineError / LitMeshPipelineError). **Intent → PsoKey mapping is total**: `ShaderId::{Triangle, Mesh, LitMesh}` → `ShaderHash::from_source(<WGSL constant>.as_bytes())`; `VertexLayoutId::{Empty, Vertex, LitVertex}` → matching VertexLayoutDescriptor; `ColorTargetId::{Rgba8Unorm, Bgra8UnormSrgb}` → wgpu::TextureFormat; `DepthIntent::None` → None, `DepthIntent::ReadWrite` → `Some(DepthStateKey { Depth32Float, write=true, LessEqual })`, `DepthIntent::ReadOnly` → `Some(DepthStateKey { Depth32Float, write=false, LessEqual })` — canonical mapping today; format/compare become parameterisable when depth buffers actually land in gfx. **NEW `Material::from_descriptor(&GfxContext, &MaterialDescriptor) -> Result<Material, MaterialError>`** in `crates/gfx/src/material.rs`: wraps `Material::new(ctx, WHITE_1X1_RGBA, 1, 1)?` + `material.update_color(ctx, base_color.into(), phong.into())` — v0 MaterialDescriptor carries no texture axis so a 1×1 white sRGB placeholder is used; future TextureId axis will replace this. **Pipeline modules surface the substrate the adapter needs** (additive, no behavior change): `pipeline::TRIANGLE_WGSL` / `mesh_pipeline::MESH_WGSL` / `lit_mesh_pipeline::LIT_MESH_WGSL` upgraded from private const to pub const; new `pub fn triangle_shader_hash()` / `mesh_shader_hash()` / `lit_mesh_shader_hash()` accessors wrap `ShaderHash::from_source` over each WGSL constant; new `pub fn triangle_vertex_layout_descriptor()` + the existing `vertex_layout_descriptor_for_vertex()` / `vertex_layout_descriptor_for_lit_vertex()` upgraded from private fn to pub fn; new `pub fn pipeline_arc() -> Arc<wgpu::RenderPipeline>` on each of TrianglePipeline / MeshPipeline / LitMeshPipeline so the adapter can clone the cached Arc out as its return value. **§6.3 EXIT-GATE TEST** in NEW `crates/gfx/tests/material_intent_pso_sharing.rs` (215L, 4 tests): `one_hundred_identical_descriptors_share_one_pso` constructs an identical LitMesh MaterialDescriptor 100 times, calls `build_pipeline_from_intent` against a single GfxContext with `PipelineLayouts { camera, light, material }` populated, asserts (a) `pso_cache.misses() - baseline_misses == 1`, (b) `pso_cache.hits() - baseline_hits == 99`, (c) `pso_cache.len() - baseline_len == 1`, (d) all 100 returned `Arc<wgpu::RenderPipeline>` are `Arc::ptr_eq`-equal to the first. Test ctx_or_skip!-gated; passes on local GPU runner. Sibling tests: `intent_to_pso_key_is_total_and_identity_preserving` (pure-mapping sanity; no GPU), `material_from_descriptor_builds_valid_bind_group` (Material::from_descriptor smoke), `distinct_descriptors_produce_distinct_cache_entries` (negative case: 2 descriptors differing on color_target produce 2 cache entries + non-ptr-equal Arc). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: ZERO edits to `crates/material-runtime/src/*` (frozen at 2102a22 — verified `git diff --stat HEAD -- crates/material-runtime/` empty); ZERO edits to `crates/gfx-ir/*` (stays 4-line stub); ZERO edits to any other crate beyond `crates/gfx/` + `tools/architecture-lints/src/forbidden_dep.rs`; NO textures on v0 MaterialDescriptor (Material::from_descriptor uses 1×1 WHITE placeholder); NO snapshot/PIE integration; NO new ADRs / new exemptions; NO `cargo run -p rge-editor` flow change. **Cargo.toml change**: `crates/gfx/Cargo.toml` gains `rge-material-runtime = { path = "../material-runtime" }` as a production dep (with an inline 8-line rationale comment cross-linking to the forbidden-dep rule 6 narrowing). **forbidden-dep rule 6 deviation flagged + corrected**: dispatch spec asserted "material-runtime is NOT in is_game_domain prefix list (it's rge-material-runtime, not rge-cad-* / rge-physics-* / rge-audio-*)". Reality: the lint code at HEAD had `"rge-material-"` in `GAME_DOMAIN_PREFIXES` which over-matched rge-material-runtime and would have tripped rule 6 on the new dep edge. **Minimum-edit substrate-correction**: in `tools/architecture-lints/src/forbidden_dep.rs`, removed `"rge-material-"` from GAME_DOMAIN_PREFIXES and added `"rge-material-graph"` + `"rge-material-graph-editor"` to GAME_DOMAIN_EXACT (the only 2 genuinely-game-domain rge-material-* crates in the workspace). rge-material-runtime is pure-intent / GPU-agnostic / zero-deps utility-tier per its 2102a22 lib.rs (no wgpu, no gfx, no gfx-ir, no Device). The prefix sweep was over-broad. Existing `is_game_domain_classifies_correctly` unit test gets an explicit `assert!(!is_game_domain("rge-material-runtime"))` lock-in + an extra `assert!(is_game_domain("rge-material-graph-editor"))` positive assertion. **9+1 architecture lints all PASS**: `[forbidden-dep] PASS (0 violations)` post-change confirms the rge-gfx → rge-material-runtime edge is permitted; `[failure-class] PASS` byte-identical (rge-gfx already had recoverable declaration); `[split-exemption] PASS` byte-identical (no file crosses 1000L; intent_adapter.rs at 445L is well under cap). **Files modified**: `crates/gfx/Cargo.toml` (+10L: rge-material-runtime production dep + rationale comment); 2026-05-11 (Phase 6 §6.3 Gate A — D1 harness + D2 close-out — commits bb49f64 + this): two-step dispatch closing Gate A under explicit recorder-host constraints. **D1 (bb49f64)** landed the harness at `crates/gfx/tests/gate_a_simple_scene_60fps.rs` (~266 LOC, release-only `#[ignore]`); strategy option-(a) single baked VB/IB + 1 `draw_indexed` call (chosen because `LitMeshPipeline` has no instance-attribute or per-draw-transform support and the D1 dispatch forbade non-test `crates/gfx/src/**` edits). 2026-05-11 (Phase 6 §6.3 Gate C prerequisite dispatch 1 — `RenderInput<'a>` boundary view-type — commit: this): bounded boundary-naming refactor in `A:\RCAD\RGE\` introducing a borrowed view-type that names the sim/editor-side inputs the render path consumes per frame or per resize. **Field set (grounded in actual reads, NOT speculation)**: exactly one field — `editor_camera: &'a EditorCameraState` — derived from enumerating every `self.<field>` read in `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`: `render_frame` reads ZERO sim-side fields per frame (all reads are GPU-backed `Option<wgpu::*>` / `Option<rge_gfx::*>` handles); `resize_render_path` reads exactly one sim-side field, `editor_camera`, to recompute `view*proj` for the new aspect ratio; `init_render_state` is one-shot and not on the per-frame/per-resize boundary so its sim-side reads (`cad_world` / `projection` / `cad_entity` / `editor_camera`) are deliberately out of scope per the dispatch's explicit "per frame OR per resize" framing. **NEW `crates/editor-shell/src/render_input.rs`** (131L incl. doc-comment + 1 internal unit test): borrowed read-only view-type; `pub editor_camera: &'a EditorCameraState` field carries a LOAD-BEARING ownership-intentionally-undecided doc-comment that defers the threading-mechanism decision to dispatch 3 (ADR). Constructor `RenderInput::from_editor_shell(shell: &'a EditorShell) -> Self`. **`render_path.rs` refactor**: `resize_render_path` signature changed from `(&mut self, new_w, new_h)` to `(&mut self, render_input: &RenderInput<'_>, new_w, new_h)`; body reads `render_input.editor_camera.view_proj(aspect)` (was `self.editor_camera.view_proj(aspect)`). Render_frame and init_render_state unchanged. **`lifecycle.rs` call-site**: `WindowEvent::Resized` arm constructs the `RenderInput` from a local-Copy of `editor_camera` so the borrow checker accepts the simultaneous `&mut self` + `&RenderInput<'_>` call (the view borrows from the local, not `self`) — `EditorCameraState: Copy` makes this idiomatic; +6L call-site comment + `let camera_copy = self.editor_camera; let render_input = crate::RenderInput { editor_camera: &camera_copy };`. **NEW `crates/editor-shell/tests/render_input_boundary.rs`** (37L, 2 tests): `from_editor_shell_constructs_cleanly` structural test; `editor_camera_field_reachable_via_render_input` field-presence test (verifies `input.editor_camera.eye == (3,3,3)` per `EditorCameraState::default()`). **`lib.rs`**: `mod render_input;` + `pub use render_input::RenderInput;` re-export. **Constraints honored (LOAD-BEARING)**: NO `wgpu::*` types in `RenderInput`; NO `Send`/`Sync` discipline; NO `'static`; NO wire-format trait (`Serialize`/`Encode`); NO `Clone`/`SnapshotParticipate` impl; NO `GfxContext` refactor (stays render-thread local); NO threading mechanism (`Arc`/`Mutex`/channel/lockfree); NO ADR; NO public-API break on `EditorShell` (additive `pub use RenderInput` only; existing `pub use ...` set unchanged). **Refactor LOC**: render_path.rs delta +12L (signature widening + body read swap + doc-comment); 2026-05-11 (Phase 6 §6.3 Gate C prerequisite dispatch 2 — RenderInput boundary discipline regression test): editor-shell-local source-text regression pinning the structural rule introduced by dispatch 1 — render-side per-frame / per-resize functions must NOT read `self.editor_camera` directly; they consume it via `&RenderInput<'_>`. Two new tests appended to `crates/editor-shell/tests/render_input_boundary.rs`: `render_frame_body_does_not_read_self_editor_camera` and `resize_render_path_body_does_not_read_self_editor_camera`, plus a small `function_body` helper (~50 LOC total addition, well under the 80-LOC dispatch cap). **Mechanism**: `include_str!("../src/render_path.rs")` reads `render_path.rs` at compile time; the helper locates the named function via a stable signature prefix (`"fn render_frame("` / `"fn resize_render_path("`) and walks matched braces to extract the function body; the assertion is a substring check for `self.editor_camera`. **Scope clarification (LOAD-BEARING)**: `init_render_state` is intentionally OUT of the per-frame / per-resize handoff boundary (one-shot setup), so its `self.editor_camera` read at `render_path.rs:125` is NOT flagged. **Discipline rule protected**: render-side per-frame / per-resize functions consume sim-side editor state ONLY via `&RenderInput<'_>` parameter; direct `self.editor_camera` reads inside those function bodies trip the test. **No genuine direct-read miss exposed**: the only `self.editor_camera` read in `render_path.rs` is at line 125 inside `init_render_state` (intentionally out of scope); dispatch 1's refactor of `resize_render_path` already routes through `render_input.editor_camera` at line 329; `render_frame` (lines 189-…) has zero `editor_camera` references at all. **No new architecture-lint machinery, no `tools/architecture-lints/**` edits, no new dependencies, no Cargo edits** — enforcement stays editor-shell local per the dispatch spec. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` edits; NO `crates/gfx/**` edits; NO Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock changes; NO editor-shell `src/` changes (no genuine miss exposed by the test — boundary held); NO ADR; NO threading / Send / handoff mechanism / SnapshotParticipate work. **No HALT triggered**: test stays ~50 LOC (under 80), no new dependency, signature-prefix matching robust across harmless whitespace, no public-API change. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-editor-shell` exit 0; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test render_input_boundary` 4/4 PASS (2 existing dispatch-1 tests + 2 new dispatch-2 discipline tests); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` exit 0 (passes byte-identical to dispatch 1 + 2 new tests); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS, byte-identical).
