Remaining Gate C path: dispatch 3 (ADR for immutability + double-buffer / arc-swap / triple-buffer mechanism — design work, the real architecture decision); dispatch 4 (owned Sendable `RenderInputOwned` variant + handoff implementation, depends on dispatch 3); dispatch 5 (Gate C empirical test per PLAN §13.6). Steps 1-2 are well-shaped and now landed; step 3 is where the real architecture decision lives. Next focus: TBD by user.

2026-05-11 (Phase 6 §6.3 Gate C prerequisite dispatch 3 — render-input handoff mechanism ADR, docs-only design): **NEW `docs/adr/ADR-117-render-handoff-mechanism.md`** (175L) pins the render-input handoff semantics for the sim/render boundary named by dispatch 1 + regression-protected by dispatch 2. The ADR is semantics-first, crate-second: dispatch 4 picks the implementation primitive (std-only manual triple-buffer-semantics, or `arc-swap`, or another vetted crate) subject to the semantics pinned here. **Decision in one sentence**: latest-only immutable snapshot via `Arc<RenderInputOwned>` with `Send + 'static`; render reads the most-recently-published snapshot at frame-start; sim publishes new snapshots whenever ready; older un-read snapshots are dropped via `Arc` strong-count; render NEVER blocks sim and sim NEVER waits for render (beyond a trivial atomic-pointer-swap). **Five sub-decisions pinned**: (1) handoff semantics = latest-only / drop-old / non-blocking both sides / `(ECS_tick_N, CadCheckpointId_N)` immutability-anchor; (2) ownership model = `Arc<RenderInputOwned>` with `Send + 'static` bound (future-proofs multi-reader + cross-thread move; the bound is pinned now so dispatch 4 ships the cross-thread substrate even though today's renderer runs single-threaded inline on `WindowEvent::RedrawRequested`); (3) anchor shape = `(ecs_tick, checkpoint_id)` as concrete fields inside `RenderInputOwned` + separate `AtomicU64` generation counter on the handoff substrate (monotonic; lets render answer "did sim publish since I last looked?" in O(1) without locking); (4) render-lag = latest-only / drop-old (sim publishes K snapshots between two render frames → render sees only the most recent at the next frame-start, the older K-1 are dropped); (5) implementation primitive **deliberately deferred to dispatch 4** with the ADR's recommendation being "start with std-only safe-Rust composition (`Arc<Mutex<Option<Arc<RenderInputOwned>>>>` with the lock held only across the trivial pointer-swap) and escalate to `arc-swap` only if measurement reveals contention or correctness issues" — workspace `unsafe_code = "forbid"` policy forecloses the manual `AtomicPtr<_>` variant requiring `Box::from_raw`. **6 alternatives compared in decision table**: (a) chosen latest-only triple-buffer-semantics; (b) manual double-buffer (collapses to chosen once `Arc<_>` ownership applied); (c) `Arc<RwLock<RenderInputOwned>>` (rejected — violates "render never blocks sim"); (d) channel-with-drop-old via `crossbeam-channel` (rejected as primary, possible dispatch-4 primitive if cleanest); (e) per-frame full clone with no handoff substrate (rejected — re-introduces synchronous coupling); (f) `SnapshotParticipate` PIE participant for `gfx.render-snapshot` (rejected — PIE is per-tick / cross-process, wrong-shape for per-frame in-process immutability, deferred per GFX_RENDER_TIER.md §11.2). **`GfxContext` remains render-thread-local-by-`RefCell`** (explicitly preserved; the handoff IS the boundary, the renderer's GPU state stays where it is). **Explicit non-decisions (6)**: wire-format / payload shape (separate ADR); whether dedicated render thread exists today (out of scope — handoff works in single-threaded execution); renderer threading model proper (out of scope); `editor_camera`'s final ownership (sim-state vs render-coord-state — placed inside `RenderInputOwned`'s scope but NOT pinned, deferred to wire-format ADR); whether `gfx.render-snapshot` PIE participant ever materialises (deferred per GFX_RENDER_TIER.md §11.2); specific crate vs std-only primitive (dispatch 4 picks). **Future work**: dispatch 4 implementation (NEW `crates/editor-shell/src/render_input/handoff.rs` or analogue, ~150-250 LOC); dispatch 5 §13.6 empirical test (~80-120 LOC); wire-format ADR (sibling); per-field ownership catalogue (in wire-format ADR); PIE participant re-evaluation when wire-format lands. **Cross-references named in ADR**: PLAN.md §1.5.2 (verbatim quote) + §13.6 (verbatim quote) + §13.2; SCENE_EXTRACTION_CONTRACT.md §3.3 / §5.2 / §6.2 / §6.3; GFX_RENDER_TIER.md §1 / §11.2 / §12; ADR-114 + ADR-115 + ADR-116 (precedent ADRs for owned-handoff + semantics-first + minimal-codification patterns). **`docs/architecture/README.md` updated**: 8 → 9 ADRs accepted; entry "117 (render-handoff mechanism for Gate C)" appended. **HARD NON-GOALS observed (DOCS-ONLY)**: NO `.rs` edits, NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` edits, NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` edits, NO implementation, NO wire-format design, NO architecture-lint changes, NO reopening Gate A/B/PSO-sharing. **HALT triggers observed (none hit)**: ADR final 175L (well under ~400L cap); only one ADR file created (no sibling needed); wire-format not a prerequisite (left as future-work followup); no source edits needed; no `Cargo.toml` edit needed (ADR may *recommend* `arc-swap` but the dep add belongs to dispatch 4). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check` (workspace) exit 0 byte-identical to pre-dispatch (no source changes); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 byte-identical (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS). Files modified: `docs/adr/ADR-117-render-handoff-mechanism.md` (NEW 175L), `docs/architecture/README.md` (+1L net — ADR count bump), `Status.md` + `change.md` + `HANDOFF.md` (this entry). **Gate C status transition: boundary-named + regression-protected → boundary-named + regression-protected + handoff-design-pinned**. Remaining Gate C path: dispatch 4 (implementation of chosen semantics, ~150-250 LOC, NEW `crates/editor-shell/src/render_input/handoff.rs` or analogue containing `RenderInputOwned: Send + 'static` + `SnapshotPublisher` + `SnapshotConsumer`); dispatch 5 (empirical test per PLAN §13.6 — render holds `Arc<RenderInputOwned>` while sim drives topology mutation, assert held snapshot's `(ecs_tick, checkpoint_id)` unchanged; ~80-120 LOC). Next focus: Gate C dispatch 4 (handoff substrate implementation) per ADR-117's chosen-by-default std-only safe-Rust composition.
2026-05-11 (Phase 6 §6.3 Gate C prerequisite dispatch 4 — `RenderInputOwned` + `RenderHandoff` handoff primitive, std-only safe-Rust composition per ADR-117): bounded single-agent substrate dispatch in `A:\RCAD\RGE\` extending `crates/editor-shell/src/render_input.rs` (was 131L from dispatch 1 → now 361L; +230L net) with the latest-only immutable render-input handoff substrate. **Module placement**: extension to existing `render_input.rs` (recommended in spec for cohesion since `RenderInput<'_>` + `RenderInputOwned` + `RenderHandoff` are tightly coupled — `as_render_input()` bridges owned → borrowed). NO new file, NO directory restructure. **NEW `RenderInputOwned`** (Clone + Debug; derive-only): `{ ecs_tick: u64, checkpoint_id: u64, editor_camera: EditorCameraState }` per ADR-117 sub-decision 3. `RenderInputOwned: Send + 'static` satisfied automatically (all fields are `Send + 'static`; `EditorCameraState: Copy` is `Send + 'static` since fields are glam f32 / Vec3). NO `unsafe`. `as_render_input(&self) -> RenderInput<'_>` borrows back to dispatch-1's view shape so render-path functions that consume `&RenderInput<'_>` accept it unchanged. **NEW `RenderHandoff`** (Debug-impl that skips lock-taking to stay panic-free under poisoning): `{ slot: std::sync::Mutex<Option<std::sync::Arc<RenderInputOwned>>>, generation: std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64 }` per ADR-117 sub-decision 5 (std-only safe-Rust composition). `RenderHandoff: Send + Sync` satisfied automatically (Mutex + AtomicU64 combo). NO `unsafe`. **Public API (additive, all `&self` — no `&mut`)**: `new() -> Self` / `Default` (empty slot, generation 0); `publish(snapshot: Arc<RenderInputOwned>)` replaces the slot then `fetch_add(1, Release)` on generation so observers re-reading generation see an already-up-to-date slot; `acquire() -> Option<Arc<RenderInputOwned>>` returns a clone of the slot Arc (latest-only retention until next publish — re-acquires within same generation are cheap); `generation() -> u64` returns `Acquire`-ordered counter (O(1) "did sim publish?" read without locking the slot). **Latest-only / drop-old semantics**: `publish` REPLACES rather than queues. Older Arc strong-counts drop to caller-only once the next publish lands; verified by RH-3 test below (Arc::strong_count == 1 after newer publish). Render never blocks sim; sim never blocks render beyond the uncontended Mutex briefly held across an `Arc::clone`. **Module-level doc-comment** expanded with full constraint catalogue: no `wgpu::*` types anywhere in this file, no `unsafe`, no new deps, no renderer-thread spawn (today's renderer continues inline on `WindowEvent::RedrawRequested`), no `SnapshotParticipate` impl. **`crates/editor-shell/src/lib.rs`**: re-export widened from `pub use render_input::RenderInput;` to `pub use render_input::{RenderHandoff, RenderInput, RenderInputOwned};`. **NEW 7 tests appended to `crates/editor-shell/tests/render_input_boundary.rs`** (+178L → 295L total): **RIO-1** `render_input_owned_is_send_and_static` (compile-time `assert_send_static::<RenderInputOwned>()` via inner-fn bound); **RH-1** `render_handoff_is_send_and_sync` (compile-time `assert_send_sync::<RenderHandoff>()`); **RH-2** `render_handoff_publish_then_acquire_returns_same_snapshot` (publish → acquire happy path; verifies same Arc allocation via `Arc::ptr_eq`); **RH-3** `render_handoff_latest_only_drops_older` (publish A, publish B without acquiring A → A's strong_count == 1 (test-holder only) + acquire returns B); **RH-4** `render_handoff_generation_advances_monotonically` (0 → 1 → 2 → 3 after three publishes); **RH-5** `render_handoff_acquire_before_publish_returns_none` (substrate-honest empty-slot None contract); **RIO-2** `render_input_owned_as_render_input_borrows_correctly` (default eye `(3,3,3)` reachable via borrowed view + `std::ptr::eq` proves borrow-only with no clone). **Constraints honored (LOAD-BEARING)**: NO `unsafe` blocks anywhere; NO new dependencies (std-only via `std::sync::{Mutex, Arc, atomic::AtomicU64}`); NO `wgpu::*` types in `RenderInputOwned` or `RenderHandoff`; NO `GfxContext` field change; NO `EditorShell` field change; NO `EditorShell` public-API break (additive `pub use ...` widening only); NO renderer-thread spawn; NO actual cross-thread call site (single-threaded inline renderer continues as-is); NO `SnapshotParticipate` impl; NO Gate C empirical mutation test (dispatch 5). **`Send + 'static` for `RenderInputOwned`**: confirmed via RIO-1 compile-time assertion + structural review (all fields are `Send + 'static`). **`Send + Sync` for `RenderHandoff`**: confirmed via RH-1 compile-time assertion + structural review (`Mutex<T: Send>: Send + Sync` and `AtomicU64: Send + Sync`). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-editor-shell` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell` clean (one minor rustfmt re-style of the use-statement after manual edit); `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell` 11/11 PASS in `render_input_boundary` test target (4 pre-existing dispatch-1+2 tests + 7 new dispatch-4 tests); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2371 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2364/0/19 pre-dispatch; +7 new tests); `cargo +nightly fmt --check --all` exit 0; `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS, byte-identical to pre-dispatch). Files modified: `crates/editor-shell/src/render_input.rs` (131L → 361L, +230L), `crates/editor-shell/src/lib.rs` (re-export widening, +1L net), `crates/editor-shell/tests/render_input_boundary.rs` (117L → 295L, +178L), `Status.md` + `change.md` + `HANDOFF.md` (this entry). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `crates/gfx/**` edits, NO `crates/cad-projection/**` edits, NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` edits, NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` edits, NO `unsafe` blocks, NO new deps, NO renderer-thread implementation, NO wire-format design, NO `GfxContext` change, NO `EditorShell` field change, NO public-API break. **HALT triggers (none hit)**: implementation needs no new dep; `Send`/`'static` satisfied without `unsafe`; no `wgpu::*` types needed; no public `EditorShell` API break; production-code addition within ADR-117's pinned ~150-250L estimate; no leaks into forbidden directories. **Gate C status transition: boundary-named + regression-protected + handoff-design-pinned → handoff-substrate-landed**. Only Gate C **dispatch 5** (empirical test per PLAN §13.6 — render holds `Arc<RenderInputOwned>` while sim drives topology mutation, assert held snapshot's `(ecs_tick, checkpoint_id)` unchanged; ~80-120 LOC) remains for full Gate C closure. Next focus: TBD (Gate C dispatch 5 is the obvious follow-on; parallel candidates: material-runtime PSO cache; kernel cavity v0 work; Phase 3.3-3.4 bench gates).

2026-05-11 (Phase 6.2 runtime integration — RenderHandoff publish/acquire wired into EditorShell): closes the IMPLEMENTATION.md §6.2 line 449-451 integration gap. The Gate C 5-dispatch chain landed the `RenderHandoff` substrate but `lifecycle.rs` never called `publish()` or `acquire()` — the runtime still constructed `RenderInput` ad-hoc from a stack-local copy of `self.editor_camera` in the `Resized` arm at lifecycle.rs ~line 619-630. This dispatch closes that loop. **`EditorShell` struct delta**: NEW `render_handoff: RenderHandoff` field (non-Option — the substrate is always present); both constructors (`with_world`, `with_world_projection_graph`) initialise it via `RenderHandoff::new()`; NEW `pub fn render_handoff(&self) -> &RenderHandoff` accessor for the end-to-end test (additive — no public-API break). **Sim-side publish**: `EditorShell::tick_redraw` (lifecycle.rs ~525-545) gains a per-redraw `Arc::new(RenderInputOwned { ecs_tick: self.tick_count, checkpoint_id: 0, editor_camera: self.editor_camera })` + `self.render_handoff.publish(...)` step BEFORE the diagnostic progress line (so the published snapshot reflects the editor systems' viewport overlay update from step 3). **Anchor sources**: `ecs_tick = self.tick_count` (editor-shell's authoritative game-system tick counter — the closest analogue to "kernel-ecs tick" pre-kernel-ecs integration; advances only when `PlayState::game_systems_run()` fires); `checkpoint_id = 0` (no cad-projection counter exists on `EditorShell` today — per dispatch spec, `0` is acceptable for v0; values matter for the empirical-invariant test, not for runtime correctness today). **Render-side acquire — `Resized` arm**: replaces the ad-hoc `let camera_copy = self.editor_camera; let render_input = crate::RenderInput { editor_camera: &camera_copy };` construction with `self.render_handoff.acquire()` + `owned.as_render_input()` + `self.resize_render_path(&render_input, ...)`. **First-resize fallback**: `Resized` can fire before any `tick_redraw` (winit's initial size negotiation may precede the first `RedrawRequested`); when the slot is empty, a fresh snapshot is published inline so the resize proceeds with the current camera — this keeps the resize path coupled to the SAME handoff substrate the render path consumes, instead of bypassing with an ad-hoc local view. **Render-side acquire — `RedrawRequested` arm**: NEW `let _snapshot = self.render_handoff.acquire();` BEFORE `render_frame()`. Per the dispatch-1+2 discipline `render_frame` reads zero sim-side state per frame (the `render_frame_body_does_not_read_self_editor_camera` test pins this); the acquire here anchors the §6.2 contract "render reads frozen WorldSnapshot{N}" by routing the render path through the same handoff substrate even when the current `render_frame` consumer is a no-op for per-frame sim state. When `render_frame` grows per-frame sim reads in a later dispatch, they pull off the held snapshot — the wiring is already in place. **NEW end-to-end test `phase_6_2_runtime_publishes_snapshot_on_tick_redraw`** in `crates/editor-shell/tests/render_input_boundary.rs` (~58L incl. doc-comment + scope-honesty preamble + assertions): construct a default `EditorShell`; assert baseline generation 0; call `tick_redraw()` once; assert generation advanced to 1; acquire the snapshot; assert `editor_camera.eye == (3, 3, 3)` (default), `ecs_tick == 0` (Editing-state `tick_redraw` does NOT advance `tick_count` — game systems are gated), `checkpoint_id == 0`. Proves the runtime publish/acquire path is live. **Forward-shape**: per ADR-117 sub-decision 5 the chosen semantics work single-threaded today and cross-threaded later WITHOUT API changes; a future dispatch installs the actual render thread on top of this same substrate. **Camera-only payload preserved** (wire-format ADR remains the explicit non-decision per ADR-117 §1); `render_frame` / `resize_render_path` signatures BYTE-IDENTICAL (verified). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `std::thread::spawn` / `tokio::spawn` / any real threading (verified `git diff` empty for `thread::spawn` / `tokio::spawn`); NO `unsafe ` additions (verified empty); NO `crates/gfx/**` / `crates/cad-projection/**` / `crates/cad-core/**` edits (verified `git diff --stat` shows only `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs` + `crates/editor-shell/tests/render_input_boundary.rs` touched); ZERO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` changes; NO `WorldSnapshot` wire-format expansion (camera-only preserved); NO second sim-side field added to `RenderInputOwned`; NO `render_path.rs` function signature changes; NO reopening Gate A/B/C closure markers (lines 468-471 preserved). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-editor-shell --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` clean (0 errors / 0 new warnings); `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` ALL PASS (94/94 — lifecycle 11 + camera 11 + ... + `render_input_boundary` 13/13 [12 prior + 1 new §6.2-1]); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` = **2373 / 2373 pass** (was 2372; +1 net — matches dispatch's expected delta); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS, byte-identical). **Total LOC delta**: `lifecycle.rs` +108 net (+118 / -10); `tests/render_input_boundary.rs` +74 added. Files modified: `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs` (struct field + 2 constructor inits + accessor + tick_redraw publish + Resized acquire/fallback + RedrawRequested acquire); `crates/editor-shell/tests/render_input_boundary.rs` (+1 test); `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` §6.2 (3 closure markers — one per bullet); `Status.md` + `change.md` + `HANDOFF.md` (this entry). **Phase 6.2 status transition: substrate-shipped (Gate C dispatch 4) → runtime-integrated**. After this lands, Phase 6 has a clean stopping point; the dedicated renderer thread spawn that ADR-117 §1 non-decisions out remains the natural next milestone whenever sim/render thread split lands.

2026-05-11 (Phase 6 §6.3 Gate C prerequisite dispatch 5 — empirical handoff invariant test, FINAL Gate C step): single empirical test added to `crates/editor-shell/tests/render_input_boundary.rs` validating PLAN §13.6's load-bearing invariant at the `RenderHandoff` boundary per ADR-117 — a render-side acquired snapshot remains stable while sim-side publishes / mutates newer snapshots. **NEW test `gate_c_held_snapshot_stable_across_subsequent_publishes`** (~55L incl. doc-comment + scope-honesty preamble + assertions). Shape: publish snapshot N (ecs_tick=100, checkpoint_id=1000, eye=(1,1,1)); render acquires N and HOLDS the Arc; sim publishes N+1 (101, 1001, eye=(2,2,2)) then N+2 (102, 1002, eye=(3,3,3)) without intervening acquire; **assert held snapshot N's `(ecs_tick, checkpoint_id, eye.x)` are unchanged**; assert fresh `acquire()` returns N+2 (latest-only / drop-old: N+1 is dropped); assert `generation()` is 3 (monotonic across 3 publishes). **Scope honesty (LOAD-BEARING)**: today's renderer runs inline on `WindowEvent::RedrawRequested` — there is NO dedicated render thread yet. This test is a **single-threaded empirical proxy** for the cross-thread invariant; it proves the handoff's `Arc` semantics preserve the held snapshot through subsequent publishes. When the dedicated renderer thread lands in a future dispatch, the same invariant must continue to hold under real concurrency. **This test does NOT certify a full render-thread architecture.** Recorded in test doc-comment + `IMPLEMENTATION.md` §6.3 line 470 closure marker + this changelog entry — three independent surface locations for the scope caveat. **`IMPLEMENTATION.md` line 470 closure marker rewritten** from `[DEFERRED — blocked on sim/render thread split per PLAN §1.5.2; substrate is single-threaded today so the property is vacuously true]` to `[CLOSED 2026-05-11 for ADR-117 RenderHandoff boundary invariant: held Arc<RenderInputOwned> stable across subsequent publishes; latest-only / drop-old; (ecs_tick, checkpoint_id) anchor preserved; single-threaded proxy today (PLAN §13.6); future dedicated renderer thread must keep the same invariant; does not certify a full render-thread architecture yet; see crates/editor-shell/tests/render_input_boundary.rs::gate_c_held_snapshot_stable_across_subsequent_publishes + ADR-117]`. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO real thread spawning (single-threaded proxy is sufficient + honestly framed); NO `crates/gfx/**` edits; NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` changes; NO `WorldSnapshot` wire format work; NO `SnapshotParticipate` impl; NO renderer thread model implementation; NO reopening Gate A / B / PSO-sharing; NO claiming a real cross-thread renderer exists. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-editor-shell` exit 0; `cargo +nightly fmt --check` (workspace) exit 0; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test render_input_boundary` 12/12 PASS (4 dispatch-1+2 + 7 dispatch-4 + 1 dispatch-5 = 12); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary all PASS, byte-identical). Files modified: `crates/editor-shell/tests/render_input_boundary.rs` (+55L), `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` (line 470 marker rewritten), `Status.md` + `change.md` + `HANDOFF.md` (this entry). **Phase 6 §6.3 Gate C status transition: handoff-substrate-landed → CLOSED for ADR-117 RenderHandoff boundary invariant** (single-threaded proxy; future dedicated renderer thread must keep the same invariant; does not certify a full render-thread architecture yet). **Phase 6 §6.3 final exit-criteria status**: PSO sharing CLOSED (line 471, commit 54cec89); Gate A CLOSED on recorder host (line 468, commits bb49f64 + 35e5078); Gate B CLOSED for CPU-idle interpretation (line 469, commits b73aa0f / 517aa88); **Gate C CLOSED for ADR-117 boundary invariant** (line 470, commits 1e719b7 → e6ed888 → 1545d15 → ac24b8d → this). The Gate C chain (5 dispatches: view-type → boundary discipline → ADR → primitive → empirical test) lands honestly: substrate exists, invariants are pinned, and future renderer-threading work has a contract to satisfy.

2026-05-11 (Phase 7 §7.3 cad-projection minimal gate-closure umbrella test — test-only dispatch, CLOSED): bounded test-only dispatch adds `crates/cad-projection/tests/phase_7_3_gate_closure.rs` (~390L incl. ~50L module-level doc + ~70L self-contained xorshift64 PRNG harness + ~280L gate test body + 1 PRNG sanity test), the consolidated §7.3 umbrella gate that mirrors §7.2's `phase_7_2_gate_closure.rs` idiom byte-for-byte where applicable. **Single gate test `phase_7_3_gate_closure_10_entities_100_edits_seed_0x7e5a_deae_3d49_c0e1`** with seed `0x7E5A_DEAE_3D49_C0E1` (`0x7E5A_DEAD_BEEF_C0DE.wrapping_add(0x7E5A_0003)` — §7.3 dispatch-tag offset from §7.2's lineage). Builds 10 `BRepHandle`-backed entities (each its own Cuboid cad node with distinct content-hashed `NodeId`s) then applies 100 random parametric edits: each picks an entity, generates new `(w,h,d)` in `[0.5, 3.0]` with edit-index jitter, adds a new Cuboid node, commits (head advances), `remap_entity` (post-2026-05-08 SSoT canonical path), then `tick`. **Five assertion categories per edit (~5 `assert*` per edit)**: (1) `cad.head().0` strictly advances per commit; (2) `report.head_advanced_to == cad.head()` — §7.3 invalidation-within-one-tick exit criterion; (3) `report.entities_reprojected == 10` (head advance + remap dirty-marks every known entity per `CadProjection::tick` docstring); (4) `projection.projected_mesh(target_entity)` returns `Some(mesh)` AND `mesh.{positions,indices,face_labels}` byte-equal `cad.graph().evaluate(new_node, &mut TessellationCache::new(), tol())` for the same node — triangle-fallback-always-available + `ProjectedMesh ↔ cad-core::evaluate()` consistency under a fresh tess cache; (5) `EntityCadMap` coherence: `node_for(entity) == Some(new_node)` AND `entity_for(new_node) == Some(target_entity)`. Initial-tick baseline checks all 10 against cad-core baseline before the loop; final post-loop sanity loops all 10 verifying coherence preserved across 100 edits. **Reused existing API (zero source / Cargo.toml changes)**: `CadProjection::{new,spawn_brep_entity,tick,projected_mesh,node_for,entity_for,remap_entity}`; `CadGraph::{new,begin_operation,graph_mut,commit,head,graph}`; `OperatorGraph::evaluate(node, &mut TessellationCache, Tolerance)`; `OperatorNode::Cuboid` / `CuboidOp`; `Tessellation.{positions,indices,face_labels}`; `World::{new,register_snapshot_component::<BRepHandle>}`. **3-run determinism confirmed**: identical "Phase 7.3 gate CLOSED: 10 entities × 100 edits = 1000 mutations. Seed: 0x7E5ADEAE3D49C0E1." stdout across consecutive runs. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `crates/cad-projection/src/**`, NO `crates/cad-core/src/**`, NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock`, NO existing-test edits (sibling `phase_7_2_gate_closure.rs` / `cad_projection_smoke.rs` / etc. byte-identical), NO new deps / modules / lints / ADRs; xorshift64 PRNG harness intentionally duplicated from §7.2 to keep §7.3 test self-contained (cross-crate test-helper would require Cargo.toml). **Phase 7 §7.x scoreboard fully closed at the same gate-test level as Phase 6**: §7.1 cad-core MVP (D-prime 2026-05-06), §7.2 persistent topology IDs (D-7.2-ζ.ζ `ae31dee`, gate-closure 1000 mutations seed `0x7E5A_DEAD_BEEF_C0DE`), §7.3 cad-projection minimal (THIS — gate-closure 1000 mutations seed `0x7E5A_DEAE_3D49_C0E1`), §7.4 topology lineage (D-7.4 prototype 2026-05-07). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-cad-projection` exit 0 (one formatter-touch on `tick` call); `cargo +nightly fmt --check` workspace exit 0; `cargo test -p rge-cad-projection --test phase_7_3_gate_closure` 2/2 PASS; `cargo test -p rge-cad-projection` ALL PASS; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2374 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2373; +1 net); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical). Files modified (5): NEW `crates/cad-projection/tests/phase_7_3_gate_closure.rs` (~390L); `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` (§7.3 inline `[CLOSED 2026-05-11 …]` marker mirroring §7.2 / §6.3 style); `Status.md` (this entry); `change.md` (this entry); `HANDOFF.md` (Phase 7 §7.3 status block update). **Remaining Phase 7 work**: only Phase 6 frame-graph (§6.x transient resources) + deferred follow-ups (renderer-thread spawn ADR, wire-format ADR, Vertex-ID propagation, Boolean topology-ID propagation, Fillet output identity unpark). Next focus: TBD by user — `cad-projection` substrate now closed at the gate-test level with byte-equal cad-core consistency verified across 1000 mutations.


2026-05-12 (Phase 6 frame-graph chapter — ADR-118 transient-resource allocator policy pinned, docs-only design dispatch): authors `docs/adr/ADR-118-frame-graph-transient-resource-allocator.md` (237L) — sibling design ADR to ADR-117 for the Phase 6 chapter. 7 sub-decisions pinned: D1 concrete TextureDescriptor + BufferDescriptor field shapes mirroring wgpu 29; D2 separate texture/buffer descriptors (NOT unified enum); D3 pool key = `(Descriptor, AliasingGroupId)`; D4 ring-buffered N frames-in-flight, initial N=2 lifetime policy; D5 greedy AliasingGroup → physical mapping using max-size-in-group; D6 trust wgpu auto-tracked hazard barriers (NOT manual emission); D7 dispatch 119 = ResourceDescriptor types + descriptor-flow through add_pass/compile (~150-250 LOC). 9 alternatives tabulated with reasons-rejected; 10 explicit non-decisions enumerated (cross-thread allocator, dynamic/persistent resources, VRAM placement primitives, shader/pipeline integration, label fields, mapped_at_creation, multi-queue, WAR/WAW, external-input swapchain, free-list ordering). Governance: docs/architecture/README.md ADR index 9 → 10 ADRs accepted; docs/§18/GFX_RENDER_TIER.md §11.1 frame-graph entry gains single-line ADR-118 cross-ref + dispatches 119/120/121 implementation arc. HARD NON-GOALS observed: NO .rs / NO Cargo.toml / NO Cargo.lock / NO architecture-lints / NO new lint / NO new doctrine doc / NO sub-ADR split / NO reopening §6.2 / §6.3 / Phase 7 closures. unsafe_code = "forbid" preserved by D6 trust-wgpu + D1 safe descriptors. Verification gates ALL GREEN: cargo +nightly fmt --check exit 0; cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical). Phase 6 frame-graph status: substrate-shipped (analytical) + ADR-118 (allocator policy) → next is dispatch 119 per D7 → 120 (pools) → 121 (FrameRecorder wiring) → Phase 6 umbrella exit gate. Files modified (5): NEW docs/adr/ADR-118-frame-graph-transient-resource-allocator.md (237L); docs/architecture/README.md (ADR count); docs/§18/GFX_RENDER_TIER.md (single-line cross-ref §11.1); Status.md + change.md + HANDOFF.md (mirroring).

2026-05-12 (Phase 6 frame-graph chapter — dispatch 119: ResourceDescriptor types + frame-graph descriptor flow, IMPLEMENTED): implements ADR-118 D7's first code step. NEW `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/descriptor.rs` (~350L incl. ~165L tests + ~50L module doc) introduces three public types: `TextureDescriptor` (9 wgpu-29 fields per D1: width / height / depth_or_array_layers / mip_level_count / sample_count / format / usage / dimension / view_dimension; `label` + `mapped_at_creation` deliberately omitted per D1 to prevent pool over-fragmentation); `BufferDescriptor` (size_bytes + usage per D1); `ResourceClassDescriptor::{Texture(TextureDescriptor), Buffer(BufferDescriptor)}` as the graph-time routing discriminator (pools remain separate at allocator level per D2; this enum exists purely for API uniformity through `add_pass`). All three derive `Clone + Copy + Debug + PartialEq + Eq + Hash` — `wgpu::TextureUsages` / `BufferUsages` / `TextureFormat` / `TextureDimension` / `TextureViewDimension` all derive `Hash + Eq` in wgpu-types 29 (verified via `/a/RustCache/cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-.../wgpu-types-29.0.3/src/{buffer,texture,texture/format}.rs`), so no hand-rolled wrapping needed. Helper `byte_size_estimate()` on both descriptors + the enum returns approximate bytes (uses `wgpu::TextureFormat::block_copy_size(None)` for textures + geometric-series 4/3 upper bound for mip pyramids) for dispatch 120's D5 "max descriptor in group" comparison. **API extension (option-a descriptor-on-write per dispatch brief)**: `FrameGraph::add_pass(name, reads: Vec<ResourceId>, writes: Vec<(ResourceId, ResourceClassDescriptor)>)` — writes carry descriptors; reads remain bare `ResourceId`. Sidecar `FrameGraph::descriptors: BTreeMap<ResourceId, ResourceClassDescriptor>` collects descriptors as `add_pass` observes writes — kept OFF `PassNode` to preserve content-derived `NodeId` stability under descriptor variation. **Descriptor consistency**: `add_pass` validates `descriptors.get(rid) == new_desc` BEFORE mutating the sidecar; conflict surfaces as new `FrameGraphError::DescriptorMismatch { resource, first_decl, conflicting }`. Mid-`writes`-vector conflict leaves no partial sidecar state (validation precedes any insert). **Compile-side**: `compile_passes(passes, descriptors)` clones the descriptor map verbatim into `CompiledFrameGraph::descriptors` (a new `#[serde(skip)]` field — wgpu types only derive serde behind a feature the workspace doesn't enable; substrate-honest given dispatch 119 ships no compiled-graph serialization path). NEW accessors `CompiledFrameGraph::descriptors() -> &BTreeMap<…>` + `descriptor(id: ResourceId) -> Option<&ResourceClassDescriptor>`. **Descriptors are NOT factored into `structural_hash`** — the analytical determinism contract remains orthogonal to descriptor metadata; two compiles with the same pass topology but different descriptors still produce equal structural hashes (verified by new test `structural_hash_orthogonal_to_descriptors`). **Optional `AliasingGroup::largest_descriptor` helper deferred** to dispatch 120 (where it lives next to the pool that consumes it). **`tests/frame_graph_smoke.rs` updated**: existing 3-pass `geometry / shadow / lighting` smoke now passes realistic descriptors (1920×1080 Depth32Float depth target, 1920×1080 Rgba8Unorm scene-color + lit color targets, 2048×2048 R32Float shadow map) and asserts the 4 descriptors round-trip through compile + recompile. NEW second smoke test `frame_graph_smoke_buffer_descriptor_round_trip` exercises a mixed buffer + texture graph (256-entry mat4 uniform buffer feeding an Rgba8Unorm render target) to prove the routing enum dispatches correctly. **Test deltas (workspace 2374 → 2398, +24 net)**: 16 unit tests in `descriptor.rs` (TD-1 per-field distinct checks for both descriptor types covering width / format / view_dimension / size_bytes / usage; TD-2 `ResourceClassDescriptor` clone+eq for both variants + cross-variant distinctness + From impls; byte-size estimate baseline + mip-chain 4/3 factor + buffer trivial + dispatch enum); 3 new tests in `compile.rs` (FG-D1 round-trip; mixed texture+buffer; structural-hash orthogonality); 4 new tests in `mod.rs` (FG-D1 add-pass-with-descriptors-compiles; FG-D2 conflicting-descriptor → DescriptorMismatch with full payload assertion; matching-descriptor accepted; mid-vector conflict leaves no partial state); 1 new smoke for buffer-descriptor round-trip. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-gfx` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-gfx --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` clean (0 errors / 0 new warnings, 2.37s); `cargo test -p rge-gfx --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` ALL PASS (156 unit + 19 integration); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` = **2398 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2374; +24 net); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical). **HARD NON-GOALS observed (verified `git diff --stat`)**: NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` changes; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` touches; NO `wgpu::Device` calls (descriptors are POD types — pool dispatch 120 owns wgpu allocation); NO `TexturePool` / `BufferPool` implementation; NO `FrameRecorder` / `LitMeshPipeline` / `MeshPipeline` edits (those are dispatch 121 / 122's lane); NO `unsafe` (workspace `forbid` preserved); NO other gfx modules touched (`material.rs` / `pso_cache.rs` / `intent_adapter.rs` / `surface.rs` byte-identical); NO ADR / doctrine / §18 / new lint / new exemption. Files modified (only 4 + 1 new): NEW `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/descriptor.rs` (~350L); `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/mod.rs` (+223 / -37 net = ~186 delta — sidecar field + DescriptorMismatch variant + add_pass signature + 4 new tests); `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile.rs` (+160 / -? — descriptors-in/out wiring + 3 new tests + 1 in-place `#[serde(skip)]` field); `crates/gfx/src/lib.rs` (+1 net — 3 new re-exports: BufferDescriptor / ResourceClassDescriptor / TextureDescriptor); `crates/gfx/tests/frame_graph_smoke.rs` (+122 / -? — descriptors threaded through existing 3-pass smoke + 1 new buffer-descriptor smoke); `Status.md` + `change.md` + `HANDOFF.md` (this entry). **Phase 6 frame-graph status transition**: ADR-118 policy (2026-05-12 docs-only) → **dispatch 119 descriptors flowing (THIS)** → dispatch 120 = `TexturePool` + `BufferPool` MVP per D3 / D4 / D5 (next) → dispatch 121 = `FrameRecorder` wiring → Phase 6 umbrella exit gate (60fps simple-scene). **Dispatch 120 readiness**: `CompiledFrameGraph::descriptors()` + `aliasing_groups()` combined give dispatch 120's pool the full `(Descriptor, AliasingGroupId)` key surface per ADR-118 D3; the "largest descriptor in group" D5 step is a 1-pass `groups.iter().filter_map(|g| g.0.iter().filter_map(|r| descriptors.get(r)).max_by_key(|d| d.byte_size_estimate()))` over the now-available data. Substrate sufficient.

2026-05-12 (Phase 6 frame-graph chapter — dispatch 120: TexturePool MVP per ADR-118 D3+D4+D5, IMPLEMENTED): lands the descriptor-keyed transient-texture pool with `(TextureDescriptor, AliasingGroupId)` keying, ring-buffered N=2 frames-in-flight via `begin_frame()` slot rotation, and greedy per-group acquisition. NEW `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/texture_pool.rs` (278L total — module doc + impl + 5 unit tests). Two new public types: **`AliasingGroupId(pub u32)`** — newtype wrapping `u32` to index `CompiledFrameGraph::aliasing_groups()` (position-based identity; deterministic per `BTreeMap<ResourceId,_>` ordering in `compile.rs`; content-hash variant deferred to a future amendment when cross-graph stability surfaces); **`TexturePool`** — owns `[SlotState; 2]` ring + `current_slot: usize`. Per-slot `SlotState` carries `free_lists: HashMap<(TextureDescriptor, AliasingGroupId), Vec<Arc<wgpu::Texture>>>` (free-list keyed per ADR-118 D3) plus `active: Vec<(TextureDescriptor, AliasingGroupId, Arc<wgpu::Texture>)>` (flat triple-vector — the descriptor is carried alongside each Arc because wgpu 29's `Texture` does not expose the original `TextureDescriptor` for free-list routing at slot reset; simpler than two maps). **Three public methods**: `new()` / `default()` start at slot 0 with empty slots; `begin_frame()` advances `current_slot = (current_slot + 1) % FRAMES_IN_FLIGHT` then drains the new slot's `active` triple-vec back into its `free_lists` keyed per-triple (the rotation policy per ADR-118 D4); `acquire(device, descriptor, group)` peeks `free_lists[(*descriptor, group)]` via `get_mut + pop`, falling back to `device.create_texture(...)` via the internal `allocate_texture` helper, then pushes `(descriptor, group, Arc::clone)` into `active` and returns the `Arc<wgpu::Texture>`. **`allocate_texture` helper** translates `TextureDescriptor` → `wgpu::TextureDescriptor` (label = None per ADR-118 D1; view_formats = &[] — view creation is the caller's lane). **`slot_stats()` accessor deliberately deferred** — the LOC budget tightened, and the dispatch brief flagged it as nice-to-have only; ship without; tests cover the same invariants through `Arc::ptr_eq` / `strong_count`. **Re-exports**: `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/mod.rs` adds `pub mod texture_pool;` + `pub use texture_pool::{AliasingGroupId, TexturePool};`; `crates/gfx/src/lib.rs` adds `AliasingGroupId, TexturePool` to the existing `frame_graph` re-export block. **Tests (+5 net, workspace 2398 → 2403)**: TP-1 structural (`new_pool_starts_at_slot_zero_and_begin_frame_rotates`) verifies the empty-start state + 0→1→0→1 rotation through 3 begin_frame calls; TP-2 GPU-gated (`acquire_with_real_device_returns_texture`) confirms acquire succeeds + pool retains its Arc in active (strong_count = 2); TP-3 GPU-gated (`acquire_then_full_ring_returns_texture_to_free_list`) — the load-bearing free-list-reuse proof: acquire X → drop caller-ref → begin_frame (slot 0 → 1) → begin_frame (slot 1 → 0, drains slot-0 active into free-list) → acquire same key returns the SAME wgpu allocation via `Arc::as_ptr` equality; TP-4 GPU-gated (`different_groups_with_same_descriptor_get_distinct_textures`) confirms distinct `AliasingGroupId`s produce distinct allocations even with identical descriptors per ADR-118 D3 (key includes group); TP-5 GPU-gated (`different_descriptors_in_same_group_get_distinct_textures`) confirms distinct descriptors in the same group produce distinct allocations. Uses local `ctx_or_skip!` macro mirroring `lit_mesh_pipeline.rs`'s pattern; 4 of 5 tests gated on `GfxContext::new_headless()` success. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `BufferPool` (dispatch 121); NO `wgpu::Queue` interaction (no `write_texture` / `write_buffer` / no uploads); NO manual hazard barriers (ADR-118 D6 trusts wgpu auto-tracking); NO `FrameRecorder` integration (dispatch 121); NO `GfxContext` mutation (`context.rs` byte-identical); NO async / fence ownership (renderer-thread spawn ADR future work); NO serialized-graph support (`CompiledFrameGraph::descriptors` is `#[serde(skip)]`; sibling wire-format ADR concern); NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` changes; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` touches; NO `unsafe` (workspace `forbid` preserved); NO other gfx modules touched (`frame_graph/{descriptor, compile, pass, resource}.rs` byte-identical aside from `mod.rs` re-export delta; `frame.rs` / `lit_mesh_pipeline.rs` / `mesh_pipeline.rs` / `pipeline.rs` / `material.rs` / `surface.rs` / `pso_cache.rs` / `intent_adapter.rs` all byte-identical); NO ADR / doctrine / §18 / new lint / new exemption. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-gfx` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-gfx --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` clean; `cargo test -p rge-gfx --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml --lib texture_pool` = **5/5 PASS**; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` = **2403 passed / 0 failed** (was 2398; +5 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical). Files modified (only 4 + 1 new): NEW `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/texture_pool.rs` (278L); `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/mod.rs` (+2L re-export delta only); `crates/gfx/src/lib.rs` (+2 idents in `frame_graph::` re-export); `Status.md` + `change.md` + `HANDOFF.md` (this entry). **Phase 6 frame-graph status transition**: dispatch 119 descriptors flowing (2026-05-12) → **dispatch 120 TexturePool landed (THIS)** → dispatch 121 = `BufferPool` mirror per ADR-118 D2 (next; sibling pool, same key shape, same N=2 ring, same single-`acquire` surface — clean mirror of TexturePool) → dispatch 122 = `FrameRecorder` wiring (`record_lit_mesh_pass` consumes `ResourceId → PhysicalHandle` resolution at the pool boundary) → Phase 6 umbrella exit gate (60fps simple-scene). **Dispatch 121 readiness**: BufferPool is a clean mirror of TexturePool — `BufferDescriptor` already shipped (dispatch 119); same `(Descriptor, AliasingGroupId)` key shape; same N=2 ring policy per D4; same greedy per-group surface per D5; `device.create_buffer(&wgpu::BufferDescriptor { ... mapped_at_creation: false, ... })` substitutes for `create_texture`. Cleared to launch.

2026-05-12 (Phase 6 frame-graph chapter — dispatch 121: BufferPool MVP per ADR-118 D2+D3+D4+D5, IMPLEMENTED): lands the descriptor-keyed transient-buffer pool as a clean mirror of dispatch 120's TexturePool (commit `e050e06`). NEW `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/buffer_pool.rs` (268L total — ~50L module doc + impl + 5 unit tests). Single new public type **`BufferPool`** — owns `[SlotState; 2]` ring + `current_slot: usize` (identical shape to `TexturePool`). Per-slot `SlotState` carries `free_lists: HashMap<(BufferDescriptor, AliasingGroupId), Vec<Arc<wgpu::Buffer>>>` (free-list keyed per ADR-118 D3) plus `active: Vec<(BufferDescriptor, AliasingGroupId, Arc<wgpu::Buffer>)>` (flat triple-vector — same rationale as TexturePool: wgpu 29's `Buffer` does not expose the original `BufferDescriptor` for free-list routing at slot reset). **Three public methods**: `new()` / `default()` start at slot 0 with empty slots; `begin_frame()` advances `current_slot = (current_slot + 1) % FRAMES_IN_FLIGHT` then drains the new slot's `active` triple-vec back into its `free_lists` keyed per-triple (D4 ring rotation); `acquire(device, descriptor, group)` peeks `free_lists[(*descriptor, group)]` via `get_mut + pop`, falling back to `device.create_buffer(...)` via the internal `allocate_buffer` helper, then pushes `(descriptor, group, Arc::clone)` into `active` and returns the `Arc<wgpu::Buffer>`. **`allocate_buffer` helper** translates `BufferDescriptor` → `wgpu::BufferDescriptor` (label = None per D1; `size = descriptor.size_bytes`; `usage = descriptor.usage`; `mapped_at_creation = false` per D1 — transient resources never CPU-map at creation). **`AliasingGroupId` reuse via `use super::texture_pool::AliasingGroupId;`** — graph-time identity is shared across both pool kinds (D2 separates pool *types*, not identities); NO redefinition. **`FRAMES_IN_FLIGHT` duplicated as a 1-LOC `const`** rather than hoisted — at this dispatch's scope, churn of moving a 1-LOC const would exceed the payoff; both pools amend it together if N changes. **`texture_pool.rs` BEHAVIORAL byte-identical** — only references it gains is by name via `AliasingGroupId` import; no edits to texture_pool source. **Re-exports**: `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/mod.rs` adds `pub mod buffer_pool;` + `pub use buffer_pool::BufferPool;`; `crates/gfx/src/lib.rs` adds `BufferPool` to the existing `frame_graph::` re-export block (formatter regrouped the line, semantically identical). **ADR-118 wording correction (single line at L212)**: "Dispatch 120 — `TexturePool` + `BufferPool` MVP per D3 / D4 / D5" → "Dispatch 120 — `TexturePool` MVP per D3 / D4 / D5; dispatch 121 — `BufferPool` MVP (clean mirror)"; records the dispatch-120/121 split at the moment BufferPool makes the split true. Filenames updated to `texture_pool.rs` / `buffer_pool.rs` from `allocator.rs` placeholder. Not scope creep — ADR wording correction folded into the dispatch that closes the split. **Tests (+5 net, workspace 2403 → 2408)**: BP-1 structural (`new_pool_starts_at_slot_zero_and_begin_frame_rotates`) verifies empty-start + 0→1→0→1 rotation across 3 `begin_frame` calls; BP-2 GPU-gated (`acquire_with_real_device_returns_buffer`) confirms acquire returns an Arc with strong_count=2; BP-3 GPU-gated (`acquire_then_full_ring_returns_buffer_to_free_list`) — the load-bearing free-list-reuse proof: acquire X → drop caller-ref → `begin_frame` × 2 (drains slot-0 active into free-list at wrap-back) → acquire same key returns the SAME wgpu::Buffer allocation via `Arc::as_ptr` equality; BP-4 GPU-gated (`different_groups_with_same_descriptor_get_distinct_buffers`) confirms distinct `AliasingGroupId`s produce distinct allocations under identical descriptor (D3 key includes group); BP-5 GPU-gated (`different_descriptors_in_same_group_get_distinct_buffers`) confirms distinct descriptors in the same group produce distinct allocations (size 4096 vs 8192). Local `ctx_or_skip!` macro mirroring `texture_pool.rs`'s pattern. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `wgpu::Queue` interaction (no `write_buffer` / no uploads); NO `map_async` / `unmap` / staging-buffer workflows (D1 "no mapped_at_creation" decision keeps those outside this allocator); NO manual hazard barriers (D6 trusts wgpu auto-tracking); NO `FrameRecorder` integration (dispatch 122); NO `TexturePool` BEHAVIORAL change (texture_pool.rs byte-identical); NO `GfxContext` mutation; NO async / fence ownership; NO serialized-graph support; NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock`; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**`; NO `unsafe`; NO new ADR / new lint / new exemption; NO other gfx modules touched (`frame_graph/{descriptor, compile, pass, resource, texture_pool}.rs` byte-identical aside from `mod.rs` re-export delta; `frame.rs` / `lit_mesh_pipeline.rs` / `mesh_pipeline.rs` / `pipeline.rs` / `material.rs` / `surface.rs` / `pso_cache.rs` / `intent_adapter.rs` all byte-identical). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-gfx` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-gfx --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` clean; `cargo test -p rge-gfx --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml --lib buffer_pool` = **5/5 PASS**; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` = **2408 passed / 0 failed** (was 2403; +5 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical). Files modified (3 + 1 new): NEW `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/buffer_pool.rs` (268L); `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/mod.rs` (+2L re-export delta only); `crates/gfx/src/lib.rs` (+1 ident in `frame_graph::` re-export — formatter regrouped the line); `docs/adr/ADR-118-frame-graph-transient-resource-allocator.md` (single-line L212 wording correction recording the dispatch-120/121 split); `Status.md` + `change.md` + `HANDOFF.md` (this entry). **Phase 6 frame-graph status transition**: dispatch 120 TexturePool (commit `e050e06`) → **dispatch 121 BufferPool landed (THIS)** → dispatch 122 = `FrameRecorder` wiring (`record_lit_mesh_pass` consumes `ResourceId → PhysicalHandle` resolution at the pool boundary) → Phase 6 umbrella exit gate (60fps simple-scene). **Dispatch 122 readiness**: both pools have identical `acquire(device, descriptor, group) -> Arc<wgpu::{Texture,Buffer}>` surface; FrameRecorder integration needs (a) per-frame `pool.begin_frame()` invocation at execution start, (b) `ResourceId → AliasingGroupId` lookup via `CompiledFrameGraph::aliasing_groups()` position, (c) per-resource descriptor pickup via `CompiledFrameGraph::descriptor(rid)`, (d) the `acquire()` dedup contract — caller MUST map each `AliasingGroupId` to one `Arc` per frame (otherwise distinct `acquire` calls within the same group return distinct allocations defeating aliasing). The dedup contract is load-bearing for D5 aliasing correctness and is flagged in HANDOFF.md.

2026-05-12 (ADR-118 dispatch-numbering cleanup — docs-only): tiny mechanical cleanup aligning ADR-118's future-work and dispatch-numbering text with shipped commits 119 (`a569a60`) / 120 (`e050e06`) / 121 (`c356f32`). Same drift category as the earlier Status.md / IMPLEMENTATION.md cleanup (commit `16d0b2e`), scoped to ADR-118 only. **8 stale lines corrected** (5 in the initial amend + 3 caught on review): (L142) "the integration with `FrameRecorder` is dispatch 121" → "dispatch 120 lands `TexturePool` (D3 / D4 / D5); dispatch 121 lands `BufferPool` (clean mirror); the integration with `FrameRecorder` is dispatch 122"; (L174) "dispatch 119 lands descriptors, 120 lands pools, 121 wires to `FrameRecorder`" → "dispatch 119 lands descriptors, 120 lands `TexturePool`, 121 lands `BufferPool` (clean mirror), 122 wires the pools to `FrameRecorder`"; (L201 non-decision row 4) "Dispatch 121 implements this wiring" → "Dispatch 122 implements this wiring"; (L206 non-decision row 9) "dispatch 121+'s concern when `FrameRecorder` integration lands" → "dispatch 122+'s concern when `FrameRecorder` integration lands"; (L207 non-decision row 10) "implementation detail of dispatch 120" → "implementation detail of the pool dispatches (120 / 121)" — captures the TexturePool/BufferPool split per ADR-118 D2; (L212 future-work pools row) "Footprint ~250-400 LOC each ... NO `wgpu::Device` actually called in unit tests (mock the descriptor → physical-handle surface); integration smoke covers real wgpu pool behavior" → "Footprint ~250-280 LOC each (TexturePool 278L; BufferPool 268L) ... Pool unit tests are GPU-gated via the `ctx_or_skip!` macro: they use a real `wgpu::Device` when available and skip gracefully on headless CI (mirroring the `lit_mesh_pipeline.rs` test pattern)"; (L213 future-work FrameRecorder row) "Dispatch 121 — `FrameRecorder` integration" → "Dispatch 122 — `FrameRecorder` integration" + appended **LOAD-BEARING `acquire()` dedup contract** description (caller MUST build per-frame `ResourceMap` via single `acquire()` per AliasingGroupId; pass-record sites read from ResourceMap only — otherwise aliasing-group reuse correctness is defeated); (L214 Phase-6-umbrella-exit-gate row) "once dispatch 121 lands" → "once dispatch 122 lands" (the chapter's exit criterion is measurable once the FrameRecorder integration substrate is in place). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `.rs` edits; NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` changes; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` changes; NO substantive ADR-118 content edits (decision rationale + alternatives table + consequences + non-decisions text byte-identical aside from the 8 listed dispatch-numbering / test-claim wording fixes); NO new ADR / new doctrine doc; NO reopening §6.x / Phase 7 closures; NO Status.md rotation (no concrete stale current-state claim found in this scope). ADR-118 net delta well within the +15-line dispatch budget. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check` exit 0 (no source changes); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical). Files modified: `docs/adr/ADR-118-frame-graph-transient-resource-allocator.md` (8 mechanical line-corrections); `change.md` (this entry). After this lands, dispatch 122 inspect-first becomes the clean next move with an ADR-118 baseline that matches the actual shipped substrate.

2026-05-12 (Phase 6 frame-graph chapter — dispatch 122: `ResourceMap` builder + `AliasingGroup::max_descriptor` analytical helper, IMPLEMENTED): lands the per-frame `ResourceId → Arc<wgpu::{Texture,Buffer}>` mapping that consumes dispatches 120 (`e050e06`) + 121 (`c356f32`) and structurally enforces ADR-118's `acquire()` dedup contract (ADR-118 L213, post-cleanup `776b6b1`). NEW `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/resource_map.rs` (~330L total — ~50L module doc + ~95L impl + ~185L tests). Two new public types: **`ResourceMap`** = `{ texture_map: BTreeMap<ResourceId, Arc<wgpu::Texture>>, buffer_map: BTreeMap<ResourceId, Arc<wgpu::Buffer>> }` per ADR-118 D2 (pools separate at the allocator level; resource-class discrimination happens at the builder boundary based on each group's max descriptor); **`ResourceMapError::MissingDescriptorForGroup(AliasingGroupId)`** — diagnostic-only failure mode for partial / hand-built `CompiledFrameGraph`s; well-formed graphs produced by `FrameGraph::add_pass` cannot trigger it. **`build_resource_map(compiled, device, &mut tex_pool, &mut buf_pool) -> Result<ResourceMap, ResourceMapError>`** iterates `CompiledFrameGraph::aliasing_groups()` exactly once, computes the max descriptor per group via the new `AliasingGroup::max_descriptor` analytical helper, calls each pool's `acquire(...)` exactly ONCE per group, then fans the returned `Arc` to every `ResourceId` member of the group. This is THE structural enforcement of ADR-118 L213's load-bearing `acquire()` dedup contract — no internal pool dedup is needed because the iteration discipline at the builder makes intra-group acquires impossible by construction. **`AliasingGroup::max_descriptor` analytical helper** (~25 LOC net delta in `compile.rs` impl block): `self.0.iter().filter_map(|rid| descriptors.get(rid)).max_by_key(|d| d.byte_size_estimate())`; returns `None` for empty groups OR groups whose members have no descriptors registered (the precondition that surfaces as `MissingDescriptorForGroup` at the builder boundary). Pure analytical — no wgpu dependency; safe to use from any compile-time analysis path. **Re-exports**: `frame_graph/mod.rs` adds `pub mod resource_map;` + `pub use resource_map::{ResourceMap, ResourceMapError, build_resource_map};`; `lib.rs` adds the three idents to the existing `frame_graph::` re-export block (formatter regrouped the list; semantically identical). **ADR-118 L213 wording softening (single bullet, β path per user choice — honesty about current coupling)**: BEFORE = "Dispatch 122 — `FrameRecorder` integration. Wires the pools to the existing `record_lit_mesh_pass` surface; pass-recording consumes `(ResourceId, PassContext)` and the allocator resolves `ResourceId → wgpu::TextureView / wgpu::BufferBinding` per the pre-frame allocation cycle. Footprint ~200-300 LOC including a real GPU integration test against `ctx_or_skip!`-style availability."; AFTER = "Dispatch 122 — `ResourceMap` builder + `AliasingGroup::max_descriptor` analytical helper. Provides the per-frame `ResourceId → Arc<wgpu::Texture> / Arc<wgpu::Buffer>` mapping that enables transient-resource consumption from pass-record sites when they grow such consumers (post-Phase 6 exit gate). Footprint ~150-200 LOC. `FrameRecorder` + `record_lit_mesh_pass` are intentionally untouched — today's pass-record sites have no transient-resource consumers; the integration with them is future work." LOAD-BEARING `acquire()` dedup contract paragraph PRESERVED (now structurally enforced by `build_resource_map`'s iteration discipline); wording adjusted to reference `build_resource_map` as the structural enforcement point. L142/L174/L201/L206/L207/L212/L214 byte-identical (those were corrected in `776b6b1`). **Honest scope discovery**: dispatch 122 inspect verified that `record_lit_mesh_pass` is a free function with no transient-resource consumer today (signature: `&mut wgpu::CommandEncoder, &HeadlessTarget, &LitMeshPipeline, &Camera, &DirectionalLight, &Material, &LitMesh, wgpu::Color` — every input is a persistent resource or a value type) and that `editor-shell::render_frame` opens its own `wgpu::CommandEncoder` directly and never touches `FrameRecorder` or the frame-graph substrate. The honest deliverable is therefore enabling substrate (the per-frame ResourceMap), not synthetic FrameRecorder wiring against sites that have no transient-resource consumers. **Tests (+9 net; workspace 2408 → 2417)**: 5 in `resource_map.rs::tests` — RM-1 GPU-gated `build_resource_map_dedup_single_group_returns_arc_equal_handles` (4-pass graph; R1 lifetime [0,1] then R2 lifetime [2,3] non-overlapping → same aliasing group; `Arc::ptr_eq(arc1, arc2) == true` via the resource map; load-bearing dedup proof); RM-2 GPU-gated `build_resource_map_distinct_groups_get_distinct_arcs` (2-pass; both writes share lifetime → MUST occupy distinct groups; distinct Arcs); RM-3 GPU-gated `build_resource_map_mixed_texture_and_buffer_routes_correctly` (mixed-class; texture in `texture_map` only, buffer in `buffer_map` only, no cross-routing); RM-4 analytical `build_resource_map_well_formed_graph_has_descriptors_for_every_group` (FrameGraph::compile precondition); RM-5 analytical `aliasing_group_max_descriptor_none_implies_missing_descriptor_error_class` (the `None` branch IS the `MissingDescriptorForGroup` precondition). 4 in `compile.rs::tests` — AG-MAX-1 `aliasing_group_max_descriptor_picks_largest_texture` (textures of side 1024 / 4096 / 2048; max returns 4096²); AG-MAX-2 `aliasing_group_max_descriptor_picks_largest_buffer` (sizes 1 / 16 / 4 KiB; max returns 16 KiB); AG-MAX-3 `aliasing_group_max_descriptor_returns_none_for_empty_group`; AG-MAX-4 `aliasing_group_max_descriptor_returns_none_for_missing_descriptors`. Local `ctx_or_skip!` macro mirroring `texture_pool.rs` / `buffer_pool.rs` patterns; 3 of 5 resource_map tests are GPU-gated (RM-1/2/3); RM-4/5 + all AG-MAX tests are pure analytical. **HARD NON-GOALS observed (verified `git diff --stat`)**: NO `FrameRecorder` touch (`frame.rs` byte-identical); NO `record_lit_mesh_pass` signature change (`lit_mesh_pipeline.rs` byte-identical); NO `editor-shell::render_path` edits (`editor-shell/**` byte-identical); NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` changes; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` changes; NO `unsafe` (workspace `forbid` preserved); NO new ADR / new lint / new exemption; NO ADR-118 line touched beyond L213 (L142/L174/L201/L206/L207/L212/L214 byte-identical); NO view caching; NO `wgpu::Queue` interaction beyond existing pool patterns; NO `cad-projection` / `cad-core` edits; NO other gfx modules touched (`{texture_pool,buffer_pool,descriptor,pass,resource,frame,lit_mesh_pipeline,mesh_pipeline,pipeline,material,surface,pso_cache,intent_adapter,mesh}.rs` byte-identical aside from `frame_graph/mod.rs` re-export delta and `compile.rs` `max_descriptor` impl + 4 tests + 2 helper fns). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-gfx` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-gfx --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` clean (2.62s); `cargo test -p rge-gfx --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` ALL PASS (175 lib + integration suites — 4 GPU-gated tests in resource_map skip on no-GPU; structural test runs always); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` = **2417 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2408; +9 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical). Files modified (3 + 1 new): NEW `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/resource_map.rs` (~330L); `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile.rs` (+~70L: `max_descriptor` helper + 4 unit tests + 2 helper fns `tex_d_size` / `buf_d_size`); `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/mod.rs` (+3L re-export delta only); `crates/gfx/src/lib.rs` (+3 idents in `frame_graph::` re-export); `docs/adr/ADR-118-frame-graph-transient-resource-allocator.md` (single-bullet L213 wording softening — β honesty path); `Status.md` + `change.md` + `HANDOFF.md` (this entry). **Phase 6 frame-graph chapter substrate complete**: dispatches 119 (descriptors, `a569a60`) + 120 (TexturePool, `e050e06`) + 121 (BufferPool, `c356f32`) + 122 (ResourceMap builder + max_descriptor, THIS) — the substrate is sufficient for Phase 6 umbrella exit gate (60fps simple-scene at `IMPLEMENTATION.md:468`). **Next focus**: Phase 6 umbrella exit gate OR Phase 7 catalog handoff / pause. Pass-record integration (`FrameRecorder` / `record_lit_mesh_pass` consuming transient resources from the `ResourceMap`) is intentional future work that lands when those sites grow consumers — the substrate now enables that wiring at zero cost. **Dispatch 122 substrate-usage recipe (for future integration)**: (a) compile `FrameGraph` → `CompiledFrameGraph`; (b) `tex_pool.begin_frame(); buf_pool.begin_frame();` at frame start; (c) `let map = build_resource_map(&compiled, device, &mut tex_pool, &mut buf_pool)?;`; (d) pass-record sites read `map.texture_map[&rid]` / `map.buffer_map[&rid]` for the resolved `Arc<wgpu::{Texture,Buffer}>`. The dedup contract is structurally enforced — pass-record sites must NOT call `pool.acquire(...)` directly.

2026-05-12 (Phase 6 frame-graph chapter — dispatch 123: umbrella analytical smoke + docs coherence, IMPLEMENTED): lands the cross-frame composition test `crates/gfx/tests/frame_graph_umbrella_smoke.rs` (189L) that exercises the chapter substrate end-to-end without touching FrameRecorder or real pass-record sites — `FrameGraph::add_pass × 3 → compile() → TexturePool::new + BufferPool::new → build_resource_map (frame 0) → both pools' begin_frame() → build_resource_map (frame 1)`. **Cross-frame freshness invariant per ADR-118 D4 PROVED**: for the same `ResourceId` across frames, `Arc::ptr_eq(frame_0_arc, frame_1_arc)` is FALSE — slot 0 holds frame-0 Arcs (in `tex_pool.slots[0].active`); after `begin_frame()` rotates to slot 1 whose `free_lists` are empty on first visit, `build_resource_map` allocates fresh. Pool-level rotation was tested in isolation (`texture_pool.rs:196` / `buffer_pool.rs:186`); the ResourceMap composition through `begin_frame` is now tested end-to-end. **Four assertions**: (1) every declared ResourceId appears in the map (all-texture graph → buffer_map empty); (2) within-frame aliasing-group Arc dedup (structural enforcement of ADR-118 L213's load-bearing dedup contract at the builder layer; every multi-member group's members map to the same Arc; precondition test that the 3-pass shadow/scene/lighting shape yields ≥1 multi-member group); (3) cross-frame freshness — distinct Arc allocations per ResourceId across slots 0 and 1 per N=2 ring policy; (4) structural_hash stable across recompile (regression guard for the deterministic-substrate contract under umbrella composition). **Docs coherence pass (additive)**: Status.md line 5 (the dispatch-122 entry, now pushed down after the dispatch-123 prepend) — "next is the Phase 6 umbrella exit gate (60fps simple-scene) OR pivot to Phase 7 / pause" framing rewritten to reflect Gate A CLOSED-on-recorder-host reality + deferred runtime-perf re-validation; `docs/§18/GFX_RENDER_TIER.md` line 234 "dispatch 121 (FrameRecorder wiring)" stale phrasing softened to enumerate the actual dispatch 119/120/121/122/123 sequence + intentional FrameRecorder integration deferral; `ADR-118` L214 reworded — "once dispatch 122 lands" replaced with "becomes the chapter's exit criterion at the substrate level once the umbrella-composition smoke (dispatch 123) lands; runtime-perf re-validation against Gate A's recorder-host CLOSED marker is deferred until real pass-record sites grow transient-resource consumers"; `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` §6 gains a small umbrella-close-out note BETWEEN the existing exit-criteria list (last bullet line 471 "100 material instances share one PSO") and "#### Abort condition" (additive; line 468 Gate A CLOSED marker UNTOUCHED — byte-identical, verified by re-read). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO FrameRecorder touch (`frame.rs` byte-identical); NO `record_lit_mesh_pass` signature change (`lit_mesh_pipeline.rs` byte-identical); NO editor-shell edits; NO Gate A CLOSED marker edit at `IMPLEMENTATION.md:468`; NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` changes; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` changes; NO new ADR / new lint / new exemption; NO frame-graph substrate file touches (`frame_graph/{pass,resource,descriptor,texture_pool,buffer_pool,compile,resource_map,mod}.rs` byte-identical — substrate frozen post-dispatch-122); NO chapter "fully closed" framing — "substrate complete + cross-frame composable; runtime-perf re-validation deferred" is the honest scope. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-gfx --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-gfx --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` clean (0.84s); `cargo test -p rge-gfx --test frame_graph_umbrella_smoke` 1/1 PASS (0.63s); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --manifest-path A:\RCAD\RGE\Cargo.toml` = **2418 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2417; +1 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical). Files modified (4 + 1 new): NEW `crates/gfx/tests/frame_graph_umbrella_smoke.rs` (189L); `Status.md` (this snapshot prepend + line-5 in-place correction); `docs/§18/GFX_RENDER_TIER.md` (line 234 dispatch-numbering phrasing softened); `docs/adr/ADR-118-frame-graph-transient-resource-allocator.md` (L214 rewording — drops "once dispatch 122 lands" recursion); `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` (§6 additive umbrella-close note; line 468 Gate A CLOSED marker UNTOUCHED); `change.md` (this entry); `HANDOFF.md` (chapter-status update). **Phase 6 frame-graph chapter status — frozen at this dispatch**: substrate complete + cross-frame composable; runtime-perf re-validation deferred. Pass-record integration (FrameRecorder / record_lit_mesh_pass consuming transient resources) is intentional future work and lands when those sites grow consumers — substrate is now complete enough to enable that wiring at zero cost.

2026-05-12 (cad-projection broader dep-style sweep — closes HANDOFF.md:1119 deferral, mechanical hygiene): `crates/cad-projection/Cargo.toml` switches `rge-kernel-ecs` + `rge-kernel-graph-foundation` from `{ path = "../../kernel/ecs" }` / `{ path = "../../kernel/graph-foundation" }` to `{ workspace = true }` (2-line delta). Closes the deferral the 2026-05-09 round-2 dep-style normalization explicitly noted at `HANDOFF.md:1119` ("cad-projection's `rge-kernel-ecs` + `rge-kernel-graph-foundation` still path-style — broader sweep deferred to next clean-up"). Workspace root `Cargo.toml:224` + `:236` already register both kernel crates in `[workspace.dependencies]` as path-based; the change is purely about which name resolves the path — Cargo.lock is **byte-identical** (sha256 `B4F4A7DF...` before and after; both styles resolve to the same workspace path-based `rge-kernel-ecs 0.0.1` / `rge-kernel-graph-foundation 0.0.1`). After this, cad-projection aligns with the workspace-managed convention used by sibling callsites editor-ui / editor-state / physics (for `rge-kernel-ecs`) + gfx / asset-store / kernel/asset (for `rge-kernel-graph-foundation`) + cad-projection's own post-2026-05-09 `rge-kernel-plugin-host` + `rge-kernel-diagnostics` edges. **Convention scoreboard after this dispatch**: `rge-kernel-ecs` workspace-style now 4 of 11 callsites (editor-ui + editor-state + physics + cad-projection); path-style remaining 7 (cad-core + editor-shell + editor-actions + script-host + script-bench + rge-editor + the workspace-root declaration itself). `rge-kernel-graph-foundation` workspace-style now 4 of 6 callsites (asset-store + kernel/asset + gfx + cad-projection); path-style remaining 2 (cad-core + the workspace-root declaration itself). Remaining path-style callsites are intentionally out of scope (each requires its own inspect-decide-execute pass; cad-core is the closest parallel target since it uses both deps in the same way). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO source code touched (every `use rge_kernel_ecs::...` / `use rge_kernel_graph_foundation::...` resolves identically under both styles — workspace-root declarations are themselves path-based, so the resolution graph is unchanged); NO public API change (cad-projection's `lib.rs` re-export surface byte-identical); NO behavior change; NO `Cargo.lock` churn (sha256 identical); NO other crate's `Cargo.toml` edited; NO architecture-lint changes; NO new ADR / new lint / new doctrine doc / new exemption; NO frame-graph / Phase 6 closure work touched (`crates/gfx/**` byte-identical); NO kernel cavity work; NO `#[allow]` reason= sweep / `csgrs` work / clippy-pedantic sweep work (those are separate audit-debt items). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-cad-projection` clean (2.76s, 0 errors / 0 new warnings); `cargo test -p rge-cad-projection` ALL PASS (lib unit + 8 integration suites including `face_selection_smoke` / `revolve_brep_face_id_lookup_smoke` / `multi_canary_integration` / `cross_substrate_determinism` / `projection_modules`); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2418 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2418; +0 net — exact target; no test changes expected); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical). Files modified (1 source + 3 docs): `crates/cad-projection/Cargo.toml` (2-line delta — both `path = "../../kernel/..."` switched to `workspace = true`); `Status.md` (line 184 strike-through + DONE marker); `HANDOFF.md` (line 1583 strike-through + DONE marker); `change.md` (this entry). **Audit-debt list status after this dispatch**: from the 5 carryover items at `HANDOFF.md:1565`, 3 of 5 are now CLOSED (csgrs catch_unwind, clippy pedantic physics+audio, cad-projection dep-style sweep); 2 remain (physics local AuditLedger stub migration W11 — option-(b) rename decision pending; `#[allow(...)]` reason= convention sweep — pure mechanical, ~86 sites).

2026-05-12 (Phase 7 D-Fillet sub-ε.α: graph-level Fillet face-identity inheritance, IMPLEMENTED): lifts the first FilletOp output-identity NON-GOAL via a graph-layer change only — no `impl BRepProvider for FilletOp`, no new face-id `KIND_FILLET` bytestring, no FilletOp source change, no FilletOp `evaluate` change, no edge-resolver change. **Substrate delta**: NEW `OperatorNode::Fillet(_)` arm in `crates/cad-core/src/topology/resolve.rs` `resolve_step` (~12L incl. inline rationale comment) — mirror of the pre-existing Transform arm — recurses to the unique input via the existing `single_input_node` helper and returns upstream face IDs unchanged. **Load-bearing structural observation**: `FilletOp::evaluate` clones upstream positions/indices verbatim and APPENDS chamfer-cap geometry — every upstream face exists bit-identical in the output mesh, so face-identity pass-through is unambiguously correct without any per-operator `BRepProvider` impl. Chamfer-cap triangles remain unnamed in v0; future direct `BRepProvider for FilletOp` with cap-face-tag enum is sub-ε.γ scope. **Edge inheritance intentionally NOT extended**: filleted edges lose 2-endpoint geometry under chamfering — pass-through would silently misrepresent topology. Edge resolver (`crates/cad-core/src/topology/edge_resolve.rs`) keeps Fillet in its catch-all returning `BRepResolveError::TopologyChangingOperator { kind: OpKind::Fillet }`; sub-ε.β is the bounded next slice. **Tests (+3 net inline in `resolve.rs::tests`)**: `resolver_cuboid_then_fillet_inherits_cuboid_face_ids` (Cuboid→Fillet chain yields face IDs byte-equal to direct `Cuboid::brep_face_ids`); `resolver_fillet_face_ids_stable_under_radius_parameter_change` (radii 0.1/0.2/0.5 produce identical face IDs — rebuild-stability claim); `resolver_cuboid_transform_fillet_chains_through_both` (composition with Transform arm). **Existing canary rewritten in lockstep** (amended MAY-list +1 file with explicit user green-light mid-dispatch): `crates/cad-core/tests/fillet_smoke.rs::fillet_node_returns_topology_changing_from_both_resolvers` renamed → `fillet_node_face_inherits_edge_returns_topology_changing` — face-resolver assertion replaced with inherited-IDs comparison against direct Cuboid face IDs; edge-resolver assertion preserved byte-identical (still `TopologyChangingOperator { kind: OpKind::Fillet }`); local docstring softened. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `impl BRepProvider for FilletOp`; NO new `BRepFaceId::KIND_FILLET`; NO new face-tag enum; NO `edge_resolve.rs` change; NO `topology/provider.rs` change; NO `topology/face_id/**` change; NO `fillet/{cuboid,extrude,revolve,loft}.rs` touch; NO FilletOp source/struct/`from_upstream`/`evaluate` change; NO new integration test file; NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock`; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**`; NO `unsafe`; NO new ADR / new lint / new exemption / new doctrine / new §18 companion. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-cad-core` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-cad-core` clean (1.33s); `cargo test -p rge-cad-core --no-fail-fast` ALL PASS; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2421 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2418; +3 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical exemption count). Files modified (3 source + 3 docs): `crates/cad-core/src/topology/resolve.rs` (new Fillet arm + module-doc D-Fillet sub-ε.α posture block + `resolve_step` doc + `pub fn` doc + 3 new tests + import additions `FilletOp` + `BRepEdgeProvider`); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/fillet/mod.rs` (NON-GOAL bullets L32-33 softened + closing posture paragraph L66-72 rewritten; FilletOp struct + impls + Operator-trait impl BYTE-IDENTICAL); `crates/cad-core/tests/fillet_smoke.rs` (in-place test rewrite per amended MAY-list; same test count; +`BRepProvider` + `BRepFaceId` imports); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **D-Fillet output-identity chapter status**: sub-ε.α LANDED — face identity flows for Cuboid→Fillet / Cuboid→Transform→Fillet chains, edge identity remains explicit-error for Fillet. 2026-05-12 (Phase 7 D-Fillet sub-ε.β: graph-level Fillet edge-identity filtered inheritance, IMPLEMENTED): lifts the second FilletOp output-identity NON-GOAL (edge-side bullet at `crates/cad-core/src/operators/fillet/mod.rs:41`) via a graph-layer change only — no `impl BRepEdgeProvider for FilletOp`, no new `BRepEdgeId` derivation, no reshape of `BRepEdgeProvider`, no FilletOp source change, no FilletOp `evaluate` change, no face-resolver change. **Inspection-first verdict (pre-dispatch)**: partition `upstream_edges \ filleted_edges` is cleanly expressible from existing substrate alone — `FilletOp::edges()` already `pub fn` returning `&[BRepEdgeId]`; set-difference is one `.filter(|e| !op.edges().contains(e))` call; FilletOp construction-time invariants (`EmptyEdgeSelection`, `EdgeNotInUpstream`, `UnsupportedEdgeGeometry`) make filter total. No tag invention, no provider-contract reshape. **Substrate delta**: NEW `OperatorNode::Fillet(op)` arm in `crates/cad-core/src/topology/edge_resolve.rs::resolve_step` (~16L incl. inline rationale) — recurses to unique upstream via existing `single_input_node` + `resolve_recursive`, filters out `op.edges()`, returns filtered Vec. **Adjacent-edge analysis (load-bearing for the "untouched" claim)**: chamfer-cap geometry adds new vertices/triangles incident at filleted edges' corner vertices but does NOT modify corner vertices or adjacent-edge geometry. Since `BRepEdgeId::for_face_pair` derives identity from face IDs only and faces inherit unchanged through Fillet (sub-ε.α), adjacent edges' byte-identity is preserved — they belong to the inherited set. **Owner-discipline invariant documented inline**: if `caller_owner ≠ op.owner()`, filter trivially passes (matches existing resolver discipline). **Filter complexity `O(n*m)`**: fine for v0; future `HashSet<BRepEdgeId>` upgrade flagged in module-doc. **Tests (+5 net inline in `edge_resolve.rs::tests`)**: `resolver_cuboid_then_fillet_filters_selected_edges_inherits_rest` (single-edge → 11 inherited byte-equal); `resolver_cuboid_then_fillet_multi_edge_selection_filters_all_selected` (3 selected → 9 inherited); `resolver_cuboid_then_fillet_all_edges_selected_returns_empty` (boundary: all 12 → empty Vec); `resolver_fillet_edge_set_stable_under_radius_parameter_change` (radii 0.1/0.2/0.5 — rebuild-stability); `resolver_cuboid_transform_fillet_chains_edges_through_both` (sub-7.2-ζ.ε composition). **Canary rewritten in lockstep** (proactively in MAY-list — sub-ε.α lesson applied): `crates/cad-core/tests/fillet_smoke.rs::fillet_node_face_inherits_edge_returns_topology_changing` renamed → `fillet_node_face_and_edge_both_inherit_with_edge_filtering`; edge-resolver assertion replaced with filtered-Vec comparison; face-resolver assertion preserved byte-identical; docstring rewritten; unused imports `BRepResolveError` + `OpKind` removed. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `impl BRepEdgeProvider for FilletOp`; NO new `BRepEdgeId` derivation; NO new `BRepFaceId::KIND_FILLET`; NO reshape of `BRepEdgeProvider` trait; NO `FilletUpstream` trait change; NO `topology/resolve.rs` change; NO `topology/{edge_id,face_id/**,provider}.rs` change; NO `fillet/{cuboid,extrude,revolve,loft}.rs` touch; NO FilletOp source/struct/`from_upstream`/`evaluate`/`edges()`/`radius()`/`owner()` change; NO new integration test file; NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock`; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**`; NO `unsafe`; NO new ADR/lint/exemption/doctrine/§18 companion; NO `HashSet<BRepEdgeId>` optimization (premature for v0). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-cad-core` exit 0 (after mid-dispatch fmt apply for `let` wrap style); `cargo build -p rge-cad-core` clean (1.48s); `cargo test -p rge-cad-core --no-fail-fast` 479 ALL PASS; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2426 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2421; +5 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical exemption count).
