2026-05-12 (Phase 6 pass-record-integration sub-α: LitMeshPipeline + record_lit_mesh_pass depth-readiness, IMPLEMENTED): opens the Phase 6 pass-record-integration chapter (Path B) — `record_lit_mesh_pass` consuming transient resources from the frame-graph substrate (dispatches 119-123) — with sub-α as the bounded immediate dispatch: API-shaping without production change. **NO production wire**; no transient texture allocation; no Gate A marker change. **Inspection-first verdict**: `record_lit_mesh_pass` is TEST-ONLY today (production `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:244-294` inlines a byte-identical 50-LoC render-pass body); no existing transient consumer (all GPU resources currently owned); natural first transient consumer is a depth buffer (Z-fight bug documented at `render_path.rs:281-285`); adding depth-testing trips Gate A re-validation per HANDOFF.md dispatch 123. **API shape (additive — `LitMeshPipeline::new` / `new_cached` byte-identical signatures)**: NEW `new_with_depth(.., depth_state: Option<DepthStateKey>) -> Result<Self, _>`; NEW `new_cached_with_depth(.., depth_state: Option<DepthStateKey>, cache) -> Result<Self, _>`; existing `new` / `new_cached` bodies delegate to `_with_depth(None)` — byte-identical preservation. `build_pipeline` gains `depth_state: Option<DepthStateKey>` param; populates `wgpu::DepthStencilState { format, depth_write_enabled: Some(d.depth_write_enabled), depth_compare: Some(d.depth_compare), stencil: default, bias: default }` when `Some`; `depth_stencil: None` when `None` (byte-identical pre-sub-α). **`record_lit_mesh_pass` signature**: final `depth_view: Option<&wgpu::TextureView>` param added; `depth_stencil_attachment` populated with `LoadOp::Clear(1.0) + StoreOp::Store + stencil_ops: None` when `Some`. Pipeline-vs-attachment matching is caller's responsibility (documented). **Tests (+3 net, all GPU-gated)**: `lit_mesh_pipeline_new_with_depth_none_matches_new_via_pso_cache` (`Arc::ptr_eq` proves delegation invariant); `lit_mesh_pipeline_new_with_depth_some_produces_distinct_pso_cache_entry` (silent-pipeline-collision boundary pinned at LitMeshPipeline level); `record_lit_mesh_pass_with_depth_attachment_succeeds` (full API path: `new_with_depth(.., Some(Depth24Plus/Less))` + Depth24Plus texture + `record_lit_mesh_pass(.., Some(&depth_view))` + `device.poll` — proves end-to-end without wgpu validation panic). API-shaped proofs only per user direction; NO visual assertion (Z-fight claim belongs to sub-β). **Test call site updates (4 byte-identical `None` additions)**: `lit_mesh_pipeline.rs::tests` (2 sites); `crates/gfx/tests/gate_a_simple_scene_60fps.rs` (1 site — Gate A pre-allocator triangle path certified preserved with `depth_view=None`); `crates/gfx/tests/render_mesh_smoke.rs` (1 site). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs` change (sub-β scope); NO transient depth texture allocation via `TexturePool::acquire` (sub-β); NO Gate A marker update at `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md:468` / `BASELINE.md` (sub-γ); NO `LitMeshPipeline::new` / `new_cached` signature change (additive new methods); NO `MeshPipeline` / `TrianglePipeline` change; NO `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/**` touch; NO `crates/gfx/src/pso_cache.rs` change (`PsoKey` 4th slot already shaped; `DepthStateKey` already publicly re-exported); NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock`; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**`; NO `unsafe`; NO new ADR/lint/exemption/doctrine/§18 companion; NO `render_path.rs:141` callsite churn (`new(..)` signature byte-identical). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-gfx` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-gfx` clean (1.06s); `cargo test -p rge-gfx --lib lit_mesh_pipeline` 16/16 PASS (3 new + 13 existing byte-identical); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2429 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2426; +3 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical exemption count). Files modified (3 source + 3 docs): `crates/gfx/src/lit_mesh_pipeline.rs` (NEW `new_with_depth` + `new_cached_with_depth` + `DepthStateKey` import + `build_pipeline` extended + `record_lit_mesh_pass` signature + 3 GPU-gated tests + 2 inline-test `None` additions + doc updates); `crates/gfx/tests/gate_a_simple_scene_60fps.rs` (L176 `None`); `crates/gfx/tests/render_mesh_smoke.rs` (L174 `None`); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **Phase 6 pass-record-integration chapter status (Path B)**: sub-α LANDED (consumer surface depth-ready); sub-β scope (production wire + Z-fight fix) and sub-γ scope (Gate A re-measurement) remain open with NO COMMITMENT — sub-α stands alone if priorities shift after α.

2026-05-12 (Phase 6 pass-record-integration sub-β: production depth wire, IMPLEMENTED): production `editor-shell::render_path` consumes the frame-graph transient-texture substrate end-to-end. **Honest scope** (per user direction): this dispatch consumes the transient substrate and prevents a depth-state regression that adding a populated depth buffer would otherwise introduce, but does NOT prove a user-visible Z-fight fix — that claim requires sub-γ measurement or a visual harness, neither of which exists yet. **Pre-dispatch inspection-first verdict**: substrate-disciplined path (`FrameGraph` + `ResourceMap`) is workable but heavy for one transient resource (3 new state fields on `EditorShell` + per-frame builder-layer ceremony); direct `TexturePool::acquire` would be cleaner but explicitly forbidden by dispatch-122 substrate-discipline rule ("pass-record sites must NOT call pool.acquire directly"). Per user guardrail, heavy path chosen — bypassing creates a second resource lifetime path. **Substrate consumption flow (per dispatch-122 recipe)**: `init_render_state` constructs `TexturePool::new()` + `BufferPool::new()` (unused but required by `build_resource_map` signature) + single-pass `FrameGraph` (one write of `DEPTH_RESOURCE_ID` at `Depth24Plus`) compiled to `CompiledFrameGraph`; `render_frame` opens with `tex_pool.begin_frame() + buf_pool.begin_frame()` (ADR-118 D4) + `build_resource_map(compiled, device, &mut tex_pool, &mut buf_pool)` + `Arc::clone(map.texture_map[&DEPTH_RESOURCE_ID])` + view + inline `depth_stencil_attachment` literal (NO refactor of inline render pass into `record_lit_mesh_pass(...)` per user direction); `resize_render_path` rebuilds `compiled_frame_graph` against new dims because `TextureDescriptor` is keyed on width/height. **Depth-state choice** (`LessEqual` + `depth_write_enabled: false`) per user direction: cuboid + overlay share single pipeline; `depth_write_enabled: false` means depth buffer stays at `Clear(1.0)` for entire frame; every fragment passes `LessEqual` against 1.0; render order determines visibility — pre-sub-β no-depth visual behavior preserved exactly. **Borrow-flow design**: pool work in scoped block FIRST (`&mut self.{texture_pool,buffer_pool}` + immutable borrow of `self.gfx_ctx` via `gfx_ctx.device()`; struct-field-level borrow splitting) before re-acquiring existing immutable borrows; no `RefCell`/`Mutex`. `build_resource_map` failure → warn + `window.request_redraw()` + return true (recoverable). **Acceptable bounded leak on resize**: 2 stale textures per resize event drain to free-lists (`FRAMES_IN_FLIGHT=2`); pool-level pruning out of scope. **New EditorShell state fields (3 `Option<T>`)**: `texture_pool` / `buffer_pool` / `compiled_frame_graph`; `None` initializers in both `new` and `with_world`. **New constants in render_path.rs**: `DEPTH_FORMAT = Depth24Plus`; `DEPTH_RESOURCE_ID` (ASCII-prefix bytes); `build_lit_mesh_compiled_frame_graph(w, h)` helper; `lit_mesh_depth_state()` helper. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `crates/gfx/src/**` change (sub-α API consumed unchanged); NO `LitMeshPipeline` / `record_lit_mesh_pass` signature change; NO `pso_cache.rs` / `frame_graph/**` change; NO Gate A marker update at `IMPLEMENTATION.md:468` / `BASELINE.md` (sub-γ); NO new ADR/lint/exemption/doctrine/§18 companion; NO pipeline splitting; NO inline-render-pass refactor into `record_lit_mesh_pass(...)` call (kept inline per user direction); NO direct `TexturePool::acquire` bypassing builder; NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock`; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**`; NO `unsafe`; NO new integration test (`render_input_boundary` Gate C invariant exercises substrate, passes unchanged). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-editor-shell` exit 0 (after mid-dispatch fmt apply for `let-expect` wrap); `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell` clean (4.43s); `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --no-fail-fast` 108/108 PASS (67 lib + 41 integration; byte-identical to pre-sub-β; Gate C invariant confirmed); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2429 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2429 post-sub-α; +0 net — wire only); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical exemption count). Files modified (2 source + 3 docs): `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle.rs` (3 new `pub(crate)` `Option<T>` fields + `None` initializers in both constructors); `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs` (new `rge_gfx` imports + new `DEPTH_FORMAT`/`DEPTH_RESOURCE_ID` consts + `build_lit_mesh_compiled_frame_graph`/`lit_mesh_depth_state` helpers + `init_render_state` step-5 pipeline upgraded + step-5b pool construction + step-7 stash extended + `render_frame` pool work scoped block + inline `depth_stencil_attachment` literal + highlight-overlay future-note comment rewrite); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **Phase 6 pass-record-integration chapter status (Path B)**: sub-α + sub-β both LANDED — consumer-API shape AND substrate consumption end-to-end; sub-γ scope (Gate A re-measurement + `BASELINE.md` update + `IMPLEMENTATION.md:468` marker softened) remains OPEN with no commitment. Visual harness for Z-fight-fix claim (separate scope) also OPEN.

(c) `Status.md` snapshot prepend; (d) `change.md` this entry; (e) `HANDOFF.md` entry. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO new perf number for post-depth path; NO new harness file; NO existing Gate A marker invalidation (pre-depth claim stays valid for the gfx-level pre-depth harness it certifies); NO architecture change anywhere; NO `crates/**/src/**` change; NO `crates/**/tests/**` change; NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` change; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change; NO `unsafe`; NO new ADR/lint/exemption/doctrine/§18 companion; 2026-05-12 (Phase 3.3-3.4 WASM bench gates re-validation, docs-only IMPLEMENTED): re-runs both formal WASM bench gates on current main HEAD and records the result + drift in `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md`. **No code change, no soak run, no Phase 3.4 exit-criterion #3 closure.** Per user direction "Option A: docs-only" + pre-dispatch inspection-first verdict that the substrate is intact, sub-3.3/3.4 re-validation captures the fresh measurement without spinning an hour-long job or losing the data. **Inspection-first re-runs (release profile, single-run point estimates, current main HEAD)**: (a) Phase 3.3 hot-reload gate: `entities=1000 cycles=100 p95_ms=0.818 max_ms=0.982 avg_ms=0.793` — PASS by a wide margin (0.8% of budget); +2.8% drift vs prior 0.796 ms (within ±5% noise band — no flag); (b) Phase 3.4 ECS-via-WASM ratio: `entities=1000 frames=10 native_per_frame_us=67.93 wasm_per_frame_us=90.82 ratio=1.34×` — PASS (89% of budget); +10.7% drift vs prior 1.21× (outside ±5% band but in-gate; halt-on-regression protocol applies — "if numbers are WORSE than previous recording but still WITHIN gate, proceed but flag the delta"). Mechanical drift driver: native_per_frame improved ~16% (81 → 67.93 µs) while wasm_per_frame improved only ~7% (98 → 90.82 µs) — host got faster at native more than at wasm, expanding relative WASM penalty. Both prior + current are single-run point estimates; per-run noise contributes. Bulk-path substrate UNCHANGED; NO architecture regression. **Phase 3.4 exit-criteria status (post-re-validation)**: #1 hot-reload p95 < 100ms → WIRED + PASS today; #2 ECS-via-WASM ≤ 1.5× → WIRED + PASS today with drift flagged; #3 1-hour session without memory leak → HARNESS WIRED, UNRUN as of 2026-05-12; release-readiness/CI deferral PRESERVED per HANDOFF.md 2026-05-11 dispatch flag; #4 component data preserved across 100 cycles → ASSERTED as part of gate #1 (passes today). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO code change anywhere (`crates/script-bench/src/**` / `crates/script-host/src/**` / `crates/runtime-wasmtime/src/**` / `crates/runtime-wasmtime-engine/src/**` untouched); NO test file change; NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` change; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change; NO 1-hour soak run (explicitly deferred per user direction "no soak run, no exit-criterion #3 closure"); NO Phase 3.4 exit-criterion #3 closure; NO W04 cross-engine table population ("_pending W04_" rows stay deferred — W04 wave out of Phase 3.4 scope per `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md:644`); NO `unsafe`; NO new ADR/lint/exemption/doctrine/§18 companion. **Verification gates**: NO `cargo fmt` / `cargo build` / `cargo test --workspace` / architecture-lint runs required for the docs-only record itself (the test re-runs that produced the measurement data are the verification — both PASS, exit 0). Workspace state unchanged from sub-γ commit `b7e4909` — 2429 / 0 / 19; 9 enforcement + 1 supplementary lints PASS byte-identical. Files modified (3 docs, 0 source): `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md` (Re-validation 2026-05-12 row tables appended to both Phase 3.3 + Phase 3.4 sections + 1-hour soak status footnote on the Phase 3.3 closing paragraph); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **Phase 3.3/3.4 chapter status**: substrate intact + 2-of-4 exit criteria certified-and-re-verified-2026-05-12 + 1-of-4 deferred per release-readiness sequencing + 1-of-4 part-of-gate-and-passing. Re-validation answers "are the WASM gates still healthy?" without pretending the long soak was done.

2026-05-12 (Phase 3.4 exit criterion #3 — 1-hour memory soak gate CLOSED on recorder host, IMPLEMENTED): runs the deferred 1-hour memory-soak gate end-to-end as a standalone release-readiness dispatch. Background-dispatched `cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib script_host::tests::phase_3_memory_soak_one_hour -- --ignored --nocapture` completed with **exit code 0** after exactly **3600.00 s wall-clock**; test result `ok. 1 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured`. **Phase 3.4 exit criterion #3 status**: deferred → **CLOSED 2026-05-12 on recorder host only**. Per user direction "1-hour memory soak run, but as a standalone release-readiness dispatch, not as a hidden follow-on. It is harness-wired, it closes the only explicitly deferred Phase 3.4 exit criterion, and it does not reopen architecture." **Three assertions HELD**: `report.elapsed >= 1 hour` ✓; `report.cycles > 0` ✓; `report.restored_components == report.cycles * report.entity_count` ✓ (load-bearing preservation invariant — test poisons all Counter components between capture and restore on every cycle). **Estimated cycle count**: ~4.4M cycles at re-validated 0.818 ms/cycle Phase 3.3 p95; exact count NOT printed (harness limitation — `MemorySoakReport` fields not surfaced to stdout despite `--nocapture`; future-improvement scope). **No panic, no OOM, no hang** in 1 hour of continuous hot-reload cycles. **Scope limitation (LOAD-BEARING)**: CONSTRAINED-CERTIFIED on recorder host only (Windows 11 / x86_64, cargo 1.94.1, wasmtime 44.0.1, single-run). Certifies: 1-hour stability + 1000-entity preservation across ~4.4M cycles + wasmtime + script-host substrate stable under sustained swap load. Does NOT certify: explicit memory-growth metrics (no `peak_rss` / `vss_delta` — implicit "no memory leak" only); allocator fragmentation; VRAM; sustained-thermal beyond 1 hour; vendor / OS parity; per-cycle latency variance over full hour. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO code change (`crates/script-bench/src/**` / `crates/script-host/src/**` / `crates/runtime-wasmtime/src/**` / `crates/runtime-wasmtime-engine/src/**` byte-identical to commit `585bcb2`; `phase_3_memory_soak_one_hour` test body byte-identical, `#[ignore]` preserved); NO test file change; NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` change; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change; NO harness-improvement; NO W04 cross-engine table population (deferred per user); NO architecture change; NO `unsafe`; NO new ADR/lint/exemption/doctrine/§18; NO `cargo fmt`/`cargo build`/`cargo test --workspace` runs required (the 3600.00 s test run IS the verification). Workspace state unchanged from re-validation commit `585bcb2`: 2429 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored; 9 enforcement + 1 supplementary lints PASS byte-identical. **Phase 3.4 exit-criteria scoreboard (post-soak)**: #1 hot-reload p95 < 100ms → CLOSED-AND-RE-VALIDATED (0.818 ms); #2 ECS-via-WASM ≤ 1.5× → CLOSED-AND-RE-VALIDATED (1.34× drift flagged); #3 1-hour session without memory leak → **CLOSED TODAY** (3600.00 s soak, assertions held); #4 component data preserved across 100 cycles → CLOSED-AND-RE-ASSERTED (held in 100-cycle gate + re-validation + ~4.4M-cycle soak). Files modified (3 docs, 0 source): `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md` (NEW "Formal 1-hour memory soak (Phase 3.4 exit criterion #3) — RUN 2026-05-12" section appended; reverts the prior 2026-05-12 re-validation footnote that flagged the soak as "harness-wired but UNRUN" since today's run makes it stale); `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md:314-319` (Phase 3.4 exit-criteria block gains CLOSED markers on all 4 bullets); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **Phase 3.4 chapter status — ALL EXIT CRITERIA CLOSED ON RECORDER HOST**: substrate intact + all 4 exit criteria certified on Windows 11 / x86_64, cargo 1.94.1, wasmtime 44.0.1. Universal / vendor-parity / CI-regression-coverage / cross-engine-comparison certifications remain OUT OF Phase 3.4 scope (W04 wave + release-readiness CI dispatches).

NO 1-hour soak re-run per user direction. Workspace state unchanged from `06eb23a`: 2429 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored; 9+1 lints PASS byte-identical. 2026-05-12 (Phase 3.4 W04 cross-engine bench comparison sub-α: raw WasmtimeCranelift column closed, IMPLEMENTED): opens the W04 cross-engine chapter with the WasmtimeCranelift column completely filled — all four `_pending W04_` cells (`script_tick_1m_iters` / `per_frame_tick_10k_entities` / `cold_start` / `memory_overhead`) flipped to measured numbers via NEW `crates/script-bench/src/wasmtime_cranelift.rs` fixture (direct wasmtime API, NO `runtime-wasmtime-engine` / `runtime-wasmtime` orchestration, NO `rge-script-host` overhead). **Per user direction**: "Choose sub-α(c): all WasmtimeCranelift rows. If we're opening W04, make the first slice a real column close. ... direct raw Cranelift only, four workloads, four measured rows, BASELINE.md updates, and log entries. Keep `script_host_counter` clearly separate from raw `WasmtimeCranelift`; no MLua/Wasmer/Bevy-extism deps, no Singlepass config yet, no docs-only substitute." Executed exactly that scope. **Measurement results (release, recorder host)**: `script_tick_1m_iters` = 713 200 ns / **0.713 ns/op (1.057× native — PASSES 1.5× target)**; `per_frame_tick_10k_entities` = 76 200 ns / **7.620 ns/entity (10.034× native — FAILS 1.5× as raw per-entity; bulk-path `script_host_counter_bulk` at 1.34× achieves target via single-host-crossing protocol; different workload shapes)**; `cold_start` = 405 100 ns / **0.405 ms (PASS < 50 ms — 0.8% of budget)**; `memory_overhead` = **13 680 bytes/module (`Module::serialize().len()` AOT-artifact proxy; PASS < 1 MB — 1.3% of budget; runtime RSS NOT measured)**. **Critical disambiguation preserved**: `wasmtime_cranelift` (raw) column measures cranelift WITHOUT script-host orchestration; existing `script_host_counter` rows measure cranelift WITH overhead. Different fixtures, different overheads; both real measurements of wasmtime cranelift JIT. **Honest finding (10× ratio for per_frame_tick_10k)**: NOT a regression — structural characteristic of raw per-entity WASM execution against memory-bound workloads (60k bounded loads/stores per frame; wasmtime bounds checks not fully optimized away; native slice iteration is bounds-check-elision + vectorization friendly). PLAN §5.6 1.5× target IS achievable for script-host workload pattern (bulk-path) but structurally violated by raw per-entity-wasm-loop pattern. NO engine reshape / PLAN §5.6 retarget / native-baseline rewrite proposed — gap recorded for transparency; downstream architectural decisions out of scope. **NEW file**: `crates/script-bench/src/wasmtime_cranelift.rs` (340L: 3 inline WAT fixtures + `WasmtimeCraneliftBench` struct with `wasmtime::Engine` configured via `cranelift_opt_level(OptLevel::Speed)` mirroring `runtime-wasmtime-engine::Engine::new` + 4 measurement methods + 1 helper fn + 4 measurement tests with phase_3_3 / phase_3_4 sibling-site println format). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `crates/script-bench/Cargo.toml` change (wasmtime + wat already direct deps); NO `crates/runtime-wasmtime-engine/**` change; NO `crates/runtime-wasmtime/**` change; NO `crates/script-host/**` change (script_host_counter preserved verbatim); NO MLua/Wasmer/BevyExtism deps; NO WasmtimeSinglepass / Winch config (sub-β scope); NO PLAN §5.6 1.5× target retarget; NO native-baseline rewrite; NO `engine_stub.rs` deletion; NO architecture change; NO new ADR/lint/exemption/doctrine/§18 companion; NO `unsafe`; NO `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change (Phase 3.4 exit criteria all already CLOSED in `06eb23a`; W04 sub-α is benchmark-breadth work, NOT a Phase 3.4 closure event). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-script-bench` exit 0 (after mid-dispatch fmt apply for 3 `let` wrap-style sites); `cargo build -p rge-script-bench --release` clean (7.49 s); `cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib wasmtime_cranelift::tests` 4/4 PASS in 0.01 s with all 4 println rows surfacing measured numbers; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2433 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2429; +4 net — exact target = 4 new measurement tests); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical exemption count). Files modified (1 source NEW + 1 source EDIT + 3 docs + 1 BASELINE): NEW `crates/script-bench/src/wasmtime_cranelift.rs` (340L); `crates/script-bench/src/lib.rs` (+1 `pub mod` + 1 scope-doc bullet); `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md` (NEW Formal W04 raw WasmtimeCranelift gates section appended + Remaining engine rows table updated with 4 measured cells for wasmtime_cranelift column + opening framing clarifying distinct measurement paths); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **W04 cross-engine chapter status**: WasmtimeCranelift column COMPLETELY CLOSED; WasmtimeSinglepass (Winch) OPEN (sub-β — same WAT fixtures will reuse at ~150-200 LoC marginal cost); MLua / WasmerSinglepass / BevyExtism columns OPEN as `_post-Phase-3_` (each new crate dep + adapter, ~300-500 LoC each). **NEXT**: TBD by user.

2026-05-12 (Phase 3.4 W04 cross-engine bench comparison sub-β: raw WasmtimeSinglepass / Winch column closed, IMPLEMENTED): closes the second W04 column with measured numbers. All four `_pending W04 sub-β_` cells (`script_tick_1m_iters` / `per_frame_tick_10k_entities` / `cold_start` / `memory_overhead`) flipped to measured numbers via NEW `crates/script-bench/src/wasmtime_singlepass.rs` (~330L mirror of sub-α with one config-strategy swap: `Config::strategy(Strategy::Winch)`). **Per user direction** four measured rows, minimal engine-config path, halt-if-broader-Engine-reshape, sharp separation of script_host_counter and raw wasmtime columns. **Config plumbing**: `winch` feature flag added to `crates/script-bench/Cargo.toml` ONLY (script-bench-local override; Cargo feature unification compiles wasmtime once with `["cranelift", "runtime", "std", "winch"]`); runtime crates STAY on default-Cranelift-only — NO workspace-root Cargo.toml change, NO broader Engine reshape. **Three sub-α WAT consts promoted from `const` → `pub(crate) const`** so sub-β re-imports — single-source WAT, no duplication. **Measurement results (release-mode, recorder host)**: script_tick_1m_iters Winch = 2.547 ns/op (**3.77× native; FAILS 1.5×**; 3.57× over Cranelift); per_frame_tick_10k_entities Winch = 9.750 ns/entity (**12.83× native; FAILS 1.5×**; **only 1.28× over Cranelift** — the central finding); cold_start Winch = 0.305 ms (PASS < 50 ms; **0.75× of Cranelift — Winch FASTER at compile**); memory_overhead Winch = 13 680 B (**1.000× of Cranelift** — empty-module artifact compiler-independent). **Central question answered**: per user direction "is the raw per-entity overhead a Cranelift-specific shape or a broader direct-WASM execution cost?" → **per-entity overhead is BROADLY characteristic of direct-WASM execution, NOT Cranelift-specific**. per_frame_tick_10k ratios: Cranelift 10.034× / Winch 12.829× over native; Winch/Cranelift = 1.28× — both compilers in same order-of-magnitude penalty band. If 10× were Cranelift-specific, Winch (less-optimizing) would show MUCH larger gap. Tight cross-compiler ratio → penalty is driven by **WASM execution model itself** (bounds-checked linear-memory access, per-element instruction overhead, no auto-vectorization). **PLAN §5.6 1.5× target structurally unachievable for any direct-WASM per-entity-loop workload regardless of compiler**; `script_host_counter_bulk` (1.34×) is the architectural answer. "Uncomfortable data" per user framing — recorded honestly. **Cranelift-vs-Winch cross-compiler analysis recorded in BASELINE.md**: Cranelift better for compute-bound + memory-access-heavy hot paths; Winch better for hot-reload / fast-iteration paths. Script-host hot-reload uses Cranelift correctly. **No engine-default change proposed**. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO workspace-root Cargo.toml change; NO crates/runtime-wasmtime-engine/** / runtime-wasmtime/** / script-host/** change (script_host_counter rows preserved verbatim); NO MLua / Wasmer / BevyExtism deps; NO PLAN §5.6 retarget; NO native-baseline rewrite; NO engine_stub.rs deletion; NO Cranelift-side change beyond 3-keyword pub(crate) WAT promotion (sub-α tests + numbers + struct + impl byte-identical); NO architecture change; NO new ADR/lint/exemption/doctrine/§18 companion; NO unsafe; NO plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md change (Phase 3.4 exit criteria already CLOSED in 06eb23a; W04 is benchmark-breadth); NO #[ignore]-marked tests; NO raw Winch hot-reload measurement (sub-β scope was 4 raw fixtures only). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-script-bench` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-script-bench --release` clean (1m 27s — pulled in wasmtime-internal-winch v44.0.1 + winch-codegen v44.0.1 + rebuild of wasmtime + cranelift-codegen; one-time cost); `cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib wasmtime_singlepass::tests` 4/4 PASS in 0.01s; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2437 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2433; +4 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical exemption count). Files modified (1 source NEW + 2 source EDIT + 1 Cargo + 4 docs): NEW `crates/script-bench/src/wasmtime_singlepass.rs` (~330L); `crates/script-bench/src/wasmtime_cranelift.rs` (3-keyword `const → pub(crate) const` promotion; byte-identical elsewhere); `crates/script-bench/src/lib.rs` (+1 `pub mod` + 1 scope-doc bullet); `crates/script-bench/Cargo.toml` (wasmtime dep gains `features = ["winch"]` + rationale comment); `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md` (NEW Formal W04 Winch section + Central question answer + cross-compiler analysis + scope limitation + table update); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **W04 cross-engine chapter status**: WasmtimeCranelift CLOSED (sub-α `4385c80`); WasmtimeSinglepass/Winch CLOSED (sub-β THIS); central question ANSWERED. **NEXT**: TBD by user (W04 sub-γ MLua / sub-δ WasmerSinglepass / sub-ε BevyExtism each its own dispatch with new deps; D-Fillet geometry depth; other).

2026-05-12 (Phase 6 sub-β visual harness — gfx-level Z-fight-prevention claim PARTIALLY GRADUATED, IMPLEMENTED): pixel-level visual verification at the GFX pipeline + render-pass layer that the post-sub-α `LitMeshPipeline::new_with_depth(Some(DepthStateKey { Depth24Plus, false, LessEqual }))` configuration + sub-β-style `depth_stencil_attachment` produces the expected visible rendering behavior — cuboid + overlay at shared positions, overlay drawn second wins on shared-position pixels; cuboid color visible elsewhere. **Per user direction** Option A green-light + tight scope: NEW `crates/gfx/tests/lit_mesh_depth_overlay_smoke.rs`; reuse `HeadlessTarget` + `ReadbackBuffer` + `render_mesh_smoke.rs` style; synthetic cuboid + overlay at shared positions, overlay drawn second with distinct color; assert overlay color wins in shared projected region AND cuboid color remains visible outside it; partial claim graduation in docs; NO editor-shell source edits, NO headless-editor architecture, NO winit bypass. **Test design**: hand-built 1×1×1 cuboid `RenderMesh` (mirrored from `render_mesh_smoke.rs::unit_cuboid_render_mesh`); `LitMesh::from_render_mesh` produces 36-vertex flat-shaded mesh; overlay = indices [6, 7, 8] (first +Z-face triangle = lower-right half); camera + light per render_mesh_smoke; pipeline via sub-α `new_with_depth(.., Some(DepthStateKey::new(Depth24Plus, false, LessEqual)))` matching editor-shell production sub-β EXACTLY; depth texture allocated DIRECTLY via wgpu (NOT via TexturePool/FrameGraph — substrate plumbing is tested by `frame_graph_*` tests; this harness verifies pipeline + render-pass visual behavior under the same depth state); single inline render pass (mirrors editor-shell `render_path.rs:244-294` pattern) with two `draw_indexed` calls (cuboid white + overlay orange via `Material::update_color` matching editor-shell HIGHLIGHT_COLOR + HIGHLIGHT_PHONG exactly); 3 pixel assertions: (40,40) inside overlay → r>80 && b<80 (orange-dominated); (24,24) inside cuboid-only triangle → r>80 && b>80 (white-ish); (4,4) outside silhouette → r+g+b<30 (near-black clear color). Assertions HELD on first try; no flakiness. **PARTIAL claim graduation framing** recorded in test module-doc + docs: certifies pipeline + render-pass behavior matches sub-β claim + render order determines visibility on shared-position pixels + no Z-fight at pipeline/render-pass level + regression boundary; does NOT certify editor-shell::render_frame end-to-end (substrate plumbing tested separately; no test exercises BOTH end-to-end without non-winit headless-editor architecture explicitly halt-and-reframed out of scope) + does NOT certify production highlight-overlay visuals using cad-core-derived ProjectedMesh + does NOT certify user-perceived 'looks correct'. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO editor-shell source edits (`crates/editor-shell/src/**` byte-identical to sub-β `999e9ff`); NO non-winit headless-editor architecture (test uses HeadlessTarget+ReadbackBuffer at gfx-tier, NOT winit-bypass at editor-shell-tier per user guardrail); NO winit bypass; NO TexturePool/FrameGraph/build_resource_map substrate usage (depth texture allocated directly via wgpu); NO new BASELINE.md section (test is behavior-regression boundary, not perf measurement); NO `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change (Phase 6 §6.3 Gate A marker stays as-of sub-γ `b7e4909`; this test partially graduates the Z-fight-prevention claim BUT does NOT close the gap sub-γ documented at IMPLEMENTATION.md:468); NO `record_lit_mesh_pass` change (sub-α signature byte-identical); NO `LitMeshPipeline` source change (sub-α API byte-identical); NO `Material` source change; NO architecture change; NO new ADR/lint/exemption/doctrine/§18 companion; NO `unsafe`; NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` change; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change; NO production behavior change. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-gfx` exit 0 (after mid-dispatch fmt apply for 2 wrap styles); `cargo test -p rge-gfx --test lit_mesh_depth_overlay_smoke` 1/1 PASS in 0.63 s; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2438 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2437; +1 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical exemption count). **Two mid-dispatch fixups**: (a) `IndexBuffer::new_u32` doesn't exist; corrected to `IndexBuffer::new(ctx, &[u32])` per buffer.rs:97; (b) unused `overlay_g`+`cuboid_g` renamed to `_overlay_g`+`_cuboid_g` to silence rustc warning. Neither structural/architectural. Files modified (1 source NEW + 3 docs): NEW `crates/gfx/tests/lit_mesh_depth_overlay_smoke.rs` (~370L); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **Phase 6 chapter status**: Z-fight-prevention claim VISUALLY VERIFIED at gfx pipeline / render-pass layer (regression boundary established); editor-shell production end-to-end Z-fight-prevention remains structurally-prevented-but-not-test-verified per user-acknowledged guardrail. **NEXT**: TBD by user.

2026-05-12 (W04 follow-on raw Winch hot-reload measurement, IMPLEMENTED): closes the empirical question raised at sub-β open ("does Winch meaningfully improve swap-window / hot-reload p95 enough to matter for fast-iteration scenarios?"). **Answer: NO — Winch does NOT meaningfully improve hot-reload p95 for the `script_host_counter` orchestrated workload.** Measured 2026-05-12 (release-mode, recorder host): Winch p95 = **0.865 ms** vs Cranelift's re-validated 0.818 ms = **1.057× ratio — within ±6% noise band**. Both pass PLAN §5.6 <100 ms gate by >100× margin (Winch 0.9% / Cranelift 0.8% of budget). **Mechanical explanation** cross-referencing sub-α/β: Winch's compile-path advantage (~25% faster cold-start: 0.305 ms vs 0.405 ms) balances against Winch's runtime-path penalty (3.57× slower script_tick: 2.547 vs 0.713 ns/op) — neither dominates for this workload. **No engine-default change proposed**; production hot-reload stays on Cranelift. **Per user direction**: "Reuse the existing hot-reload benchmark shape if possible. Use the already-added Winch config path; no engine default change. Record measurements only; no PLAN target retarget. Update BASELINE.md / logs if it lands. Halt if hot-reload harness is tightly coupled to script_host_counter orchestration and cannot isolate raw Winch without a broader harness rewrite." Inspection-first verdict: harness IS engine-agnostic; one new ~12 LoC `new_with_strategy` constructor + one sibling test isolates raw Winch cleanly. Green-light at "If Winch p95 comes in ugly, just document it." **NEW `ScriptHostBench::new_with_strategy(strategy: Strategy)` constructor**: builds wasmtime engine via `Config::strategy(strategy)` + `Engine::new(&cfg)`; compiles same 3 Counter WAT fixtures; existing `ScriptHostBench::new()` byte-identical (Engine::default → Cranelift; preserves all phase_3_3 / phase_3_4 / soak / hygiene tests). **NEW test**: `script_host::tests::w04_hot_reload_swap_wasmtime_singlepass` mirrors `formal_100_cycle_preservation_gate_uses_1000_entities` shape; uses `new_with_strategy(Strategy::Winch)` + same `hot_reload_preservation(HotReloadConfig::formal())` workflow (same orchestration; only JIT backend swaps); assertion budget loosened to PLAN §5.6 abort threshold (<500 ms, per IMPLEMENTATION.md:323) rather than 100 ms PASS gate — Winch is measurement-only sibling row; Cranelift carries 100 ms PASS responsibility. **Measurement raw output**: `entities=1000 cycles=100 p95_ms=0.865 max_ms=1.219 avg_ms=0.797`. Comparison: p95 1.057× Cranelift; max 1.241×; avg 1.005× (essentially identical). Preservation invariant `restored_components == cycles * entity_count` HELD under Winch — script-host's capture/restore IS engine-agnostic, validated empirically. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `Engine::default()` behavior change (`new()` byte-identical); NO `rge-script-host/**` change; NO `rge-runtime-wasmtime/**` / `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine/**` change (production stays Cranelift); NO PLAN §5.6 target retarget; NO native-baseline rewrite; NO `engine_stub.rs` deletion; NO MLua / Wasmer / Bevy-extism deps; NO other W04 column population (sub-γ/δ/ε stay _post-Phase-3_); NO long-run Winch soak (separate dispatch if surfaced); NO architecture change; NO new ADR/lint/exemption/doctrine/§18 companion; NO `unsafe`; NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` change (winch feature already enabled at sub-β `12beba6`); NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change; NO `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change (Phase 3.4 exit criteria all already CLOSED). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-script-bench` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-script-bench --release` clean (58.37s); `cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib script_host::tests::w04_hot_reload_swap_wasmtime_singlepass` 1/1 PASS in 0.09s; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2439 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2438; +1 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS, byte-identical exemption count). Files modified (1 source + 1 BASELINE + 3 docs): `crates/script-bench/src/script_host.rs` (+~42 LoC: import update + new_with_strategy constructor + Winch test); `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md` (NEW W04 follow-on Winch hot-reload section + table cell update); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **W04 chapter status**: WasmtimeCranelift CLOSED (sub-α `4385c80`); WasmtimeSinglepass / Winch raw bench CLOSED (sub-β `12beba6`); WasmtimeSinglepass / Winch hot-reload sibling CLOSED (this dispatch); central direct-WASM-per-entity-penalty ANSWERED; fast-iteration-Winch question ANSWERED (Winch does NOT meaningfully improve hot-reload p95). **NEXT**: TBD by user.

2026-05-12 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter — ADR-119 substrate decisions pinned, IMPLEMENTED design-only): authors `docs/adr/ADR-119-real-round-fillet-substrate.md` pinning 8 substrate-architectural decisions for the real round fillet chapter BEFORE any geometry code lands. **Per user direction**: Option A ADR-only; no source code. **ADR number confirmed**: ADR-118 latest → ADR-119 next. **Eight decisions pinned**: D1 NEW operator (`RoundFilletOp`) beside chamfer `FilletOp`, NOT in-place evolution; D2 filleted curved edges INHERIT upstream `BRepEdgeId` (semantic-identity, caller handles preserved); D3 cap-face / corner-patch identity PRESSURE-DRIVEN (nameless for v0; lift path documented as `BRepFaceId::KIND_FILLET_ROUND_CAP` + face-tag enum); D4 original faces RETAIN `BRepFaceId` under face-strip removal (identity = semantic surface, NOT mesh shape — workspace-wide commitment); D5 spec/trait/resolver substrate PARALLEL to chamfer's (new `RoundFilletSpec` + `pub(crate) trait RoundFilletUpstream` + new `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)` resolver arms in BOTH face + edge resolvers); D6 existing chamfer `FilletOp` BYTE-IDENTICAL (preserved with all sub-ε.α/β identity behavior); D7 chapter shape = 6 sub-dispatches (sub-α Cuboid substrate; sub-β Extrude; sub-γ Revolve cap-side; sub-δ Loft; sub-ε multi-edge corner blending cross-cutting; sub-ζ circular-path Revolve via multi-segment spec); D8 multi-edge blending + circular-path Revolve are EXPLICIT LATER SUBS not sub-α (matches chamfer-precedent visible-degeneracy + construction-time rejection framing). **5 alternatives explicitly rejected**: in-place evolution with mode enum; skip ADR + write sub-α directly; defer chapter entirely (PLAN.md commits at :1086 / :300 / :303); cap-face NAMED for v0; filleted-edge NEW ID on rounding. **Consequences enumerated**: what's binding (D1-D8); what stays open (RoundFilletSpec exact field set; tessellation segments; face-strip algorithm; variable-radius / curvature-continuity / lineage deferrals); what this ADR DOES NOT change (chamfer FilletOp byte-identical; PLAN §5.6/§7.1 unchanged; BRepEdgeId/BRepFaceId derivations unchanged; ADR-098 topology lineage unchanged; FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md stays PARKED with supersession trail noted). **Workspace + test-count projection** (subsequent dispatches NOT this ADR): +44 to +68 tests over 6 sub-dispatches. **Doctrine alignment**: ADR aligns with workspace memory pointers (`rge_doctrine_as_substrate_posture.md` — ADR warranted as substrate-defining; `rge_dispatch_pattern.md` — design-pin dispatch matches ADR-118 precedent; `rge_audit_layer_classification.md` — ADR at decision-time, NOT retroactive; `synthesis_seven_axis_decomposition.md` — decisions span mechanization/doctrine/topology/authority axes). **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO source code anywhere; NO sub-α/β/γ/δ/ε/ζ implementation; NO `RoundFilletOp` struct or `OpKind::RoundFillet` variant landed; NO `BRepFaceId::KIND_FILLET_ROUND_CAP` bytestring (D3 pressure-deferred); NO chamfer `FilletOp` change (D6 byte-identical); NO test file change; NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` change; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change; NO new architecture lint enforcing ADR commitments; NO `docs/architecture/FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md` change (stays PARKED; supersession trail in ADR-119 doctrine-refs header); NO `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change; NO `plans/PLAN.md` change; NO §18 companion doc; NO new lint/exemption/doctrine doc beyond ADR-119; NO `unsafe`; NO commitment to sub-α landing date or ordering (D7 flexible-order framing). **Verification gates**: NO `cargo fmt`/`build`/`test`/`lint` runs required for docs-only ADR (pure-design). Workspace state unchanged from raw-Winch-hot-reload `72349a6`: 2439 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored; 9 enforcement + 1 supplementary lints PASS byte-identical exemption count. Files modified (1 docs NEW + 3 logs): NEW `docs/adr/ADR-119-real-round-fillet-substrate.md` (~640L incl. header + Context + 8 numbered Decisions + Consequences + 5 Alternatives + Doctrine alignment); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **D-Fillet real round geometry chapter status**: substrate decisions PINNED; NO implementation commitment. Chapter UNCOMMITTED — user/architecture review decides whether/when to open sub-α. This ADR commits to SUBSTRATE DECISIONS for the chapter IF AND WHEN it opens.

2026-05-12 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter sub-α — Cuboid-only substrate, IMPLEMENTED): first geometry code for ADR-119 lands. **Per user direction at green-light**: "Land sub-α now, prove the ADR shape, defer multi-edge blending and circular-path Revolve to their own subs. Correctness target is single-edge Cuboid plus non-corner-sharing multi-edge cases. Corner-sharing multi-edge is visually imperfect and not a success criterion. Halt if first slice needs per-face full vertex duplication of upstream positions, new cap/corner IDs, or any change to chamfer FilletOp or its resolver arms." Executed exactly that scope; halt-condition triggers never fired. **Decisions D1-D6 proven in code**: **D1** NEW operator `RoundFilletOp` in `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/`, byte-distinct from chamfer's `FilletOp` (distinct types, distinct trait `RoundFilletUpstream`, distinct resolver arms, distinct BLAKE3 prefix `b"round_fillet:"` vs chamfer's `b"fillet:"`); **D2** filleted curved edges INHERIT upstream `BRepEdgeId` — `topology::edge_resolve::resolve_step::OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)` arm recurses to upstream and returns ALL upstream edges INCLUDING the filleted ones unchanged (distinct from chamfer's filter-out behavior; verified via `resolver_cuboid_then_round_fillet_inherits_all_edges_including_filleted` test asserting the filleted edge IS in the surviving set); **D3** cap-face / cylinder triangles nameless via `TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE`; **D4** original faces RETAIN identity under face-strip removal — `topology::resolve::resolve_step::OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)` arm recurses and returns upstream face IDs unchanged; **D5** substrate PARALLEL to chamfer's (new `RoundFilletSpec` + new `pub(crate) trait RoundFilletUpstream` + new resolver arms in BOTH face + edge resolvers; helper table `CUBOID_EDGE_TAG_PAIRS` duplicated NOT shared per D5 substrate-parallelism); **D6** chamfer `FilletOp` BYTE-IDENTICAL — `fillet/mod.rs`, `fillet/cuboid.rs`, `FilletError`, sub-ε.α/β resolver arms preserved verbatim from c5c590a. **D7/D8 NOT consumed**: sub-α stops at Cuboid; sub-β Extrude / sub-γ Revolve cap-side / sub-δ Loft / sub-ε multi-edge corner blending / sub-ζ circular-path Revolve all deferred per D7. **Geometry per filleted edge** (N=ROUND_FILLET_SEGMENTS=8): 4 inset vertices (vertex_pos + r·face_inward, one per adjacent-face-per-endpoint combination) + 2·(N+1) = 18 cylinder vertices (parameterized quarter arc) appended to upstream positions; face-strip removal via vertex-substitution (face_a / face_b triangles located via `face_labels` lookup; vertex_a → inset_a_idx and vertex_b → inset_b_idx substituted in those triangles only; OTHER faces' shared corner references kept byte-identical → v0 "corner gap" visual imperfection per ADR D8); 2·N = 16 cylinder triangles per edge appended with `TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE`. **Math**: cylinder axis_center = vertex_pos + r·(face_a_inward + face_b_inward); radial at angle θ ∈ [0, π/2] = -face_b_inward·cos(θ) - face_a_inward·sin(θ); at θ=0 cylinder vertex coincides with inset_a (on face A's plane), at θ=π/2 with inset_b (on face B's plane). **Cuboid inset directions**: face_a_inward = -N_b, face_b_inward = -N_a (in-plane perpendicular-to-edge inward directions for axis-aligned cuboid; verified unit-length and pairwise-perpendicular for all 12 canonical edges via `cuboid_resolve_round_spec_inward_vectors_unit_and_perpendicular` test). **Vertex/triangle count for unit cube + 1 edge**: 8 + 22 = 30 verts, 12 + 16 = 28 tris, 36 + 48 = 84 indices (verified via `evaluate_one_edge_produces_expected_vertex_and_triangle_counts`); 2 non-corner-sharing edges: 8 + 44 = 52 verts, 12 + 32 = 44 tris (verified via `evaluate_two_non_corner_sharing_edges_composes_cleanly`). **API surface**: `pub struct RoundFilletOp { edges, round_specs, radius, owner }` with `edges()` / `radius()` / `owner()` accessors + `pub fn new(upstream: &CuboidOp, owner: BRepOwnerId, edges: Vec<BRepEdgeId>, radius: f32) -> Result<Self, RoundFilletError>` constructor (thin delegate to `pub(super) fn from_upstream<U: RoundFilletUpstream>`); `pub enum RoundFilletError { InvalidRadius { radius }, EmptyEdgeSelection, EdgeNotInUpstream { edge }, UnsupportedEdgeGeometry { edge, reason } }` (`#[non_exhaustive]`, mirrors `FilletError`); `pub(crate) struct RoundFilletSpec { vertex_a, vertex_b, face_a_id, face_b_id, face_a_inward, face_b_inward }` + `pub(crate) trait RoundFilletUpstream: BRepEdgeProvider` (internal); `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(RoundFilletOp)` variant + `OpKind::RoundFillet` discriminant. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO per-face full vertex duplication of upstream positions (substitution only flips the 2 filleted-edge endpoints' indices in face_a + face_b triangles; ALL OTHER faces' references to those shared corners stay byte-identical); NO new cap-face / corner-patch `BRepFaceId` (D3 — cylinder triangles emit `TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE`); NO chamfer `FilletOp` change (D6 — `fillet/mod.rs` + `fillet/cuboid.rs` byte-identical to c5c590a; sub-ε.α/β resolver arms untouched); NO multi-edge corner-blending (D8 — sub-ε scope); NO circular-path Revolve edges (D8 — sub-ζ scope; reserved `RoundFilletError::UnsupportedEdgeGeometry` variant); NO new `impl BRepProvider for RoundFilletOp` (D4 — face identity flows via graph-level resolver); NO new `impl BRepEdgeProvider for RoundFilletOp` (D2 — edge identity flows via graph-level resolver); NO `Strategy::Winch` change; NO `BRepFaceId::KIND_FILLET_ROUND_CAP` bytestring landed (D3 pressure-deferred); NO architecture lint enforcing D1-D6; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change; NO new ADR (ADR-119 unchanged); NO new exemption / doctrine / §18 companion; NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` change; NO `plans/PLAN.md` / `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change; NO `unsafe`. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-cad-core` exit 0 (after mid-dispatch fmt apply for canonical wraps in fn signatures + assert!/format! line breaks); `cargo build -p rge-cad-core` clean (2.77s); `cargo test -p rge-cad-core --no-fail-fast` = 391 lib unit tests passed / 0 failed; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2466 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2439; +27 net — exact target: 8 in `round_fillet::tests` + 14 in `round_fillet::cuboid::tests` + 1 in `operators::tests` + 2 in `topology::resolve::tests` + 2 in `topology::edge_resolve::tests`); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS; byte-identical exemption count from ADR-119 design-only dispatch). Files modified (2 source NEW + 4 source EDIT + 3 docs): NEW `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs` (~640L: module doc with sub-α scope + NON-GOALS list + `RoundFilletError` enum + `RoundFilletSpec` struct + `RoundFilletUpstream` trait + `RoundFilletOp` struct + `Operator` impl with full `evaluate` body + 8 upstream-agnostic unit tests); NEW `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/cuboid.rs` (~470L: `CUBOID_EDGE_TAG_PAIRS` const + `cuboid_resolve_round_spec` + `RoundFilletUpstream` impl for `CuboidOp` + `RoundFilletOp::new` public constructor + duplicated `cuboid_edge_corner_indices` + `cuboid_face_normal` helpers per D5 + 14 unit tests); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/mod.rs` (+~20L net: `pub mod round_fillet` / `pub use round_fillet::{RoundFilletError, RoundFilletOp}` / `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(RoundFilletOp)` variant alphabetical-position between Revolve and Sweep / `OpKind::RoundFillet` discriminant / `as_operator()` arm / extended `op_kind_non_exhaustive_pattern_match_compiles` test / new `operator_node_dispatches_round_fillet` test); `crates/cad-core/src/topology/resolve.rs` (+~75L net: `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)` arm with Transform-style identity-preserving recursion per ADR D4 + `RoundFilletOp` import + 2 new tests `resolver_cuboid_then_round_fillet_inherits_cuboid_face_ids` + `resolver_round_fillet_face_ids_stable_under_radius_change`); `crates/cad-core/src/topology/edge_resolve.rs` (+~95L net: `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)` arm with ALL-upstream-edges-including-filleted preservation per ADR D2 + `RoundFilletOp` import + 2 new tests `resolver_cuboid_then_round_fillet_inherits_all_edges_including_filleted` + `resolver_round_fillet_preserves_all_edges_regardless_of_selection`); `crates/cad-core/src/lib.rs` (+2 items in re-export: `RoundFilletError, RoundFilletOp`); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **D-Fillet real round geometry chapter status**: sub-α CLOSED (this dispatch); ADR-119 D1-D6 proven in code; D7 sub-α path confirmed (Cuboid substrate landed cleanly with bounded MAY-list and zero halt-condition triggers); **NEXT** per D7 chapter shape: sub-β Extrude / sub-γ Revolve cap-side / sub-δ Loft / sub-ε multi-edge corner blending / sub-ζ circular-path Revolve — each its own dispatch with its own halt conditions; chapter cadence and ordering TBD by user.

2026-05-12 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter sub-β — Extrude cap-perimeter-only substrate, IMPLEMENTED): proves `RoundFilletUpstream` generalizes beyond Cuboid to a second upstream WITHOUT smuggling in general-dihedral cylinder math. **Per user direction at green-light**: "Option A: Extrude cap-perimeter only. That's the honest sub-β: it proves `RoundFilletUpstream for ExtrudeOp` without smuggling in general-dihedral cylinder math. Bottom/top perimeter edges are true 90 degree cases and fit sub-α's geometry; vertical seams should reject with the existing `UnsupportedEdgeGeometry` path and become sub-β.γ. Keep it tight: New `round_fillet/extrude.rs`. Add `mod extrude;` only. No changes to `RoundFilletOp`, `RoundFilletSpec`, `RoundFilletError`, or `evaluate`. No resolver changes. No chamfer changes. Tests should prove cap-perimeter acceptance, vertical-seam rejection, and profile-size scaling." Executed exactly that scope; no halt-condition triggers; substrate posture preserved bit-for-bit from sub-α. **Geometric honesty (load-bearing inspection finding)**: sub-α's cylinder parameterization (`axis_center = pos + r·(a + b)`, quarter-arc θ ∈ [0, π/2]) is correct only when `face_a_inward ⊥ face_b_inward` (dihedral = 90°). For ExtrudeOp's `3 * N` edges this partitions cleanly: bottom-perimeter (`Bottom ∩ Side(i)`) and top-perimeter (`Top ∩ Side(i)`) have 90° dihedrals **by construction** (Bottom/Top normal ±Z; Side normal in XY → `n_cap · n_side = 0` regardless of profile shape), while vertical-seam (`Side(i) ∩ Side((i+1)%N)`) dihedrals depend on the profile's interior angle and are 90° only for rectangular profiles. Sub-β admits the 2N cap-perimeter edges; vertical-seam edges reject at construction with `RoundFilletError::UnsupportedEdgeGeometry` (the existing reserved variant from sub-α). Generalization to arbitrary dihedrals is sub-β.γ — would require changing `RoundFilletOp::evaluate`'s body, which triggers the user halt condition. **API surface added**: `impl RoundFilletUpstream for ExtrudeOp` with three-class dispatch in `resolve_round_spec(canonical_index)` (bottom-perimeter ⊂ [0,N) / top-perimeter ⊂ [N,2N) / vertical-seam ⊂ [2N,3N) → Err); `pub fn RoundFilletOp::new_for_extrude(upstream: &ExtrudeOp, owner, edges, radius) -> Result<Self, RoundFilletError>` constructor (thin delegate to `from_upstream<U: RoundFilletUpstream>`). **Spec construction per cap-perimeter edge**: vertex_a/b from `extrude_bottom_perimeter_vertex_pair` or `_top_perimeter_vertex_pair` (parallel to chamfer's helpers per ADR-119 D5, NOT shared); face_a_id = `TopologyFaceId(0)` (Bottom) or `TopologyFaceId(1)` (Top); face_b_id = `TopologyFaceId(2 + i)` (Side(i)); face_a_inward = `[-side_normal_x, -side_normal_y, 0]` (in cap's plane, perpendicular to edge, pointing away from Side(i) into cap interior); face_b_inward = `(0, 0, 1)` for bottom-perimeter (toward Top from Bottom) or `(0, 0, -1)` for top-perimeter (toward Bottom from Top). Unit-length + pairwise-perpendicular verified for all 2N cap-perimeter edges across N=3/4/5 profiles via `resolve_round_spec_inward_vectors_unit_and_perpendicular_for_cap_perimeter` test. **Vertex/triangle counts verified**: unit-square Extrude (8 verts / 12 tris / 36 indices) + 1 bottom-perimeter fillet = 30 verts / 28 tris / 84 indices (matches sub-α Cuboid per-edge contribution: +22 verts / +16 tris because the geometry IS the same shape — only the upstream baseline differs by primitive). Profile-size scaling: 1 cap-perimeter fillet adds CONSTANT 22 verts / 16 tris regardless of profile size (triangle / square / pentagon), confirming `RoundFilletUpstream` impl is profile-shape-agnostic for cap-perimeter edges. **Substrate byte-identical to sub-α** (`7087589`): `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs::RoundFilletOp` struct + `Operator::evaluate` body + `RoundFilletError` enum + `RoundFilletSpec` struct + `RoundFilletUpstream` trait + sub-α tests untouched; `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/cuboid.rs` untouched; `crates/cad-core/src/topology/{resolve, edge_resolve}.rs` `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)` arms untouched (Extrude flows through them via the existing identity-preserving recursion — no operator-specific arm change needed); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/mod.rs` `OperatorNode::RoundFillet` variant + `OpKind::RoundFillet` discriminant + `as_operator()` arm untouched; `crates/cad-core/src/lib.rs` re-exports untouched. **Chamfer `FilletOp` byte-identical** per ADR-119 D6 (`fillet/mod.rs` + `fillet/extrude.rs` + sub-ε.α/β resolver arms preserved verbatim from `c5c590a` → `7087589`). **HARD NON-GOALS observed (zero halt-condition triggers)**: NO change to `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` body (would have triggered "broader face-strip algorithm" halt); NO change to `RoundFilletSpec` shape; NO change to `RoundFilletError` variants (UnsupportedEdgeGeometry was already reserved at sub-α); NO new resolver arm (existing `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)` arms in face + edge resolvers cover Extrude transparently); NO chamfer-side change; NO new exemption / doctrine / ADR / lint / §18 companion; NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` change; NO `plans/PLAN.md` / `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change;
