Files modified (1 source NEW + 1 source EDIT + 3 docs): NEW `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/extrude.rs` (~620L: module doc with sub-β scope + edge-class dispatch + `RoundFilletUpstream` impl + `RoundFilletOp::new_for_extrude` constructor + `extrude_bottom_perimeter_vertex_pair` + `_top_perimeter_vertex_pair` + `extrude_side_outward_normal` helpers (duplicated per D5) + 19 unit tests covering cap-perimeter accept + vertical-seam reject + profile-size scaling + helper correctness); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs` (+1 line: `mod extrude;` declaration after `mod cuboid;` — only change to substrate); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **D-Fillet real round geometry chapter status**: sub-α CLOSED (`7087589`); sub-β CLOSED (this dispatch); ADR-119 D1-D6 proven across TWO upstreams (Cuboid + Extrude cap-perimeter); D7 chapter cadence confirmed (substrate generalizes cleanly; per-upstream impls compose without rippling). **NEXT** per D7: sub-β.γ Extrude vertical-seam (general-dihedral cylinder math — changes `evaluate`) / sub-γ Revolve cap-side / sub-δ Loft / sub-ε multi-edge corner blending (cross-cutting) / sub-ζ circular-path Revolve — each its own dispatch with its own halt conditions; chapter cadence and ordering TBD by user.

2026-05-12 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter sub-γ — Revolve cap-side-only substrate, IMPLEMENTED): proves `RoundFilletUpstream` generalizes to a THIRD upstream WITHOUT changing `RoundFilletOp::evaluate`, `RoundFilletSpec`, `RoundFilletError`, the resolver arms, or any prior upstream impl. Substrate byte-identical to sub-α (`7087589`) + sub-β (`d147ba4`); only addition is `mod revolve;` + the new `round_fillet/revolve.rs` file. **Per user direction at green-light**: "Option A: Revolve cap-side only. The inspection clears the important boundary: partial-mode cap-side edges are straight, two-endpoint, and 90 degree by construction, while side-side edges remain circular-path rejects. That fits the existing `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` math and avoids the general-dihedral rewrite. Keep it tight: New `round_fillet/revolve.rs`. Add only `mod revolve;`. Full-mode rejects all edges. Partial side-side rejects. Partial start/end cap-side accepts. No changes to `RoundFilletOp`, `RoundFilletSpec`, `RoundFilletError`, `evaluate`, resolver arms, or prior upstream impls. The face-strip localization check is the one to watch; halt if substitution leaks beyond the target cap ring/side face." Executed exactly that scope; zero halt-condition triggers; the load-bearing localization test EMPIRICALLY pins substitution-localization-no-leak. **Inspection-first finding (load-bearing 90° dihedral algebraic proof)**: For start-cap-side edge at θ=0 — StartCap normal `n_A = (0,0,-1)` (per `partial_path.rs:105-108`); Side(i) outward normal at θ=0 derived from `∂P/∂t × ∂P/∂θ` where `P(t,θ) = ((x_i + t·dx)·cos θ, y_i + t·dy, (x_i + t·dx)·sin θ)` evaluates to `(dy, -dx, 0)/√(dx²+dy²)`; `n_A · n_side(0) = 0`. ✓ For end-cap-side edge at θ=angle — EndCap normal `n_A = (-sin(angle), 0, cos(angle))` (+tangent direction per `partial_path.rs:117-120`); Side(i) outward normal at θ=angle = `(dy·cos(angle), -dx, dy·sin(angle))/√(dx²+dy²)`; `n_A · n_side(angle) = (-sin·cos + cos·sin)·dy/||·|| = 0`. ✓ regardless of angle or profile shape. Both cap-side classes therefore fit sub-α's cylinder math (`axis_center = pos + r·(a+b)`, quarter-arc θ ∈ [0, π/2]) without requiring any change to the evaluate body. **Three-class dispatch**: `resolve_round_spec(canonical_index)` returns specs for partial-mode `[N, 2N)` start-cap-side + `[2N, 3N)` end-cap-side; returns Err for full-mode (any canonical index — all edges are closed circular paths) AND partial-mode `[0, N)` side-side (1/k-circular arcs). All Err returns propagate via `from_upstream` into `RoundFilletError::UnsupportedEdgeGeometry { edge, reason }` (variant reserved at sub-α + already validated against this case at sub-β). **API surface added (PUBLIC)**: `pub fn RoundFilletOp::new_for_revolve(upstream: &RevolveOp, owner: BRepOwnerId, edges: Vec<BRepEdgeId>, radius: f32) -> Result<Self, RoundFilletError>` — thin delegate to `from_upstream<U: RoundFilletUpstream>` (mirrors chamfer `FilletOp::new_for_revolve`'s shape but byte-distinct per D5). **Internal**: `impl RoundFilletUpstream for RevolveOp` + three private helpers (`revolve_start_cap_side_vertex_pair`, `revolve_end_cap_side_vertex_pair`, `revolve_profile_edge_xy` — parallel to chamfer per ADR-119 D5, NOT shared; CCW-profile convention matches sub-β + chamfer-revolve precedent). **Per-edge spec construction**: start-cap-side: vertex_a/b = (ring 0 indices i / (i+1)%N); face_a_id = `TopologyFaceId(N)` (StartCap); face_b_id = `TopologyFaceId(local)` (Side(local)); face_a_inward = `(-dy, dx, 0)/√(dx²+dy²)` (90°-CCW rotation of profile-edge tangent in XY plane, points into StartCap interior for CCW profile); face_b_inward = `(0, 0, 1)` (+swept-tangent at θ=0, perpendicular to edge in Side(local)'s tangent plane). End-cap-side: vertex_a/b = (ring `segments` indices `segments*N + i` / `segments*N + (i+1)%N`); face_a_id = `TopologyFaceId(N+1)` (EndCap); face_b_id = `TopologyFaceId(local)` (Side(local)); face_a_inward = `(-dy·cos(angle), dx, -dy·sin(angle))/√(dx²+dy²)` (90°-CCW rotation rotated to θ=angle); face_b_inward = `(sin(angle), 0, -cos(angle))` (-swept-tangent at θ=angle). Unit-length + pairwise-perpendicular verified for ALL `2*N` cap-side edges across 4 (profile, segments, angle) tuples including arbitrary non-π/2 angle 1.234 rad. **Face-strip localization invariant pinned by `evaluate_face_strip_localization_no_leak_beyond_target_faces` (user-flagged load-bearing watch test)**: pentagon profile + segments=6 + angle=π/2 (60 side tris + 6 cap tris = 66 upstream tris); fillet single start-cap-side edge i=0; iterate EVERY upstream-triangle slot post-substitution. Slots WHERE triangle-label is face_a (StartCap=5) or face_b (Side(0)) AND original index is vertex_a (0) or vertex_b (1) → MUST be substituted (assert flipped). All OTHER slots → MUST be byte-identical (assert NO leak — the `leaked_non_target_slots` Vec must be empty post-iteration). Additionally pinned: adjacent Side(4) at ring 0 has BR = (4+1)%5 = 0 = vertex_a, and Side(1) at ring 0 has BL = 1 = vertex_b; these references MUST be preserved (assert byte-identical) per the v0 "corner gap" pattern shared with Cuboid + Extrude precedents. **Verified vertex/triangle counts (per-edge contribution constant)**: partial Revolve (ring profile, segments=8, angle=π/2) baseline = `4*9 = 36 verts / 4*4-4 = 12 cap-tris + 2*4*8 = 64 side-tris = 76 tris total / 228 indices`; +1 start-cap-side fillet → +22 verts / +16 tris / +48 indices (matches sub-α Cuboid + sub-β Extrude cap-perimeter contribution exactly — geometry IS the same per-edge contribution regardless of upstream primitive). End-cap-side fillet: same +22/+16/+48 contribution (mirror across rotation axis). Profile-size + segments + angle scaling: per-edge contribution stays constant across (profile=ring/pentagon, segments=3/6/8/12/16, angle=π/2/π/1.234rad) tuples. **Substrate posture (byte-identical to sub-α `7087589` + sub-β `d147ba4`)**: `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs::RoundFilletOp` struct + `Operator::evaluate` body + `RoundFilletError` enum + `RoundFilletSpec` struct + `RoundFilletUpstream` trait + sub-α 8 unit tests preserved verbatim (only addition: 1 line `mod revolve;` declaration after `mod extrude;`); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/cuboid.rs` byte-identical (sub-α 14 tests); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/extrude.rs` byte-identical (sub-β 19 tests); `crates/cad-core/src/topology/{resolve, edge_resolve}.rs` `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)` arms byte-identical (Revolve flows through existing identity-preserving recursion arms); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/mod.rs` `OperatorNode::RoundFillet` variant + `OpKind::RoundFillet` discriminant + `as_operator` arm + all sub-α tests byte-identical; `crates/cad-core/src/lib.rs` re-exports byte-identical. **Chamfer `FilletOp` byte-identical** per ADR-119 D6 — `fillet/mod.rs` + `fillet/cuboid.rs` + `fillet/extrude.rs` + `fillet/revolve.rs` + `fillet/loft.rs` + sub-ε.α/β resolver arms preserved verbatim from `c5c590a` → `7087589` → `d147ba4`. **HARD NON-GOALS observed (zero halt-condition triggers across the dispatch)**: NO `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` change (would have triggered "broader face-strip algorithm" halt — protected by the architectural decision that the evaluate body is geometry-shape-driven NOT upstream-driven, AND by the algebraic 90° dihedral proof showing sub-α math holds for cap-side); NO `RoundFilletSpec` field set change; NO `RoundFilletError` variant addition (UnsupportedEdgeGeometry already reserved at sub-α); NO new resolver arm (existing arms cover Revolve transparently); NO change to prior upstream impls (`round_fillet/cuboid.rs` + `round_fillet/extrude.rs` byte-identical); NO chamfer-side change (D6); NO new architecture lint enforcing edge-class restrictions; NO new ADR / new exemption / new doctrine / §18 companion; NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` change; NO `plans/PLAN.md` / `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change; NO `unsafe`; NO side-side circular-path support (deferred to sub-ζ); NO general-dihedral cylinder math (would change evaluate; deferred); NO chamfer's centroid-based inward direction (round-fillet's cap-side analysis uses face-perpendicular formulas — algebraically derived for true 90° dihedrals, NOT the centroid heuristic chamfer uses for non-perpendicular cases). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-cad-core` exit 0 (after one mid-dispatch fmt apply for canonical wraps — no semantic change); `cargo build -p rge-cad-core` clean (2.75s); `cargo test -p rge-cad-core --lib --no-fail-fast` = 428 unit tests passed / 0 failed (was 410; +18 net for sub-γ — all 18 in `operators::round_fillet::revolve::tests`); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2503 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2485; +18 net — exact target); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS; byte-identical exemption count from sub-β `d147ba4` — sub-γ substrate absorbs cleanly without any new exemption). Files modified (1 source NEW + 1 source EDIT + 3 docs): NEW `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/revolve.rs` (~640L: module doc with sub-γ scope + 5-section structure + `RoundFilletUpstream` impl with three-class dispatch + `RoundFilletOp::new_for_revolve` constructor + `revolve_start_cap_side_vertex_pair` + `revolve_end_cap_side_vertex_pair` + `revolve_profile_edge_xy` helpers + 18 unit tests: 5 invariant rejects + 1 full-mode all-edges-unsupported + 1 partial-mode side-side rejection + 1 mixed-selection rejection + 3 cap-side accepts (start/end/all-2N) + 2 evaluation counts (start/end) + 1 profile+segments+angle scaling test + **1 face-strip-localization-no-leak load-bearing test** + 3 helper/resolver correctness tests); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs` (+1 LINE NET: `mod revolve;` declaration after `mod extrude;`); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **D-Fillet real round geometry chapter status**: sub-α CLOSED (`7087589`); sub-β CLOSED (`d147ba4`); sub-γ CLOSED (this dispatch); ADR-119 D1-D6 proven across THREE upstreams (Cuboid full + Extrude cap-perimeter + Revolve cap-side); D7 chapter cadence further confirmed — substrate generalizes per-upstream without rippling AND the explicit-rejection-of-out-of-scope-edge-classes pattern (via pre-reserved `UnsupportedEdgeGeometry`) scales cleanly to mode-dependent operators. **NEXT** per D7 chapter shape: sub-δ Loft (next 90° upstream slice — Loft Bottom/Top × Side dihedrals are 90° by construction same as Extrude/Revolve, fits same scope pattern) / sub-β.γ Extrude vertical-seam (general-dihedral cylinder math — DOES change `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` body; sibling dispatch with its own design-pin and halt conditions) / sub-ε multi-edge corner blending (cross-cutting) / sub-ζ general circular-path Revolve (multi-segment spec evolution; subsumes Revolve full-mode + partial side-side) — each its own dispatch with its own halt conditions; chapter cadence and ordering TBD by user.

2026-05-12 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter sub-δ — Loft HALT-AND-REFRAME, no code change): inspection-first finding halts sub-δ Loft entirely; advances chapter cadence by deferring Loft until sub-β.γ general-dihedral cylinder math lands. **Per user direction at inspection report**: "Choose Option A: halt sub-δ entirely. The inspection found the right boundary. Loft is not another guaranteed-90 degree upstream; its side geometry can be tilted/non-planar, so forcing it into the current cap-side machinery would either be dishonest or nearly valueless. No placeholder API is needed yet." **Inspection-first algebraic finding (load-bearing halt rationale)**: For Loft bottom-perimeter edge `i`, Side(i)'s adjacent triangle `(BL, BR, TR)` has plane normal `n_side = (dx_a, dy_a, 0) × (ex, ey, length) = (dy_a·length, -dx_a·length, dx_a·ey − dy_a·ex)` where `(dx_a, dy_a) = profile_a[i+1] − profile_a[i]` and `(ex, ey) = profile_b[i+1] − profile_a[i]`. Bottom normal is `(0, 0, -1)`. Dot: `n_side · n_Bottom = -(dx_a·ey − dy_a·ex)`, which equals zero ONLY when `(ex, ey) ∥ (dx_a, dy_a)`. For Extrude (profile_b == profile_a) this always holds; for general Loft it generally doesn't. Counter-example: profile_a = unit square, profile_b = Y-translated unit square (identity vertex pairing) → bottom-perimeter edge 0 has `dx_a·ey − dy_a·ex = 1 ≠ 0`. Side(0)'s triangle tilts; cap-side dihedral is NOT 90°. Symmetric problem for top-perimeter; vertical-seam edges are diagonals with even less constraint. **Loft halt-condition triggered** per user's "Halt if Loft has twist/non-planar side geometry that breaks the cap-side equivalence". Sub-δ would either force Extrude-like approximation (dishonest cylinder placement off the actual tilted side surface) or require general-dihedral cylinder math (which IS sub-β.γ's scope). **Resolution**: halt sub-δ entirely; no placeholder API; chapter advances to sub-β.γ as the real next blocker for BOTH Extrude vertical-seam AND Loft. After sub-β.γ lands, Loft can become a thin upstream layer atop the general-dihedral machinery; circular-path Revolve (sub-ζ) remains separate. **No code changes this dispatch**: substrate byte-identical to sub-γ `c0b881a`. **Verification gates**: NO `cargo fmt`/`build`/`test`/`lint` runs required for docs-only halt (substrate untouched). Workspace state unchanged from sub-γ `c0b881a`: 2503 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored. **D-Fillet chapter status**: sub-α + sub-β + sub-γ CLOSED; sub-δ HALTED with reframe; ADR-119 D1-D6 proven across THREE 90°-upstream cases (Cuboid full + Extrude cap-perimeter + Revolve cap-side); D7 chapter cadence refined — "all guaranteed-90° upstreams" plateau is COMPLETE; the chapter's next blocker is the shared `evaluate`-body math change. **NEXT**: sub-β.γ general-dihedral cylinder math.

2026-05-12 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter sub-β.γ — general-dihedral cylinder math, IMPLEMENTED): replaces sub-α/β/γ's 90°-only cylinder formulas in `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` with a single general-dihedral code path that supports ANY non-degenerate dihedral angle. Substrate posture: `RoundFilletOp` struct + `RoundFilletSpec` fields + `RoundFilletError` enum variants + `RoundFilletUpstream` trait + per-upstream impls + resolver arms ALL byte-identical to sub-γ `c0b881a`; ONLY the `evaluate` body math changes (inset placement / axis_center / arc parameterization) + a new private `DIHEDRAL_EPSILON_SQ` constant + 6 new tests pinning the general-dihedral invariants. **Per user direction at green-light**: "Green-light sub-β.γ under Posture A. Use one general-dihedral code path and assert tight geometry equivalence for the existing 90 degree cases. I would not add a byte-identical fast path; two formulas would become the real maintenance risk. One small tightening: clamp `dot_ab` to [-1.0, 1.0] before `acos` / `sqrt`, then apply the near-degenerate guard. That keeps future computed normals from producing NaN on tiny float overshoot without changing the spec shape. Proceed with the MAY-list as written: `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` math only, no struct/spec/error/trait shape changes, no upstream impl edits, no resolver edits, and tests pinning 60 degree, 90 degree, 120 degree, radius, endpoints, and degenerate rejection." Executed exactly that scope; zero halt-condition triggers. **General-dihedral formulas (derived algebraically; reduce to sub-α/β/γ exactly at φ=90°)**: for unit inward vectors `a` and `b` with interior dihedral `φ = arccos(a · b)`: `inset_a = pos + r·(1 + a·b)/sin(φ)·a` (= `r·cot(φ/2)·a`; = `r·a` at φ=90°); `axis_center = pos + r·(a + b)/sin(φ)` (= `r·(a+b)` at φ=90°); `vertex(θ) = axis_center + r·(cos(θ+φ)·a − cos(θ)·b)/sin(φ)` for θ ∈ [0, π−φ] (= `axis_center + r·(−sin(θ)·a − cos(θ)·b)` at φ=90° — byte-identical to sub-α's `−b·cos(θ) − a·sin(θ)` radial). Reductions verified algebraically AND empirically pinned by the 90°-regression test. **`dot_ab.clamp(-1.0, 1.0)` tightening per user direction**: applied before `acos` / `sqrt` to prevent NaN on tiny float overshoot from non-unit-length inputs (future upstreams computing normals with ULP-level drift). The near-degenerate guard `sin_phi_sq < DIHEDRAL_EPSILON_SQ` (with `DIHEDRAL_EPSILON_SQ = 1e-6` → `|sin(φ)| < 1e-3` → φ within ~0.057° of 0° or 180°) catches truly-degenerate dihedrals where the formulas would divide by zero; rejection routed through existing `OpError::InvalidParameter` (NO new `RoundFilletError` variant per Posture A). **`RoundFilletSpec` shape UNCHANGED**: still carries only vertex_a / vertex_b / face_a_id / face_b_id / face_a_inward / face_b_inward; the general-dihedral math derives `dot_ab` / `sin_phi` / `phi` / `inset_scale` / `axis_scale` at evaluation time from the existing two inward vectors. **Face-strip identity contract PRESERVED**: substitution loop body byte-identical (`if *label == spec.face_a_id { (inset_a1_idx, inset_a2_idx) } else if *label == spec.face_b_id { (inset_b1_idx, inset_b2_idx) } else { continue; }` followed by index flip on vertex_a/vertex_b) — only POSITIONS of the 4 inset vertices change (no longer `pos + r·a` but `pos + r·cot(φ/2)·a`); their INDICES, their roles in the face-strip substitution, and their pairing with vertex_a/vertex_b stay identical. **Six new tests pinning invariants** (all in `operators::round_fillet::tests`): `evaluate_60_degree_dihedral_inset_distance_matches_cot_half_phi` (acute dihedral; inset reaches further than 90° case at r·√3); `evaluate_90_degree_dihedral_geometry_equivalence_with_sub_alpha_formula` (regression — inset / axis_center / cylinder-radius all within 1e-5 of sub-α formula); `evaluate_120_degree_dihedral_inset_distance_matches_cot_half_phi` (obtuse dihedral; inset closer at r/√3); `evaluate_cylinder_vertex_radius_invariant_across_dihedrals` (every cylinder vertex at distance r from its axis_center across 60°/90°/120°); `evaluate_arc_endpoints_coincide_with_inset_vertices` (vertex(θ=0) ≈ inset_a1 AND vertex(θ=arc_span) ≈ inset_b1 within 1e-4 across all 3 dihedrals — pins arc parameterization consistency with inset placement); `evaluate_rejects_near_degenerate_dihedral_at_zero_and_pi` (parallel-inward a·b=1, anti-parallel a·b=-1, and ULP-overshoot non-unit a·b>1 all reject via `OpError::InvalidParameter`). Synthetic-spec test helpers `synthetic_upstream_for_general_dihedral_tests()` + `make_synthetic_op(face_a_inward, face_b_inward, radius)` use direct `pub(super)` field-access (visible to `mod.rs::tests` since super=round_fillet) — bypass `RoundFilletUpstream::resolve_round_spec` so tests can exercise non-90° specs that no current upstream impl produces. **Substrate byte-identical to sub-γ `c0b881a`**: `RoundFilletOp` struct + `RoundFilletError` + `RoundFilletSpec` + `RoundFilletUpstream` trait + ALL prior tests preserved verbatim (sub-α 8 mod.rs tests + 14 cuboid.rs tests + sub-β 19 extrude.rs tests + sub-γ 18 revolve.rs tests + 2 resolve.rs tests + 2 edge_resolve.rs tests + 1 operators::tests dispatches_round_fillet); `round_fillet/cuboid.rs` + `round_fillet/extrude.rs` + `round_fillet/revolve.rs` byte-identical (no per-upstream impl edits per user direction); `topology/{resolve, edge_resolve}.rs` resolver arms byte-identical; `operators/mod.rs` + `lib.rs` byte-identical. **Chamfer `FilletOp` byte-identical** per ADR-119 D6. **HARD NON-GOALS observed (zero halt-condition triggers)**: NO `RoundFilletSpec` shape change; NO face-strip substitution semantic change; NO `RoundFilletError` variant addition; NO upstream impl edits; NO resolver-arm edits; NO chamfer change; NO regression in sub-α / sub-β / sub-γ test suites (all 64 prior `round_fillet` tests pass without modification — the 90° cases reduce to sub-α formulas exactly within geometry-equivalence epsilon, and all existing tests check COUNTS / LABELS / INDICES not exact float positions, so any 1-ULP drift from `f32::PI − f32::FRAC_PI_2` is undetectable downstream); NO `ROUND_FILLET_SEGMENTS` constant change (N=8 stays; arc_span = π − φ subdivides the dihedral-dependent span — finer tessellation for acute dihedrals comes for free); NO byte-identical fast path for 90° (Posture A — one code path; two formulas would be the real maintenance risk per user direction); NO new architecture lint; NO new ADR / new exemption / new doctrine / §18 companion (one new SPLIT-EXEMPTION annotation on `round_fillet/mod.rs` for the 1000-line file-size cap is per PLAN.md §1.3 Rule 3 — same pattern `extrude.rs::SPLIT-EXEMPTION` + `loft.rs::SPLIT-EXEMPTION` already use; file-size lint compliance, NOT a new architectural exemption — the architecture-lints supplementary report shows byte-identical exemption count from sub-γ); NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` change; NO `plans/PLAN.md` / `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change; NO `unsafe`; NO per-upstream extension to non-90° edges (sub-δ Loft revisit / sub-β.γ-extend Extrude vertical-seam are subsequent dispatches that consume the general-dihedral machinery this lands). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-cad-core` exit 0 (no diff); `cargo build -p rge-cad-core` clean (1.64s); `cargo test -p rge-cad-core --lib --no-fail-fast` = 434 unit tests passed / 0 failed (was 428; +6 net for sub-β.γ); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2509 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2503; +6 net — exact target; all 6 new tests in `operators::round_fillet::tests`); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS after adding SPLIT-EXEMPTION annotation; byte-identical architectural-exemption count from sub-γ `c0b881a` — the SPLIT-EXEMPTION is file-size-cap compliance, NOT an architectural exemption). **Files modified (1 source EDIT + 3 docs)**: `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs` (+~270L net: 1 SPLIT-EXEMPTION header annotation per PLAN.md §1.3 Rule 3; 1 new `DIHEDRAL_EPSILON_SQ` private constant after `ROUND_FILLET_SEGMENTS`; `evaluate` body inset/axis_center/arc-parameterization section replaced ~70 lines → ~110 lines with general-dihedral formulas + `dot_ab.clamp` + degenerate-dihedral guard + inline math derivation comments; 6 new tests + 2 test helpers `synthetic_upstream_for_general_dihedral_tests` + `make_synthetic_op` appended to `mod tests`); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **D-Fillet real round geometry chapter status**: sub-α + sub-β + sub-γ CLOSED (90° upstream plateau); sub-δ HALTED with reframe; sub-β.γ CLOSED (this dispatch); ADR-119 D1-D6 proven across THREE 90°-upstream cases AND the shared evaluate-body math now supports any non-degenerate dihedral. **NEXT**: per-upstream lifts can now land thinly atop the general-dihedral machinery — candidate sub-dispatches sub-β.γ-extend (Extrude vertical-seam lifts `UnsupportedEdgeGeometry` on canonical `[2N, 3N)` edges) / sub-δ.revisit (Loft as thin upstream layer atop general-dihedral substrate) / sub-ε multi-edge corner blending (cross-cutting) / sub-ζ general circular-path Revolve (multi-segment spec evolution; subsumes Revolve full-mode + Revolve partial side-side) — each its own dispatch with its own halt conditions; chapter cadence and ordering TBD by user.

2026-05-12 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter sub-δ.revisit — Loft full upstream layer, IMPLEMENTED): adds `impl RoundFilletUpstream for LoftOp` as a thin upstream layer atop the sub-β.γ general-dihedral substrate (`cae3a84`). Sub-β.γ's general-dihedral cylinder math made the previously-halted Loft case implementable WITHOUT modifying `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` or `RoundFilletSpec` shape; sub-δ.revisit lands the per-upstream spec-construction dispatch using TRUE incident-side-triangle plane normals (not chamfer's Extrude-style XY-only approximation). All `3 * N` Loft edges (bottom-perimeter + top-perimeter + vertical-seam) accept under sub-β.γ machinery, proven empirically on a non-uniform translated-square Loft with a 45° interior dihedral. **Per user direction at green-light**: "Green-light sub-δ.revisit: Loft full upstream layer. The general-dihedral substrate is now doing exactly what it was meant to do, and the triangle-incident convention is tight enough as a v0 local rule. Pin it with inline comments and tests, not an ADR addendum. Scope as written: New `round_fillet/loft.rs`. Add only `mod loft;`. Accept bottom/top perimeter and vertical-seam edges using the incident side-triangle normals. Reject only mismatched profile lengths / zero normals / genuinely degenerate cases. No changes to `RoundFilletOp`, `RoundFilletSpec`, `evaluate`, prior upstream impls, resolver arms, chamfer, or ADR. Make sure the tests include the non-uniform translated Loft case; that's the proof that this is no longer just the old 90 degree plateau wearing a clever hat." Executed exactly that scope; zero halt-condition triggers. **Triangle-incidence convention (v0, load-bearing)**: Each Loft `Side(i)` quad is split into 2 triangles by the diagonal `bot_i → top_{i+1}` (`loft.rs::evaluate` L320-340): `Tri 1(i) = (bot_i, bot_{i+1}, top_{i+1})` and `Tri 2(i) = (bot_i, top_{i+1}, top_i)`. For non-uniform Lofts (`profile_a ≠ profile_b`) the two triangles generally have DIFFERENT plane normals — the quad is non-planar. The v0 convention resolves the ambiguity by picking the triangle INCIDENT to the selected edge: bottom-perimeter `i` → Side(i)'s Tri 1 (contains both `bot_i` + `bot_{i+1}`); top-perimeter `i` → Side(i)'s Tri 2 (contains both `top_i` + `top_{i+1}`); vertical-seam `i` → Side(i)'s Tri 1 + Side((i+1)%N)'s Tri 2 (both contain `bot_{i+1}` + `top_{i+1}`). Pinned by inline module-doc comment + the `evaluate_translated_loft_*` test family — NOT an ADR addendum per user direction. **Inward-direction algorithm (orientation-robust)**: `candidate = normalize(cross(edge_tangent, n_own_face))` (perpendicular to both → in face's tangent plane); sign-check `dot(candidate, -n_other_face)` — flip if negative. Works for any non-degenerate dihedral because the `n_own_face`-component of `n_other_face` projects away from the dot test (since `candidate ⊥ n_own_face` by construction). Reduces to sub-β Extrude's formula byte-for-byte for the perpendicular case (`profile_b == profile_a` → `N_tri1 = (dy_a·length, -dx_a·length, 0)` matches Extrude's side-normal exactly). **Geometric verification — 45° dihedral on translated Loft** (the algebraic example pre-validated in sub-δ.revisit inspection report, now empirically pinned by `resolve_round_spec_translated_loft_bottom_perimeter_0_yields_45_degree_dihedral`): `profile_a = unit_square at origin`, `profile_b = unit_square translated by (0, 1)`, length=1. Bottom-perimeter edge 0 → Side(0)'s Tri 1 = `((0,0,0), (1,0,0), (1,1,1))`; un-normalized N = `(0, -1, 1)` (norm `√2`); n_face_b = `(0, -1, 1)/√2`. n_face_a (Bottom) = `(0, 0, -1)`. After orient_inward algorithm: face_a_inward = `(0, 1, 0)`, face_b_inward = `(0, 1, 1)/√2`. `a · b = 1/√2 ≈ 0.7071` ⇒ interior dihedral φ = `arccos(1/√2) = 45°`. Sub-β.γ's evaluate accepts this (`sin_phi_sq = 0.5 ≫ 1e-6 = DIHEDRAL_EPSILON_SQ`); per-edge geometry produces +22v/+16t/+48i (same as sub-α/β/γ — the per-edge contribution is dihedral-agnostic). **API surface added (PUBLIC)**: `pub fn RoundFilletOp::new_for_loft(upstream: &LoftOp, owner: BRepOwnerId, edges: Vec<BRepEdgeId>, radius: f32) -> Result<Self, RoundFilletError>` — thin delegate to `from_upstream<U: RoundFilletUpstream>`; **first** upstream constructor that lifts the 90° dihedral restriction (Cuboid + Extrude cap-perimeter + Revolve cap-side are all 90°-by-construction; Loft is the first with genuinely non-90° dihedrals on accept-path edges). **Internal**: `impl RoundFilletUpstream for LoftOp` with three-class dispatch + 7 private helpers (parallel to chamfer per ADR-119 D5, NOT shared): `loft_bottom_perimeter_vertex_pair` / `loft_top_perimeter_vertex_pair` / `loft_vertical_seam_vertex_pair` (positional indexing); `loft_bottom_edge_tangent` / `loft_top_edge_tangent` / `loft_vertical_seam_edge_tangent` (unit edge tangent vectors — vertical-seam tangent is generally DIAGONAL in 3D when profile_a ≠ profile_b); `loft_side_tri1_outward_normal` / `loft_side_tri2_outward_normal` (true triangle plane normals — non-zero Z component for non-uniform Lofts). Plus shared `cross3` / `dot3` / `neg3` / `normalize3` / `orient_inward` / `solve_inward_directions` helpers local to `round_fillet/loft.rs`. **Rejection cases (genuinely unsupported/degenerate, per user direction)**: `profile_a.len() != profile_b.len()` (defensive — `LoftOp::evaluate` already rejects, but resolver also rejects so callers get early-fail signal even if only querying `brep_edge_ids` + this trait without calling `evaluate`; rejected via `RoundFilletError::UnsupportedEdgeGeometry`); zero-magnitude triangle plane normals (collinear corners — unreachable for valid Loft since Polygon2D rejects coincident adjacent points + length > 0); zero-length edge tangents (defensive — same Polygon2D rejection). Near-degenerate dihedrals are NOT pre-empted at construction — sub-β.γ's evaluate-time `DIHEDRAL_EPSILON_SQ` guard catches them. **Substrate posture (substrate byte-identical to sub-β.γ `cae3a84`)**: `RoundFilletOp` struct + `Operator::evaluate` body + `RoundFilletError` enum + `RoundFilletSpec` struct + `RoundFilletUpstream` trait + sub-α/β/γ + sub-β.γ tests preserved verbatim (sub-α 8 mod.rs tests + 14 cuboid.rs tests + sub-β 19 extrude.rs tests + sub-γ 18 revolve.rs tests + sub-β.γ 6 general-dihedral tests + 2 resolve.rs tests + 2 edge_resolve.rs tests + 1 operators::tests dispatches_round_fillet — total 70 prior round_fillet tests, ALL pass without modification); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/cuboid.rs` byte-identical; `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/extrude.rs` byte-identical; `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/revolve.rs` byte-identical; `crates/cad-core/src/topology/{resolve, edge_resolve}.rs` resolver arms byte-identical (LoftOp upstream flows through existing identity-preserving recursion arms; face IDs inherit per ADR D4, edge IDs inherit per D2 — `LoftOp::BRepProvider` + `BRepEdgeProvider` impls already provide everything the arms recurse into); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/mod.rs` (`OperatorNode::RoundFillet` + `OpKind::RoundFillet` + `as_operator()` arm + dispatches test) byte-identical; `crates/cad-core/src/lib.rs` re-exports byte-identical. **Chamfer `FilletOp` byte-identical** per ADR-119 D6 — `fillet/{mod,cuboid,extrude,revolve,loft}.rs` + sub-ε.α/β resolver arms preserved verbatim from `c5c590a` → `7087589` → `d147ba4` → `c0b881a` → `cae3a84` → THIS DISPATCH. **HARD NON-GOALS observed (zero halt-condition triggers)**: NO `RoundFilletOp` struct shape change (would have triggered HALT-A); NO `RoundFilletSpec` shape change (would have triggered HALT-B — current proposal uses only existing 6 fields; trigonometric scalars derived in evaluate at sub-β.γ landed); NO `RoundFilletError` variant addition; NO `evaluate` change (sub-β.γ already lifts the 90°-only restriction — that's the whole point of this dispatch being a thin upstream layer); NO chamfer-side change (D6); NO change to prior upstream impls (cuboid.rs + extrude.rs + revolve.rs byte-identical); NO resolver-arm edit (existing arms cover LoftOp transparently); NO new ADR / new ADR addendum (triangle-incidence convention pinned by inline module-doc comment + tests per user direction "not an ADR addendum"); NO new architecture lint; NO new exemption / doctrine / §18 companion; NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` change; NO `plans/PLAN.md` / `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change; NO `unsafe`; NO regression in 70 prior round_fillet tests (all pass — counts/labels/indices unchanged for sub-α/β/γ paths; sub-β.γ general-dihedral tests pass because the math reduces to sub-α byte-for-byte at 90°). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-cad-core` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-cad-core` clean (1.76s); `cargo test -p rge-cad-core --lib --no-fail-fast` = 450 unit tests passed / 0 failed (was 434; +16 net for sub-δ.revisit); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2525 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2509; +16 net — exact target; all 16 new tests in `operators::round_fillet::loft::tests`); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS; byte-identical architectural-exemption count from sub-β.γ `cae3a84` — no new SPLIT-EXEMPTION needed: `round_fillet/loft.rs` lands at ~810 LoC, well under 1000-line cap). **Sixteen new tests pinning invariants** (all in `operators::round_fillet::loft::tests`): 5 invariant rejects (zero/neg/non-finite/empty/unknown radius); 1 mismatched-profile-lengths reject; 3 acceptance tests across Loft variations (identity-profile = Extrude / **translated non-uniform 45° dihedral** / twisted rotated-profile); 3 evaluate counts tests (**translated bottom-perimeter at 45° dihedral** — the load-bearing user-flagged case; translated vertical-seam edge / identity bottom-perimeter at 90°); 1 resolve_round_spec face-ID-mapping correctness across canonical emission order for translated Loft; 1 inward-vectors-unit invariant across 3 Loft variations × 3N edges; 1 **45° dihedral verification** (`a·b ≈ 1/√2`) for translated bottom-perimeter edge 0 — the geometric proof; 1 **face-strip localization no-leak watch test** for translated Loft (Loft-specific load-bearing invariant, mirrors sub-γ Revolve's). Files modified (1 source NEW + 1 source EDIT + 3 docs): NEW `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/loft.rs` (~810L: module doc with sub-δ.revisit scope + edge eligibility + triangle-incidence convention + inward-direction algorithm + substrate posture sections + `RoundFilletUpstream` impl with three-class dispatch + `RoundFilletOp::new_for_loft` constructor + 7 private helpers + 4 vector primitives + `orient_inward` + `solve_inward_directions` + 16 unit tests); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs` (+1 LINE NET: `mod loft;` declaration after `mod extrude;` — alphabetical position between extrude and revolve); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **D-Fillet real round geometry chapter status**: sub-α + sub-β + sub-γ + sub-β.γ + sub-δ.revisit CLOSED; ADR-119 D1-D6 proven across FOUR upstreams (Cuboid full + Extrude cap-perimeter + Revolve cap-side + Loft full) AND the shared evaluate-body math now supports any non-degenerate dihedral; D7 chapter cadence confirmed — substrate generalizes per-upstream without rippling AND the general-dihedral substrate composes correctly with new per-upstream layers that use true incident-triangle plane normals. **NEXT**: candidate sub-dispatches sub-β.γ-extend (Extrude vertical-seam lifts `UnsupportedEdgeGeometry` on canonical `[2N, 3N)` edges; thin per-upstream lift atop the general-dihedral machinery now that Loft has proven the pattern) / sub-ε multi-edge corner blending (cross-cutting; ADR D8 scope) / sub-ζ general circular-path Revolve (multi-segment spec evolution; subsumes Revolve full-mode + Revolve partial side-side — DIFFERENT shape from non-90° dihedrals; remains separate per user direction) — each its own dispatch with its own halt conditions; chapter cadence and ordering TBD by user.

2026-05-12 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter sub-β.γ-extend — Extrude vertical-seam lift, IMPLEMENTED): lifts the sub-β vertical-seam rejection in `round_fillet/extrude.rs` to acceptance using sub-β.γ's general-dihedral machinery + sub-δ.revisit Loft's proven orient_inward / solve_inward_directions algorithm. **Completes Extrude coverage** — all `3 * N` Extrude edges (bottom-perimeter + top-perimeter + vertical-seam) now accept. **Per user direction at green-light**: "Green-light sub-β.γ-extend exactly as scoped. The two reject-test flips are legitimate boundary bookkeeping: those tests assert the old unsupported seam behavior, and this dispatch is specifically lifting that boundary. Keep the scope tight to `round_fillet/extrude.rs` plus log docs; no shared `RoundFilletSpec` / `evaluate` / resolver / prior-upstream / chamfer changes. Halt only if the vertical-seam spec needs shape changes, the face-strip no-leak watch fails, unmodified sub-β or prior upstream tests regress, or the degenerate guard rejects valid Extrude seams. Otherwise proceed." Executed exactly that scope; zero halt-condition triggers. **Geometric setup (simpler than sub-δ.revisit Loft)**: Extrude has `profile_b == profile_a` positionally, so vertical-seam tangent is purely `e_t = (0, 0, 1)` (NOT diagonal-in-3D as Loft's can be) AND each Side(i) is a planar quad (NOT a non-planar quad with triangle-incidence ambiguity). The orient_inward / solve_inward_directions algorithm proven by Loft works unchanged. **Three-class dispatch in `extrude.rs::resolve_round_spec` now**: `[0, N)` bottom-perimeter (sub-β; UNCHANGED) → spec with 90° dihedral; `[N, 2N)` top-perimeter (sub-β; UNCHANGED) → spec with 90° dihedral; `[2N, 3N)` vertical-seam (sub-β.γ-extend; **WAS Err, NOW spec**) → uses `extrude_side_outward_normal` for both adjacent Side(i) and Side((i+1)%N), `e_t = (0, 0, 1)`, `solve_inward_directions` returns the orientation-robust inward pair. Dihedral equals the profile's interior polygon angle: 90° for square, 60° for equilateral triangle, 108° for regular pentagon. **Two pre-sub-β.γ-extend reject tests FLIPPED to acceptance tests** (per user direction "two reject-test flips are legitimate boundary bookkeeping"): `new_for_extrude_rejects_vertical_seam_edges_with_unsupported_edge_geometry` → `new_for_extrude_accepts_vertical_seam_edges_after_general_dihedral_lift` (asserts all 4 square vertical-seam edges accept); `new_for_extrude_rejects_mixed_selection_with_vertical_seam` → `new_for_extrude_accepts_mixed_selection_with_vertical_seam` (cap-perimeter + vertical-seam selection accepts). Plus 1 additional trailing-assertion flip inside the existing `resolve_round_spec_face_ids_match_canonical_emission_order` test (the final 2 lines previously asserted Err on canonical 8 — now assert acceptance + face_a=Side(0), face_b=Side(1); rest of the test unchanged). **Sub-β.γ-extend new tests** (9 new): `new_for_extrude_accepts_all_3n_edges_for_square_profile` (full Extrude upstream closure proof — 12 edges for square); `new_for_extrude_accepts_all_3n_edges_for_pentagon_profile` (15 edges for pentagon); `evaluate_one_vertical_seam_edge_produces_expected_counts_on_square` (+22v/+16t/+48i — same per-edge contribution as cap-perimeter; the contribution is dihedral-agnostic); `evaluate_one_vertical_seam_edge_produces_expected_counts_on_pentagon` (108° interior dihedral; same +22v/+16t/+48i contribution — arc subtends `π - φ` radians = 72° for pentagon vs 90° for square but `ROUND_FILLET_SEGMENTS = 8` subdivides identically); `resolve_round_spec_vertical_seam_face_ids_match_canonical_emission_order` (square vertical-seam canonical 8/9/11 → face_a=Side(local), face_b=Side((local+1)%N) with wrap at 11→Side(3)∩Side(0)); **`resolve_round_spec_square_vertical_seam_yields_90_degree_dihedral` — load-bearing regression** (all 4 square vertical-seam edges have `a·b ≈ 0`, matching square's 90° interior angle; pins the regression boundary at the 90° special case); **`resolve_round_spec_pentagon_vertical_seam_yields_108_degree_dihedral` — load-bearing non-90° verification** (all 5 pentagon vertical-seam edges have `a·b ≈ cos(108°) ≈ -0.309`; proves general-dihedral path runs on real non-synthetic Extrude geometry; combined with sub-δ.revisit Loft's 45° pinning this gives two distinct non-90° upstream proof-points); `resolve_round_spec_inward_vectors_unit_for_vertical_seam_across_profile_shapes` (square 90° / triangle 60° / pentagon 108° — unit-length invariant across dihedral spectrum; perpendicularity NOT asserted since vertical-seam dihedrals are profile-angle-dependent NOT 90°-by-construction); **`evaluate_face_strip_localization_no_leak_for_pentagon_extrude_vertical_seam` — load-bearing watch test** (the user-flagged halt condition's empirical guard; scans every upstream-triangle slot post-substitution for pentagon Extrude vertical-seam fillet; asserts target slots flip + ALL other slots byte-identical — adjacent Side(4) shares vertex 0 in its ring quad but is NOT in target face set so its references stay byte-identical, ditto Side(2) for vertex 2, ditto Bottom/Top fan triangles referencing bot_1/top_1). **Helpers added to `extrude.rs`** (parallel to sub-δ.revisit Loft per ADR-119 D5, NOT shared): `extrude_vertical_seam_vertex_pair(i, profile_count) -> (u32, u32)` returns `((i+1)%N, N+(i+1)%N)`; 4 vector primitives `cross3` / `dot3` / `neg3` / `normalize3`; `orient_inward(e_t, n_own, n_other)` + `solve_inward_directions(e_t, n_a, n_b)` — **byte-identical duplicates from `round_fillet/loft.rs`** per ADR-119 D5 substrate-parallelism rationale "intentional duplication so any future Extrude-specific or Loft-specific evolution stays unilateral". **Substrate posture (substrate byte-identical to sub-δ.revisit `cf64962`)**: `RoundFilletOp` struct + `Operator::evaluate` body (sub-β.γ general-dihedral math; UNCHANGED) + `RoundFilletError` enum + `RoundFilletSpec` struct (same 6 fields) + `RoundFilletUpstream` trait + `RoundFilletOp::new_for_extrude` constructor signature byte-identical (only the resolver dispatch within the impl block expanded for vertical-seam acceptance); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs` UNCHANGED (no new `mod` line; extrude.rs already in the module tree); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/cuboid.rs` byte-identical (sub-α); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/revolve.rs` byte-identical (sub-γ); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/loft.rs` byte-identical (sub-δ.revisit); `crates/cad-core/src/topology/{resolve, edge_resolve}.rs` resolver arms byte-identical; `crates/cad-core/src/operators/mod.rs` (`OperatorNode::RoundFillet` variant + `OpKind::RoundFillet` + dispatches test) byte-identical; `crates/cad-core/src/lib.rs` re-exports byte-identical. **Chamfer `FilletOp` byte-identical** per ADR-119 D6 — `fillet/{mod,cuboid,extrude,revolve,loft}.rs` + sub-ε.α/β resolver arms preserved verbatim from `c5c590a` → `7087589` → `d147ba4` → `c0b881a` → `cae3a84` → `cf64962` → THIS DISPATCH (6 round-fillet sub-dispatches landed, chamfer unchanged across all 6). **HARD NON-GOALS observed (zero halt-condition triggers)**: NO `RoundFilletSpec` shape change (would have triggered "vertical-seam spec needs shape changes" halt — current proposal uses only existing 6 fields); NO face-strip substitution leak (the load-bearing watch test pins no-leak empirically — would have triggered "face-strip no-leak watch fails" halt); NO regression in 5 unmodified sub-β tests (zero/neg/non-finite/empty/unknown radius rejects + 3 cap-perimeter accept tests + 4 evaluate tests + 3 helper/resolver tests — all 15 unmodified pass; the 2 flipped + 1 trailing-assertion-flip are the deliberate boundary-shift bookkeeping per user direction; would have triggered "unmodified sub-β or prior upstream tests regress" halt if any of those 15 broke); NO degenerate-guard false-rejection on valid Extrude (`ExtrudeOp::evaluate` enforces strict convexity → no interior angle outside `(0°, 180°)` exclusive → sub-β.γ's `DIHEDRAL_EPSILON_SQ = 1e-6` guard never fires for valid Extrude inputs — would have triggered "degenerate guard rejects valid Extrude seams" halt if it did); NO `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` change; NO `RoundFilletError` variant addition; NO chamfer change (D6); NO change to prior upstream impls (cuboid + revolve + loft byte-identical); NO resolver-arm edit; NO new ADR / ADR addendum; NO new architecture lint; NO new doctrine / §18 companion; one new SPLIT-EXEMPTION annotation on `round_fillet/extrude.rs` for file-size cap (1146 LoC vs 1000-line cap, per PLAN.md §1.3 Rule 3 — same pattern as `round_fillet/mod.rs` at sub-β.γ; file-size compliance, NOT architectural exemption); NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` change; NO `plans/PLAN.md` / `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change; NO `tools/architecture-lints/**` change; NO `unsafe`; NO regression in prior `round_fillet` tests beyond the 3 deliberate flips (sub-α 8 mod.rs tests + 14 cuboid.rs tests + sub-β 16 unchanged extrude.rs tests + sub-γ 18 revolve.rs tests + sub-β.γ 6 general-dihedral tests + sub-δ.revisit 16 loft.rs tests + 2 resolve.rs tests + 2 edge_resolve.rs tests + 1 operators::tests dispatches_round_fillet — total 83 prior round_fillet tests, ALL pass without modification under sub-β.γ-extend lift). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-cad-core` exit 0 (no diff after fmt apply); byte-identical architectural-exemption count from sub-δ.revisit `cf64962` — the new SPLIT-EXEMPTION is file-size-cap compliance per PLAN.md §1.3 Rule 3, NOT an architectural exemption; matches the existing `round_fillet/mod.rs::SPLIT-EXEMPTION` from sub-β.γ at the same line-cap boundary). **Files modified (1 source EDIT + 3 docs)**: `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/extrude.rs` (+~430L net: 1 SPLIT-EXEMPTION header annotation per PLAN.md §1.3 Rule 3; 2026-05-13 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter sub-ζ Commit 1 — RoundFilletSpecKind inert enum-wrapper refactor, IMPLEMENTED): introduces `pub(crate) enum RoundFilletSpecKind { TwoEndpoint(RoundFilletSpec) }` as a behaviorally-inert refactor preparing sub-ζ Commit 2's `Path(RoundFilletPathSpec)` variant + Revolve circular-path support. The 2-endpoint code path stays mechanically stable — workspace count holds at **2534 / 0 / 19** (pre-Commit-1 == post-Commit-1; inert gate passes). **Per user direction at Codex execution-order confirmation**: "Commit 1: inert wrapper only. Add `RoundFilletSpecKind::TwoEndpoint(RoundFilletSpec)` only. Change storage/trait return types to the wrapper. Wrap all existing upstream returns. Add `expect_two_endpoint()` for tests. Verify zero behavior/test-count drift: workspace stays `2534 / 0 / 19`. Commit 1 should not include a dead Path variant or `unreachable!()` arm. One-variant enum first is cleaner and makes the inert gate sharper." Executed exactly that scope; zero halt-condition triggers. **Wrapper introduction load-bearing details**: (1) Added `pub(crate) enum RoundFilletSpecKind` with ONE variant `TwoEndpoint(RoundFilletSpec)` — Commit 2 will add `Path(...)` and Rust's exhaustive-match requirement will force the `match` in `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` to grow a `Path` arm explicitly. (2) Added `#[cfg(test)] pub(crate) fn RoundFilletSpecKind::expect_two_endpoint(&self) -> &RoundFilletSpec` accessor for test sites that unwrap the variant before field access. (3) Changed `RoundFilletUpstream::resolve_round_spec` return type from `Result<RoundFilletSpec, ...>` to `Result<RoundFilletSpecKind, ...>`. (4) Changed `RoundFilletOp::round_specs` element type from `Vec<RoundFilletSpec>` to `Vec<RoundFilletSpecKind>`. (5) Wrapped `RoundFilletOp::evaluate`'s for-loop body in `for spec_kind in &self.round_specs { let spec = match spec_kind { RoundFilletSpecKind::TwoEndpoint(spec) => spec, }; /* 110-line body byte-identical inside arm */ }`. (6) Wrapped 9 per-upstream `Ok(RoundFilletSpec { ... })` returns in `::TwoEndpoint(...)`: cuboid.rs (1 via trait impl `.map(::TwoEndpoint)`), extrude.rs (3 dispatch branches: bottom/top/vertical-seam), loft.rs (3 dispatch branches), revolve.rs (2 cap-side branches). (7) Wrapped `make_synthetic_op` test helper's `vec![RoundFilletSpec { ... }]` in `vec![::TwoEndpoint(RoundFilletSpec { ... })]`. (8) Updated ~25 test direct-call sites of `resolve_round_spec` to shadow the binding via `let spec = spec.expect_two_endpoint();` before field access — test SEMANTICS preserved; assertion STRUCTURE preserved byte-identical. **Resolver arms** (`topology/{resolve, edge_resolve}.rs` `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)`) UNTOUCHED — pattern uses `_` ignore and never bound the inner value; the enum-wrapper is invisible to the resolver layer (verified pre-execution at sub-ζ inspection-first phase). **Substrate posture (substrate byte-identical inside the TwoEndpoint arm)**: `RoundFilletSpec` struct (the 2-endpoint shape; 6 fields) is byte-identical to sub-β.γ-extend `978f507`. `RoundFilletError` enum byte-identical. `RoundFilletOp` struct shape preserved (only the `round_specs` field's element type changed). `RoundFilletOp::evaluate`'s body INSIDE the TwoEndpoint arm is byte-identical to sub-β.γ-extend — same 110 lines of general-dihedral cylinder math + face-strip substitution + cylinder triangle emission. Only the for-loop wrapper added the `match` dispatch. **Chamfer `FilletOp` byte-identical** per ADR-119 D6. **HARD NON-GOALS observed (zero halt-condition triggers across the wrapper introduction)**: NO `RoundFilletPathSpec` yet (deferred to Commit 2); NO `::Path` variant (deferred); NO `unreachable!()` / `todo!()` placeholder arms (per user direction "Commit 1 should not include a dead Path variant or unreachable!() arm"); NO Revolve side-side rejection lift (deferred to Commit 2); NO new tests added at Commit 1 (would shift the inert workspace count — load-bearing for the inert-gate proof); NO behavior change observable to any existing test; NO resolver-arm edit; NO chamfer change; NO ADR change; NO new architecture lint / exemption / doctrine / §18 companion; NO `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` change; NO `plans/PLAN.md` / `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` change; NO `unsafe`. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-cad-core` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-cad-core` clean; `cargo test -p rge-cad-core --lib --no-fail-fast` = 459 unit tests passed / 0 failed (UNCHANGED from sub-β.γ-extend `978f507` — wrapper introduction is behaviorally inert); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2534 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (UNCHANGED — exact match to pre-Commit-1; **load-bearing inert gate passes**); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS; byte-identical architectural-exemption count from sub-β.γ-extend `978f507`). Files modified (5 source EDIT + 0 docs at Commit 1 — docs land at Commit 2 with the full sub-ζ scope): `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs` (+~60L net: `RoundFilletSpecKind` enum + `expect_two_endpoint()` accessor + trait return-type change + `round_specs` field-type change + `evaluate` match wrap + `make_synthetic_op` wrap); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/cuboid.rs` (trait impl wraps free fn return via `.map(::TwoEndpoint)`); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/extrude.rs` (3 dispatch-branch wraps + 6 test-site shadow-binds); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/loft.rs` (3 dispatch-branch wraps + 9 test-site shadow-binds); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/revolve.rs` (2 cap-side dispatch wraps + 5 test-site shadow-binds). **D-Fillet chapter status**: sub-α + sub-β + sub-γ + sub-β.γ + sub-δ.revisit + sub-β.γ-extend CLOSED; sub-ζ Commit 1 (inert wrapper) CLOSED. **NEXT**: sub-ζ Commit 2 — add `RoundFilletPathSpec` struct + `::Path` variant + Path branch in `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` + lift Revolve side-side circular-path rejections + add multi-segment-path tests.

2026-05-13 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter sub-ζ Commit 2 — RoundFilletPathSpec + Revolve circular-path side-side acceptance, IMPLEMENTED): adds `RoundFilletPathSpec` struct + `::Path(RoundFilletPathSpec)` variant to `RoundFilletSpecKind` + multi-segment swept-cylinder Path branch in `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` + lifts Revolve full-mode AND partial-mode side-side rejections to Path-spec acceptance. **Completes Revolve coverage** — all `N` full-mode edges (closed loops) + all `3N` partial-mode edges (cap-perimeter + cap-side + side-side open arcs) now accept under the round-fillet substrate. **Per user direction at green-light** (after Codex execution-order confirmation): "Then proceed" — covered the full 2-commit plan. Executed exactly that scope; zero halt-condition triggers. **Path spec geometric model**: For each path-ring r along the seam, position `pos_r = r * N + (i+1)%N`, with per-ring inward directions `path_face_a_inwards[r]` (rotated Side(i) outward normal around Y-axis to θ_r) and `path_face_b_inwards[r]` (similarly for Side((i+1)%N)). Edge tangent at ring r is the swept tangent `(-sin(θ_r), 0, cos(θ_r))`. The dihedral angle along the path is CONSTANT (equals the profile's interior polygon angle at the shared vertex), but the inward DIRECTIONS rotate around Y-axis with θ — hence per-ring storage. For partial mode `θ_r = r * angle / segments`, M+1 ring positions, `closed_loop = false`. For full mode `θ_r = r * 2π / segments`, M = segments ring positions (no separate closing ring; index wraps via `(r+1) % M`), `closed_loop = true`. **Path branch in `RoundFilletOp::evaluate`** (~140 new LoC inside the match arm): for each ring, run sub-β.γ general-dihedral math (inset scale + axis_center + cross-section vertices); face-strip multi-ring substitution scans Side(i) and Side((i+1)%N) triangles for path-vertex occurrences and substitutes inset indices keyed by ring; cylinder cross-section stitching connects ring r to ring r+1 (or wraps r=M-1 to r=0 for closed loops) with 2N triangles per ring-step. Per-edge contribution: 2*(M+1) inset verts + (N+1)*(M+1) cross-section verts + 2*M*N cylinder triangles for partial; 2*M + (N+1)*M + 2*M*N for full. **Revolve side-side rejection lift**: `revolve.rs::resolve_round_spec` partial mode `[0, N)` and full mode all edges now route to `revolve_side_side_path_spec(...)` returning `::Path(RoundFilletPathSpec)`; previously both rejected with `UnsupportedEdgeGeometry`. **Three reject-test flips + 2 trailing-assertion flips** (deliberate boundary-shift bookkeeping mirroring sub-β.γ-extend's 3 reject-test flips at `978f507`): `new_for_revolve_full_mode_all_edges_unsupported` → `new_for_revolve_full_mode_all_edges_accept_as_closed_loop_path`; `new_for_revolve_partial_mode_side_side_unsupported` → `new_for_revolve_partial_mode_side_side_accepts_as_open_arc_path`; `new_for_revolve_partial_mode_mixed_selection_rejects_first_unsupported` → `new_for_revolve_partial_mode_mixed_selection_accepts_post_sub_zeta`. Plus 2 trailing-assertion flips inside `resolve_round_spec_face_ids_match_canonical_emission_order` (canonical 2 Err → Path accept with face IDs) and `resolve_round_spec_inward_vectors_unit_and_perpendicular_for_cap_side`'s side-side loop (`is_err()` → Path accept + unit-length per-ring check). **Eight new Path-branch tests** pinning sub-ζ Commit 2 invariants: `evaluate_one_partial_side_side_edge_produces_expected_counts` (load-bearing — proves Path branch runs end-to-end; +99v/+128t for ring profile, segments=8, angle=π/2); `evaluate_one_full_mode_side_side_edge_produces_expected_counts_closed_loop` (full-mode +88v/+128t — closed-loop wrap); `resolve_round_spec_partial_side_side_path_face_ids` (Side(i) ∩ Side((i+1)%N) with wrap at last edge); `resolve_round_spec_full_mode_side_side_path_is_closed_loop_with_segments_rings` (closed_loop=true; M=segments rings); **`resolve_round_spec_square_partial_side_side_yields_90_degree_dihedral_per_ring` — load-bearing regression** (square profile interior=90°, every ring's `a·b ≈ 0`); **`resolve_round_spec_pentagon_partial_side_side_yields_consistent_dihedral_per_ring` — load-bearing non-90° proof + dihedral-constancy** (pentagon interior is non-90°; verifies the constant-dihedral-along-path invariant — combined with sub-δ.revisit Loft 45° + sub-β.γ-extend Extrude pentagon 108°, the chapter now has THREE non-90° proof-points spanning BOTH the TwoEndpoint AND Path branches); `resolve_round_spec_partial_side_side_path_vertices_match_revolve_layout` (square segments=8: M+1=9 path vertices `[1, 5, 9, ..., 33]`); `resolve_round_spec_full_mode_side_side_path_vertices_match_full_path_layout` (square segments=8 full: M=8 path vertices `[1, 5, 9, ..., 29]`). **Substrate posture**: `RoundFilletOp` struct + `RoundFilletSpec` struct + `RoundFilletError` enum + `RoundFilletUpstream` trait signature byte-identical from sub-ζ Commit 1 `1052a3d` (only `RoundFilletSpecKind` enum grew the `Path` variant + `RoundFilletOp::evaluate`'s match arm grew the `Path` body); `round_fillet/{cuboid, extrude, loft}.rs` byte-identical (no per-upstream impl edits for non-Revolve operators); `round_fillet/revolve.rs` updated with side-side Path-spec construction + 3 reject flips + 2 trailing flips + 8 new tests + new vector primitives + `orient_inward` / `solve_inward_directions` helpers (duplicated from loft.rs per ADR-119 D5); `topology/{resolve, edge_resolve}.rs` resolver arms byte-identical; `operators/mod.rs` + `lib.rs` byte-identical. **Chamfer `FilletOp` byte-identical** per ADR-119 D6. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO `RoundFilletSpec` shape change (Path spec is a SEPARATE struct alongside, not a modification); NO `RoundFilletError` variant addition; NO `evaluate` 2-endpoint code path change (the TwoEndpoint arm of the match is byte-identical from sub-β.γ-extend `978f507`); NO resolver-arm edit; NO change to prior upstream impls beyond revolve.rs; NO chamfer change (D6); NO new ADR / lint / exemption / doctrine / §18 companion; one new SPLIT-EXEMPTION on `revolve.rs` for 1000-line file-size cap (1287 LoC, per PLAN.md §1.3 Rule 3 — same pattern as `mod.rs::SPLIT-EXEMPTION` at sub-β.γ + `extrude.rs::SPLIT-EXEMPTION` at sub-β.γ-extend); NO Cargo change; NO PLAN/IMPLEMENTATION change; NO `unsafe`; NO regression in sub-α/β/γ/β.γ/δ.revisit/β.γ-extend + sub-ζ-Commit-1 tests (all 459 pre-Commit-2 tests pass; the 3 flipped + 2 trailing-flipped tests are deliberate boundary-shift bookkeeping). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-cad-core` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-cad-core` clean (2.60s); `cargo test -p rge-cad-core --lib --no-fail-fast` = 467 unit tests passed / 0 failed (was 459 at sub-ζ Commit 1; +8 net for sub-ζ Commit 2 — 8 new Path-branch tests; the 3 flipped + 2 trailing-flipped tests stay in the test count); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2542 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored** (was 2534; +8 net — exact target; all 8 new tests in `operators::round_fillet::revolve::tests`); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS after SPLIT-EXEMPTION; byte-identical architectural-exemption count from sub-β.γ-extend `978f507`). Files modified (1 source EDIT mod.rs + 1 source EDIT revolve.rs + 3 docs): `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs` (+~150L: `RoundFilletPathSpec` struct + `Path` variant + `expect_path()` accessor + Path branch in `evaluate`); `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/revolve.rs` (+~290L: SPLIT-EXEMPTION header + side-side dispatch lift + `revolve_side_side_path_spec` helper + vector primitives + `orient_inward` / `solve_inward_directions` + 3 reject-test flips + 2 trailing-assertion flips + 8 new Path-branch tests); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **D-Fillet chapter status**: sub-α + sub-β + sub-γ + sub-β.γ + sub-δ.revisit + sub-β.γ-extend + sub-ζ Commit 1 + sub-ζ Commit 2 CLOSED; ADR-119 D1-D6 proven across ALL FOUR upstreams with **COMPLETE edge coverage**: Cuboid full (12 edges) + Extrude full (3N edges; sub-β cap-perimeter + sub-β.γ-extend vertical-seam) + Revolve **full** (cap-side TwoEndpoint via sub-γ + side-side Path via sub-ζ Commit 2; both partial-mode open arcs AND full-mode closed loops) + Loft full (3N edges; sub-δ.revisit). Shared evaluate-body math supports BOTH 2-endpoint general-dihedral AND multi-segment swept-cylinder geometry. **NEXT** per D7 chapter shape — only ONE sub-dispatch remains: sub-ε multi-edge corner blending (torus-patch geometry at corners where 2+ filleted edges meet; cross-cutting; ADR D8 scope — genuinely broader shape change since it modifies how multiple selected edges INTERACT at shared corners rather than treating each edge independently). Cadence and ordering TBD by user.
2026-05-13 (Phase 7 D-Fillet real round geometry chapter sub-epsilon - multi-edge corner blending, IMPLEMENTED): closes the final ADR-119 D8 sub-dispatch after sub-zeta. `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` now defers face-strip substitution until all selected specs have emitted candidate insets, records candidate replacements per `(TopologyFaceId, original vertex)`, resolves shared face-vertex collisions to one face-corner inset, and appends nameless `TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE` corner-patch fan geometry for vertices touched by 2+ selected `TwoEndpoint` / open `Path` specs. This lifts the old corner-sharing "visually weird but topologically valid" posture while preserving the v0 identity boundary. Coverage added across all four upstream families: Cuboid two corner-sharing edges (including the shared NegZ inset pinned at `[-0.4, -0.4, -0.5]`), Extrude bottom-perimeter + vertical-seam, Loft bottom-perimeter + vertical-seam, and Revolve mixed cap-side `TwoEndpoint` + side-side `Path`. Source files modified: `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs`, `cuboid.rs`, `extrude.rs`, `loft.rs`, `revolve.rs`; docs modified: `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`. Hard non-goals observed: no `RoundFilletSpec` / `RoundFilletPathSpec` shape change, no `RoundFilletError` variant, no resolver-arm edit, no chamfer edit, no Cargo change, no new ADR/lint/doctrine, no analytic/G2 torus patch, no variable-radius/setback solver, no cap/corner-patch `BRepFaceId`. Verification gates all green: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-cad-core` exit 0; `git diff --check` exit 0; `cargo build -p rge-cad-core` exit 0; `cargo test -p rge-cad-core --lib --no-fail-fast` = 471 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored (+4); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` exit 0 = 2546 passed / 0 failed / 19 ignored (+4 net from 2542); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS). D-Fillet chapter status: sub-alpha + sub-beta + sub-gamma + sub-beta.gamma + sub-delta.revisit + sub-beta.gamma-extend + sub-zeta Commit 1 + sub-zeta Commit 2 + sub-epsilon CLOSED; ADR-119 D1-D8 complete. 2026-05-14 (Phase 6 §6.3 Gate A post-depth measurement — CLOSED on recorder host + gfx-level synthetic harness via additive `#[ignore]` release test, IMPLEMENTED via the new `ai_handoffs/` AI-handoff-protocol multi-role chain): closes the post-depth Gate A measurement gap documented at `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md:468` and `plans/BASELINE.md` §6.3 since sub-γ docs-only acknowledgment 2026-05-12. **Per `MAIN-RENDER-POSTDEPTH-GATEA-001` dispatch through `ai_handoffs/`**: Planner / OpenAI Codex issued TASK 2026-05-13_18-12-00 + CORRECTION 2026-05-13_18-23-00 (narrowly opens `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` for status-marker wording only); Reviewer1 / OpenAI Codex APPROVED twice (initial + post-correction); Reviewer2 / Anthropic Claude APPROVED (re-affirmed scope, flagged latent risks); Executor / Anthropic Claude implemented + measured + updated docs. **New harness**: `crates/gfx/tests/gate_a_simple_scene_depth_60fps.rs` — additive, release-only, `#[ignore]`, GPU-dependent integration test that mirrors the pre-depth Gate A harness (`gate_a_simple_scene_60fps.rs`) byte-for-byte in methodology (1000 cubes / 10×10×10 grid / static camera Z=-40 / 1280×720 / 60 warmup + 600 sample / 3 runs / P95 ≤ 16.67 ms / variance ≤ 30%) but switches the pipeline construction to `LitMeshPipeline::new_with_depth(.., Some(DepthStateKey::new(wgpu::TextureFormat::Depth24Plus, false, wgpu::CompareFunction::LessEqual)))` (sub-α API, matches editor-shell production sub-β EXACTLY) and the recording call to `record_lit_mesh_pass(.., Some(&depth_view))` over a per-frame `Depth24Plus` depth texture allocated once and reused (via `wgpu::TextureDescriptor` + `wgpu::TextureUsages::RENDER_ATTACHMENT`; not via `TexturePool` / `FrameGraph` substrate — that's editor-shell production plumbing tested separately by `frame_graph_*` tests). **Recorder-host run** (NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Ti / Vulkan / DiscreteGpu / Windows): run 0 P50=0.084 ms P95=0.125 ms max=0.866 ms; run 1 P50=0.085 ms P95=0.122 ms max=1.996 ms; run 2 P50=0.089 ms P95=0.122 ms max=1.294 ms → **min-of-3 P95 = 0.122 ms** (median P95 = 0.122 ms, max P95 = 0.125 ms, median P50 = 0.085 ms, worst frame across all 1800 sample frames = 1.996 ms, variance across runs = **2.6%** well under the 30% gate). About **+9% slower than pre-depth** (0.122 ms vs 0.112 ms min-P95) — the measured cost of the depth attachment + the depth-write-disabled compare-on-read pipeline state. Still ~137× under the 16.67 ms gate (compared to ~150× for pre-depth). **Substrate posture**: zero non-test `crates/gfx/src/` edits; `record_lit_mesh_pass` already handles `Option<&wgpu::TextureView>` correctly (LoadOp::Clear(1.0), StoreOp::Store, no stencil) — only the optional arg flipped from `None` to `Some(&depth_view)`; `LitMeshPipeline::new_with_depth` already existed via sub-α; no changes to `LitMeshPipeline`, `record_lit_mesh_pass`, `TexturePool`, `FrameGraph`, `ResourceMap`, or PSO cache behavior; no production source change anywhere. **Docs updated** per TASK + CORRECTION: `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md:468` replaces the "POST-DEPTH PRODUCTION-PATH MEASUREMENT GAP" wording with the new "POST-DEPTH GATE A — CLOSED" entry (pre-depth CLOSED entry untouched alongside); `plans/BASELINE.md` §6.3 replaces the "Post-sub-β measurement gap" paragraph with the new "Post-depth Gate A — CLOSED 2026-05-14" paragraph (pre-depth measurement remains valid for the no-depth gfx path; the new entry adds the post-depth measurement; scope language preserved); `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` prepended with the new snapshot. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO production gfx source edits; NO editor-shell-end-to-end harness (deferred per CORRECTION packet); NO visual-fidelity broadening (`MAIN-RENDER-VISUAL-FIDELITY-002` is the queued Turn 2 dispatch); NO non-winit editor-shell perf harness (still deferred); NO PLAN target retargeting; NO new ADR / new lint / new exemption / new doctrine / new sec-18 companion; NO Cargo change; NO `unsafe`; NO architectural-lint exemption count shift. **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-gfx` exit 0; `cargo test -p rge-gfx --release --test gate_a_simple_scene_depth_60fps -- --ignored --nocapture` exit 0 (real GPU run, 1 passed / 0 failed in 1.33s); `cargo test -p rge-gfx --test lit_mesh_depth_overlay_smoke` exit 0 (1 passed); `cargo test -p rge-gfx --test frame_graph_umbrella_smoke` exit 0 (1 passed); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2549 passed / 0 failed / 20 ignored** (was 2549/0/19 at sub-ε hardening `889da8a`; 2026-05-14 (Phase 6 §6.3 visual-fidelity Turn 2 — `lit_mesh_depth_overlay_smoke` multi-sample broadening, IMPLEMENTED via the `ai_handoffs/` AI-handoff-protocol multi-role chain): per `MAIN-RENDER-VISUAL-FIDELITY-002` dispatch after `MAIN-RENDER-POSTDEPTH-GATEA-001` closeout (commit `03d3f05`). Broadens the existing Phase 6 sub-β visual harness `crates/gfx/tests/lit_mesh_depth_overlay_smoke.rs` from 1 sample point per region to 3 sample points per region (9 total assertions), addressing the documented weakness that single-point sampling can miss diagonal-boundary leakage and region inversion. **Geometry, depth state, and color thresholds unchanged**: same synthetic 1×1×1 cuboid + single-triangle orange overlay covering the lower-right half of the +Z face; same `DepthStateKey::new(Depth24Plus, depth_write_enabled=false, LessEqual)` matching editor-shell production sub-β; same per-pixel `r > 80` / `b < 80` (overlay), `r > 80` / `b > 80` (cuboid-only), `r+g+b < 30` (background) thresholds. **Only the sample count grows**: from `(40,40)` / `(24,24)` / `(4,4)` (one each) to overlay `(40,40)` + `(44,24)` + `(38,44)`, cuboid-only `(24,24)` + `(20,30)` + `(30,20)`, background `(4,4)` + `(60,4)` + `(4,60)` (three each). All samples chosen well inside their respective regions (margin ≥ 4 from any boundary; specifically the diagonal `x + y = 64`). **New top-level helpers** added to the test file: `assert_overlay_pixel`, `assert_cuboid_only_pixel`, `assert_background_pixel` — small fns local to the test file mirroring the existing `ctx_or_skip` + `unit_cuboid_render_mesh` precedent; **no broad shared test utility module** per the TASK's explicit `Constraints / Non-Goals`. **Substrate posture**: zero `crates/gfx/src/**` edits; zero edits to any other crate's source; zero changes to `LitMeshPipeline`, `record_lit_mesh_pass`, `Material`, `Camera`, `DirectionalLight`, `HeadlessTarget`, `ReadbackBuffer`, or any other production API; zero new test files. **HARD NON-GOALS observed**: NO production code changes; NO performance claims added (per TASK); NO editor-shell end-to-end claim; NO `plans/BASELINE.md` edits (per TASK; the post-depth Gate A perf measurement landed at commit `03d3f05` separately); NO `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` edits; NO new ADR / new lint / new exemption / new doctrine / new sec-18 companion; NO Cargo change; NO `unsafe`; NO architectural-lint exemption count shift; NO second-camera-pose harness (TASK explicit non-goal). **Verification gates ALL GREEN**: `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-gfx` exit 0; `cargo test -p rge-gfx --test lit_mesh_depth_overlay_smoke -- --nocapture` exit 0 (1 passed in 0.70s with all 9 broadened samples); `cargo test -p rge-gfx --test render_mesh_smoke -- --nocapture` exit 0 (1 passed; companion regression preserved); `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` = **2549 passed / 0 failed / 20 ignored** (unchanged from GATEA-001 closeout `03d3f05` — broadening sample points doesn't add or remove tests, just strengthens existing assertions); `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exit 0 (9 enforcement + 1 supplementary PASS; byte-identical exemption count); `git diff --check` exit 0. Files modified (4 total): `crates/gfx/tests/lit_mesh_depth_overlay_smoke.rs` (test-only, +helpers + multi-sample), `Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + this `change.md` entry. **Second real substrate-level dispatch through the `ai_handoffs/` AI-handoff protocol** (Planner OpenAI → Reviewer1 OpenAI → Reviewer2 Claude → Executor Claude); no correction round needed (the Turn-2 dependency on Turn-1 closeout was correctly held by Claude until Codex's `MAIN-RENDER-POSTDEPTH-GATEA-001_CLOSEOUT_2026-05-14_02-31-01` landed, validating the protocol's queued-dispatch dependency-resolution path end-to-end).
