zero `egui_winit::State` adapter usage; zero `egui_wgpu::Renderer` integration; `editor-shell/Cargo.toml` declares no egui dep of any kind; `editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml` declares no egui dep of any kind; workspace `Cargo.toml` does pin `egui = "0.34"` / `egui-winit = "0.34"` / `egui-wgpu = "0.34"` / `egui_dock = "0.19"` but only `editor-ui` consumes `egui` + `egui_dock` (and only as `&mut egui::Ui` widget-substrate consumer), while `egui-winit` and `egui-wgpu` are workspace-declared-but-referenced-by-no-crate; `editor-shell::render_path::render_frame_to_target` (lines 471-582) ends at `gfx_ctx.queue().submit()` with no post-cuboid UI pass; `lifecycle/mod.rs::WindowEvent::RedrawRequested` ticks game systems, acquires the render-input snapshot, and calls `render_frame()` with no UI step between or after. **The egui-stripping was deliberate, not a stub**: `lifecycle/mod.rs:18` documents verbatim that *"The original rustforge file pulls in wgpu device/queue/pipeline state and an egui overlay; W03 strips those out (gfx wave W21+ owns wgpu) and keeps only the lifecycle skeleton + PIE plumbing,"* and `:810` confirms *"egui-overlay routing + IR-rebuild + close-persist stripped."* These are historical markers indicating the W03 refactor consciously deferred host integration to a later wave. **The inspector widget I landed in `1d4ddbc` is callable from nothing today** — `widgets::inspector::ui(&InspectorSnapshot, &mut egui::Ui)` requires a `&mut egui::Ui` scope, and no production code obtains one. Status: **NOT READY for live inspector-tab wiring**, defer until the egui host materializes. **Four snapshot-delivery options compared in the recorded section** for the future host-design dispatch (academic until host exists): (A) captured `Arc<dyn Fn() -> InspectorSnapshot + Send + Sync>` closure (blocked today by `EditorShell`'s non-`Sync` ownership of winit/wgpu state); (B) `Arc<RwLock<InspectorSnapshot>>` shared slot (pragmatic if single-threaded host suffices); (C) handoff-style substrate mirroring `editor-shell::render_input::RenderHandoff` per ADR-117 with `Mutex<Option<Arc<T>>>` + `AtomicU64` generation counter (right answer if editor grows toward dedicated render thread; precedent exists); (D) static snapshot embedded in tab body (toy demo only, stale-by-construction, collapses to A/B/C the moment refresh is required). Recommendation for the eventual host design: Option C. **Explicit rejections codified in the recorded section** — what NOT to dispatch next: (1) NOT an `InspectorTabBody { snapshot }` wrapper (Option D scaffolding for non-existent host); (2) NOT a snapshot-delivery substrate before the host exists (host design dictates the right one); (3) NOT egui-* deps added to editor-shell or rge-editor today (doctrine-significant decision belonging in host preflight); (4) NOT replacing `"tab/inspector"`'s `PlaceholderTabBody` with a stub returning `Default::default()` snapshots (sham progress); (5) NOT a `ShowInspector` menu Command variant (the 23 existing variants have no menu handlers wired); (6) NOT an egui-rendering test pre-host (locks in design choices not yet made). **Recommended next dispatch**: read-only `egui host integration preflight` scoping (1) where the host lives — `editor-shell` extension vs new `crates/editor-egui-host` between editor-shell and editor-ui vs `editor/rge-editor` binary-only host; (2) input-routing semantics — how `egui_winit::State::on_window_event` interacts with the existing Phase 9 `EditorKeyCommand` keyboard branch (Ctrl+Z/Y/S); (3) render-pass composition — same encoder vs separate submit, depth-buffer interaction, queue-ordering with cuboid + highlight overlay; (4) DockState ownership and TabBody enum shape; (5) snapshot delivery mechanism selection per host architecture; (6) dep-edge / cycle / forbidden-dep / editor-state-ownership lint implications; (7) test strategy (egui has render-to-pixels but egui-wgpu does not); (8) `resumed`-callback timing for `egui_winit::State` + `egui_wgpu::Renderer` instantiation. **Two-arm revisit trigger** codified: re-run this preflight when **either** (a) the egui host integration preflight lands a host-design decision, **or** (b) a non-inspector consumer of the egui host materializes first (menu-bar / viewport gizmo / status-bar dirty indicator), at which point inspector wiring becomes incremental rather than substrate-defining. **Scope envelope strictly honored**: no source / test / Cargo / lint / ADR / automation / GitHub-issue edits; `tools/` untouched; no new lint; no automation cap raise; `ai-auto` cap unchanged at 80; cron still stopped. The 11 headless tests in `editor-shell/tests/inspector_snapshot_smoke.rs` + 14 in `editor-ui/tests/inspector_widget_smoke.rs` continue to pin the producer + formatter + render-fn contracts (not theater — they pin the API surface so the eventual host can consume them with confidence). Verification: `git diff --check` exit 0; `git diff --stat` shows only `plans/BASELINE.md` and `change.md` modified; the 9+1 architecture lint gate is markdown-agnostic and was not re-run since no source / Cargo edits occurred. Files modified (2 total, both docs): `plans/BASELINE.md` (new `Live-inspector wiring preflight (Phase 9)` section appended, ~135 lines) and `change.md` (this entry).

2026-05-21 (Phase 9 editor-usability preflight — read-only audit of the editor's user-facing surface recorded in `plans/BASELINE.md`, IMPLEMENTED): docs-only, single primary file (`plans/BASELINE.md`) + this change-log entry. Appends a new `## Editor-usability preflight (Phase 9)` section directly under the §1.1 reflection-scale preflight that landed in commit `f308ce2`. Audits what a user can actually do today in the `rge-editor` binary against IMPLEMENTATION.md §9's "Editor usability — friction points from real authoring" axis, using read-only inspection of `editor-shell` / `editor-ui` / `editor-actions` / `editor-state` / `components-editor` / `anim-graph-editor` / `material-graph-editor` plus grep over file-extension string literals, `CommandBus::submit` call sites, `KeyboardInput` event branches, and `io-gltf` / `io-image` / `io-3mf` public-API call sites. **Headline findings**: (a) the `rge-editor` binary entry point is `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs:38-96` (one hardcoded `CuboidOp(1.0, 1.0, 1.0)` constructed and rendered via `EditorShell::with_world_projection_graph`); (b) 8 workflows work end-to-end today — launch + render hardcoded cuboid, mouse cursor tracking, left-click face picking + orange highlight overlay (sub-ε), Play/Stop/Pause/Step PIE byte-identical-snapshot, editor-coord state persists across Play/Stop, workspace layout RON persistence (egui_dock), render handoff latest-only, time-scale dilation — all with file:line citations; (c) **312 `#[test]` annotations across `editor-*` crates** (74 integration / 238 unit) cover the *internals* well but have **no coverage** of the full UI event loop (mouse clicks / keyboard input through `window_event`), WASM script reload via editor, multi-entity scene complexity, or fillet/loft operator-graph UX flow. **Top 3 usability gaps recorded**: (1) **no scene/project persistence** — zero `open_file` / `load_project` / `save_project` symbols, zero `.rge` / `.rgeproj` / `.scene` / `.project` extension matches, `rge-data` RON serializer never called by editor; the authoring loop is structurally unmeasurable because there is no loop to friction-test; (2) **Command Bus is fully implemented but structurally unreachable from the editor UI** — `crates/editor-actions/src/bus.rs:86,143` defines `CommandBus::submit` with full `UndoStack` + 500ms-coalesce + audit-ledger plumbing and 23 unit tests, but zero call sites from `editor-shell` / `rge-editor` dispatch any user-triggered command through the bus, and `lifecycle.rs:776` catch-all `_ => {}` silently swallows every `KeyboardInput` event; (3) **`MenuRegistry` + `io-*` asset loaders both ready internally but never called from the editor** — `MenuRegistry::resolve` produces `ResolvedEntry` values that no editor-shell code acts on, `io-gltf::import_glb` / `io-image::load_path` / `io-image::load_bytes` have zero call sites from the editor surface, no drag-drop, no CLI file arg. Cross-cutting pattern: substrate-first architecture has worked but no user-input path connects any closed subsystem to a visible scene change; the editor today is a rendering + picking testbed with battle-tested internals nobody can drive from the UI. **Rejected/boundedness note for the F → `SpawnCuboidAt` proposal**: a candidate follow-up to wire `F` → `Command::SpawnCuboidAt(Vec3)` through `CommandBus::submit` → spawn a second cuboid + `Ctrl+Z` → `CommandBus::undo()` with a headless test was considered and **rejected as not bounded**. Verified `CommandBus::submit(action: Box<dyn Action>, world: &mut World)` at `bus.rs:143`, `Action::apply(&self, world: &mut World)` at `action.rs:87`, `Action::revert(&self, world: &mut World)` at `action.rs:96` — the `Action` trait is **World-only** and has no access to `CadGraph` / `CadProjection` / editor-shell render projection state. A visible CAD-cuboid spawn requires mutating `CadGraph` and producing a fresh `CadProjection` snapshot, neither of which lives in `World`; the current cuboid is constructed in `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs:47-68` against standalone `CadGraph` + `CadProjection` instances handed to `EditorShell` once at construction time via `with_world_projection_graph`, and the render path is explicitly single-cuboid / sub-δ scope. Landing F → `SpawnCuboidAt` as a "small" dispatch would force either a CommandBus context redesign (changing the World-only invariant on which 23 `editor-actions` tests rely) or a parallel "editor command" channel that forks the architecture and the audit-ledger story. **Defer code until a separate CommandBus integration design dispatch decides whether the bus stays `(&mut World)`-only or grows a richer context** (e.g. `(&mut World, &mut CadGraph, &mut CadProjection)` or a typed `&mut EditorCommandCtx`), and what the corresponding `Action::apply` / `revert` signature looks like — that design dispatch can itself be docs-only (ADR / design note plus a stub adapter); it does not have to land the full multi-context bus. **Two-arm revisit trigger** codified: re-run this preflight when **either** (a) a CommandBus integration design dispatch lands a decision on the bus's command-context shape, **or** (b) a non-CAD user-input path lands first (e.g. `Ctrl+S` / `Ctrl+O` bound to the working workspace-layout RON load/save substrate per `workspace_round_trip.rs`, or PIE Play/Stop bound to a keyboard shortcut) and forces the catch-all at `lifecycle.rs:776` to grow a real `KeyboardInput` branch without first redesigning the bus. Until **at least one** of those fires, **defer all user-facing editor wire-up dispatches**. Also rejected for the same architectural-cost reason: `Ctrl+S` workspace-RON-only (ships a half-loop with no `Ctrl+O` partner); `--load <gltf-path>` CLI arg (foreign-mesh-into-editor ingestion is unexercised and the projection cache assumes `CadGraph`-owned meshes); `MenuRegistry::resolve` to a no-op handler (provides nothing user-visible). **Scope envelope strictly honored**: no source / test / Cargo / lint / ADR / automation / pilot-type / GitHub-issue edits; `tools/` untouched; no new lint; no automation cap raise; `ai-auto` cap unchanged at 80; cron still stopped. Verification: `git diff --check` exit 0; `git diff --stat` shows only `plans/BASELINE.md` and `change.md` modified; the 9+1 architecture lint gate is markdown-agnostic and was not re-run since no source / Cargo edits occurred. Files modified (2 total, both docs): `plans/BASELINE.md` (new `Editor-usability preflight (Phase 9)` section appended, ~110 lines) and `change.md` (this entry). Local main goes from 0 ahead of origin (post-push of `f308ce2`) to 1 ahead with this commit; push remains explicitly human-gated.

2026-05-21 (Phase 9 §1.1 reflection-scale honesty preflight — read-only adoption-state audit recorded in `plans/BASELINE.md`, IMPLEMENTED): docs-only, single primary file (`plans/BASELINE.md`) + this change-log entry. Appends a new `## §1.1 Reflection-scale honesty preflight (Phase 9)` section directly under the §1.10.4 invalidation-radius preflight that landed in commit `16147ca`. Audits current production-vs-test adoption of the reflection substrate (`kernel/types` + `crates/macros-reflect`) via grep over all workspace `*.rs`. Links the existing `kernel/types/BUDGET.md` (taken 2026-05-05) as the source of truth for Phase 1.1 compile-budget numbers (5-pilot wall-clock 7.5 s, ~23 LLVM lines/field, ~9,000-line 100-type extrapolation, all PASS) — explicitly does NOT duplicate those numbers; the BUDGET doc remains canonical. **Headline findings**: (a) **kernel/types is real substrate but not load-bearing in production yet** — 1,151 source LoC across 7 files, complete `Reflect` trait + `TypeId` + `FieldDescriptor` + `SchemaVersion` + `UiHint` + RON serde-bridge surface, hand-rolled FNV-1a-128 TypeId hash, no `inventory` / `linkme` / global registry; (b) **7 reflected types in the workspace today, all 7 test-only** (5 anonymous `Pilot1..5` calibration probes in `crates/macros-reflect/tests/compile_budget_5_pilots.rs` + `RenderPass` + `WithValidate` fixtures in `crates/macros-reflect/tests/fixtures/render_pass.rs`); (c) **0 production reflected types**; (d) **0 production consumers of `rge-macros-reflect`** — the proc-macro is imported only in macros-reflect's own tests + its own `lib.rs` doc example; (e) **0 production `use rge_kernel_types::Reflect`** (or any kernel/types reflection API) imports in workspace `src/` — only 2 doc-comment-only mentions in `crates/components-spatial/src/lib.rs:20` and `crates/rge-data/src/lib.rs:39,75`, neither acted on; (f) `script-host` has the only normal-Cargo dep on `kernel/types` outside macros-reflect, but it is **dead substrate** — zero `use rge_kernel_types::...` references anywhere in `crates/script-host/src/` or `tests/` (the dep was declared in anticipation of the generic hot-reload migration referenced in BASELINE.md's Phase 3.2 Notes/caveats, but never wired up). **Phase 9 §9 reflection-scale evaluation is structurally unmeasurable** until production adoption begins — the §13.2 reflection-cache budget (1000 components ≤ 2 MB), §13.10 schema-size metric (> 10 K typed-components × fields = warn), and the qualitative "100+ reflected types" Phase 9 axis are all vacuously satisfied because production reflected-type count is 0; Phase 1.1 abort (> 30 s on 5 pilots ⇒ STOP) is PASS at 7.5 s but unverified against any real-consumer workload. **Two-arm revisit trigger** codified: re-run this preflight when **either** (a) any production crate (non-test) adds its first `#[derive(Reflect)]` or imports `rge_kernel_types::Reflect`, **or** (b) `script-host` actually wires its declared `kernel/types` dep into the generic hot-reload migration path (replaces the hand-rolled `CounterSnapshot` with a `Reflect`-driven value-walk). **Document + defer** explicitly chosen over "tiny adoption task" path — closest adoption candidates (editor inspector widget, script-host generic hot-reload, `rge-data` `pub use Reflect` doc-comment promise) all rejected because no real production consumer requires reflection today; landing adoption without a consumer would risk mis-shaping the consumer API since inspector / hot-reload-migration / asset-metadata / component-RON have different optimal trait surfaces. **Scope envelope strictly honored**: no source / test / Cargo / lint / ADR / automation / pilot-type / GitHub-issue edits; `tools/` untouched; no new lint; `ai-auto` cap unchanged at 80. Verification: `git diff --check` exit 0; `git diff --stat` shows only `plans/BASELINE.md` and `change.md` modified; the 9+1 architecture lint gate is markdown-agnostic and was not re-run. Files modified (2 total, both docs): `plans/BASELINE.md` (new §1.1 section appended, ~135 lines) and `change.md` (this entry).

2026-05-28 (ISSUE-243 — docs+measurement W04 raw Wasmtime Cranelift cold-start recorder-host rebaseline, IMPLEMENTED via the `ai_handoffs/` dispatch protocol — orchestrator semantics per `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`). Re-measures the W04 raw Wasmtime Cranelift cold-start row on the recorder host (Windows 11 Pro for Workstations / x86_64 / NVIDIA RTX 4060 Ti — recorder-host status confirmed against the existing `HANDOFF.md` / `Status.md` recorder-host references) using the existing focused test `crates/script-bench/src/wasmtime_cranelift.rs::tests::w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift` unchanged. Three back-to-back focused-release runs were performed from the workspace root via `cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib wasmtime_cranelift::tests::w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift -- --nocapture`; raw `--nocapture` stdout for each: `w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift: duration_ns=417400 duration_ms=0.417`, `w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift: duration_ns=257300 duration_ms=0.257`, `w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift: duration_ns=255600 duration_ms=0.256`. Selected **min-of-3 = 255 600 ns / 0.256 ms**, **36.9% faster** than the 2026-05-12 wasmtime 44 single-run anchor of 405 100 ns / 0.405 ms recorded in `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md` under "Formal W04 raw WasmtimeCranelift gates — RUN 2026-05-12". The greater-than-20%-slowdown observation threshold is not triggered; the movement is reported as an observation only with no pass/fail action and no regression investigation. 2026-05-28 (ISSUE-245 — CI-wiring W04 raw Wasmtime Cranelift cold-start observational signal, IMPLEMENTED via the `ai_handoffs/` dispatch protocol — orchestrator semantics per `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`). Wires the existing focused release test `crates/script-bench/src/wasmtime_cranelift.rs::tests::w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift` (referenced unchanged; byte-identical to the file at commit `97fda49`, the post-#244 `main` HEAD as of the dispatch filing) into GitHub Actions as a no-gate observational signal. **What landed**: a new `script-bench-cold-start-observational` job appended to `.github/workflows/bench.yml` alongside the byte-identical existing `script-bench-compile` job. The new job runs on `ubuntu-latest`, uses the same workflow triggers (`push` to `main`, `pull_request` to `main`), installs the pinned toolchain via the same `dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable` + `Swatinem/rust-cache@v2` pattern as the existing job (`shared-key: script-bench-cold-start-observational` to avoid clobbering the existing `script-bench-compile` cache), and in a single `bash` step runs `cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib wasmtime_cranelift::tests::w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift -- --nocapture` three times in succession, captures each run's `duration_ns` from stdout via `grep -oE 'duration_ns=[0-9]+'`, and computes the min-of-3 via pure bash arithmetic (`min=$(( d1 < d2 ? d1 : d2 )); min=$(( min < d3 ? min : d3 ))`). No helper script, no Python, no bench harness, and no threshold environment variable were introduced. The job emits a Markdown table to `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` containing the three per-run `duration_ns` values, the min-of-3 `duration_ns`, `cargo --version`, `rustc --version`, the `wasmtime` version read from `Cargo.lock` via a one-line `awk` extraction, and the three required caveat lines verbatim: "Observational only. No new timing threshold; inherits the harness's `< 2s` ceiling assertion."; "Runner is GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest, NOT the DENTAL recorder host."; and "Numbers are NOT comparable to the `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md` recorder-host row." The job uses default cargo-test failure semantics (`continue-on-error` is unset) so a compile error or a test panic fails the job, distinct from any timing observation; `set -euo pipefail` is enabled inside the bash step so a missing `duration_ns` capture also fails the job rather than emitting a misleading "0" sample. The harness's existing `assert!(duration > Duration::ZERO)` and `assert!(duration < Duration::from_secs(2))` invariants are inherited unchanged and remain the only timing-related failure path; the 2-second ceiling is structurally many orders of magnitude above any plausible CI sample and is expected never to trip. **Local single-sample verification** for the same command CI will invoke, run from the workspace root on the recorder host (Windows 11 Pro for Workstations / x86_64): `cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib wasmtime_cranelift::tests::w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift -- --nocapture` produced `w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift: duration_ns=276700 duration_ms=0.277`; exit code 0; test result `ok. 1 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 28 filtered out`. This single sample was performed solely to confirm the harness still prints the expected stdout line shape (per TASK packet §Verification Gates "Local test run") and was explicitly **NOT** appended to `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md`. Toolchain captured for evidence: `cargo 1.92.0 (344c4567c 2025-10-21)` / `rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08)` / `wasmtime 44.0.1` (per `Cargo.lock`). **Hard non-goals observed**: NO edit to any Rust source / test / bench / fixture, including `crates/script-bench/src/**` / `crates/script-bench/tests/**` / `crates/script-bench/benches/**`; NO `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` / `**/Cargo.toml` edit; NO `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md` edit (no CI-host baseline row appended — that is a separate deferred chip per the TASK packet's Planner Notes follow-up list); NO edit to any workflow other than `.github/workflows/bench.yml` (`architecture.yml` / `deny.yml` / `fmt.yml` / `powershell.yml` / `tests.yml` untouched); NO new file added under `.github/` (no composite action, no helper script, no `dependabot.yml`); 2026-06-02 (MENUBAR-RECONCILE-DRAINTEST — planning-substrate hygiene + test, via the `ai_handoffs/` dispatch protocol). Forward-only reconciliation that the File menu bar landed FUNCTIONAL (#287 `69b33e6` + #288 `7979f8b`), recorded into Status.md / HANDOFF.md / plans/BASELINE.md and here. #287 (MENUBAR-FILE-SUBSTRATE) added a host File menu bar (Open / Save / Save As New Project) enqueuing `editor_ui::menus::Command` onto a host→shell FIFO `MenuCommandHandoff` (bounded `VecDeque<Command>`); #288 (MENUBAR-FILE-WIRING) wired `editor-shell` to drain it at the top of `render_frame` and route each Command one-way into the existing `handle_open_request` / `handle_save_request` / `handle_save_as_new_project_request` (+ the forbidden-dep-validated `editor-shell → rge-editor-ui` edge). So Open/Save/Save-As are now menu-discoverable AND functional, not keyboard-only. This dispatch also: added a `render_frame` drain-placement test (`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`) closing the deep-audit's P2 gap (the four route tests called `drain_and_route_menu_commands` directly; none drove `render_frame`, so the load-bearing top-of-`render_frame` drain placement was structurally untested); and fixed the stale `EguiHost::render` rustdoc (which still called menu rendering a "future dispatch"). STILL DEFERRED (narrowed): the `MenuRegistry::resolve` data-driven dispatch (W08 registry) — the File bar is a direct host menu bar (`egui::MenuBar::new().ui`); Edit/View/Play menus + accelerators + plugin entries unbuilt (the wire-vs-ratify decision is the named next frontier). Provenance: the 2026-06-02 post-menu-arc read-only 5-axis deep audit (authority intact; no kernel-cavity pressure; this drained the lone Layer-1 status-doc lie + the P2 test gap). Docs + 1 test + 1 rustdoc; 0 production logic; 0 Cargo.toml. Gate: VERIFY OK 7/7 + 10/10 arch lints.

2026-05-23 15:20:00 - REPO TRANSFER (operational, not a dispatch). Repository moved from `CADRust/RGE` to `RustCADs/RGE` to escape the exhausted Actions minutes on the CADRust org (3,000 min/month allotment hit, ~87% consumed by sibling `CADRust/RCAD` repo per metered-usage view). GitHub auto-redirects the old `CADRust/RGE` URL for ~1 year; local `origin` remote updated from `https://github.com/CADRust/RGE.git` to `https://github.com/RustCADs/RGE.git` via `git remote set-url` + `git fetch origin` + `git remote prune origin` (17 stale `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-*` remote-tracking refs pruned). **All carried over via transfer semantics**: ~130 issues (including the 100 `ai-auto`-labelled issues that ISSUE-128's cap-stop-state audit reached), ~30 PRs, all labels (`ai-auto` / `ai-dispatch-done` / `ai-dispatch-failed` / `ai-dispatch-retry` / `ai-dispatch`), releases, branches, tags, and all 6 GitHub Actions workflow definitions (Format check, Architecture lints, Workspace tests, Script bench compile, Supply chain (cargo-deny), CodeQL). **Visibility changed**: `CADRust/RGE` was flipped `private → public` mid-transfer because RustCADs free-tier policy required a public source for the incoming transfer; reverting to private at the new home is now an operator decision (not done in this commit). **CI verified executing** on RustCADs runners: the fmt rerun `#26332202630` on commit `2ce4404` completed `success` (was `completed/failure` on CADRust); CodeQL auto-triggered as `#26336370076` and progressed real steps with `runner_name: "GitHub Actions 1000000000"` populated and `steps_count: 10/11` recorded. This is the inverse of the ISSUE-100 (task #8) CI audit's documented "empty runner_name, 0 steps, ~2-second failure" pattern — that pattern is fully resolved on RustCADs because the org has its own Actions-minutes allotment uncoupled from `CADRust/RCAD`'s monthly burn. Reruns of `#26332202631` (Architecture lints), `#26332202627` (Workspace tests), `#26332202626` (Script bench compile), and `#26332202614` (Supply chain) all triggered cleanly within seconds — 3 of 4 had runners attached within 15 s, the 4th queued normally. **Freeze posture unchanged**: scheduler `RGE-AiDispatch` remains `Disabled`, `ai-auto` cap remains hard-frozen at 100 in both `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1` `[ValidateRange(1, 100)]`, no task #23 seeded, no autonomous work since #22 / ISSUE-128. **No script edits required**: dispatch automation scripts auto-detect repo slug via the `gh` CLI, so no `Invoke-AiDispatch*.ps1` / `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1` / `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` / workflow-file edits were needed; the only operational change is the local `origin` URL plus this `change.md` entry. **Historical immutability preserved**: prior dated `change.md` entries — including the 2026-05-08 "first public commit pushed to CADRust/RGE on GitHub" record at line 672 — and all `ai_handoffs/` packets (TASK / EXEC / etc.) remain unedited as immutable historical records; they were accurate at the time and the GitHub redirect keeps their `CADRust/RGE` URLs resolvable. **Hard non-goals observed**: NO source / test / fixture / Cargo / lint / ADR / workflow / script / schema edits; NO dispatch packet rewrites; NO `ai-auto` cap change; NO scheduler re-enable; NO retroactive replacement of `CADRust/RGE` references in `ai_handoffs/` / `ai_dispatch_logs/` / earlier `change.md` entries. **Verification (docs-only gates)**: `git diff --name-only` lists exactly `change.md`; `git status --short --untracked-files=all` shows no untracked files; local `git fetch origin` resolves against `https://github.com/RustCADs/RGE.git` and `origin/main` HEAD matches `2ce4404`. Files modified (1 doc total): `change.md` (this entry appended; no rewrite of any prior entry, no edit to any other doc). **Three required references present in this entry**: GitHub commit `2ce4404`, GitHub Actions run `#26332202630` (Format check rerun on RustCADs/RGE), and `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-100_EXEC_2026-05-23_00-37-25+0300.md` (the original CI failure-boundary audit that diagnosed the empty-runner pattern this transfer resolves).

2026-06-06 (GUARD-FLIGHT-READY + ADR-121 reconciliation, docs-only). Forward-only state reconciliation after two governance updates landed on `main`. First, `36365e5` added `docs/adr/ADR-121-minimal-handoff-protocol-enforcement.md` as **Status: Proposed**: advisory-first packet/scope validation plus claim/lock convention, explicitly not wired into `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, dispatch scripts, templates, schemas, workflows, scheduler behavior, or live automation. Second, PR #318 squash-merged as `a0258db` after branch fix `9168989`: `Invoke-AiDispatchGuard.ps1` now has the live driver-supervision path described in `AUTONOMOUS_WATCH.md`, with stdout/stderr capture, source-tagged `signal` / `command` / `prose` classification, hard-rule sweeps, optional Claude assessment, child-tree abort, and `abort-report.md` output. The pre-merge fix closed the final-output safety gap by draining already-exited children before declaring completion and sweeping both command and signal queues; it also tightened protected-ref/force-push matching so `feature/main-fix` is allowed while any `main`/`master` target, force flag, or leading `+` refspec trips. New `tools/dispatch-tests/GuardSafetyMonitor.Tests.ps1` covers the guard monitor logic including the final-drain command-abort path. **Verification:** local focused Pester 36/36; local full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 351/351; local PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings; `git diff --check` clean for this reconciliation; GitHub PR #318 checks all green before merge. **Scope:** docs-only reconciliation confined to `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `change.md`; no source/test/Cargo/workflow/schema/automation/task-brief/scheduler behavior changed by this commit. **Current posture:** no open issues, no open PRs, local `main` aligned with `origin/main`; scheduler not registered/changed; guard not pointed at the real auto driver with `-PublishMode main`; no live autonomous run against RGE. **Next gate:** explicit arming under `AUTONOMOUS_WATCH.md`/`AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md` section 18, or separate accepted ADR-121 implementation TASK; neither is implied by this reconciliation.

2026-06-06 (ADR-121 advisory validator slice, implementation-tooling only). ADR-121 moves from Proposed to **Accepted for advisory implementation only**, and the first standalone tooling slice lands without touching live automation. New root script `Test-HandoffPacket.ps1` validates existing protocol invariants: canonical `<DISPATCH_ID>_<TYPE>_<TIMESTAMP>.md` filenames, required header fields, non-empty `RELATED_FILES`, EOF-anchored five-key footer, valid `HANDOFF_STATUS`/`NEXT_ROLE`/integer exit code, header/footer consistency, optional `.meta.json` sidecar consistency, and `CLOSEOUT` evidence sections (final commits with hash for closed packets, verification gates, test-count delta, remaining risks, follow-ons). It also implements the optional ADR-121 TASK scope envelope parser (`<!-- handoff:envelope v1 -->`) with legacy-safe `UNCHECKED`, narrow gitignore-like glob semantics (`**`, `*`, trailing `/**`, no brace expansion), `ai_handoffs/**` exemption, path-only incidental exemptions (`Cargo.lock`, `**/*.meta.json`), `MUST_NOT_EDIT` dominance, empty-MAY denylist mode, and Planner-owned `SCOPE_OVERRIDE` downgrade to `WARN`. CLI output is advisory-first: `HANDOFF_VALIDATE: PASS|WARN|FAIL` plus JSON, exiting 0 on FAIL unless `-Blocking` is explicitly passed. New `tools/dispatch-tests/HandoffPacketValidator.Tests.ps1` pins packet pass/fail behavior, sidecar mismatch detection, closeout evidence checks, legacy-envelope `UNCHECKED`, scope pass/fail, incidental exemptions, denylist mode, brace-glob rejection, and Planner-only override semantics. **Explicit non-changes:** no `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` integration, no queue/loop/auto/guard script behavior change, no scheduler change, no schema/workflow/template change, no claim/lock implementation, no blocking gate. Historical-envelope smoke and any advisory verify wiring remain future explicit work. **Verification:** focused `HandoffPacketValidator.Tests.ps1` Pester 12/12; full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 363/363; PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (ADR-121 historical scope smoke, advisory-only). First executable smoke for ADR-121 D7 lands. New `tools/dispatch-tests/HandoffScopeHistoricalSmoke.Tests.ps1` hand-authors marker-delimited envelopes for five representative recent commits and checks them against the real touched-file sets from `git diff-tree`: `5730f5c` (validator slice), `a0258db` (guard flight-ready merge), `1bdee7e` (Codex executor dry-run plumbing), `dbd2bda` (guard/ADR docs reconcile), and `36365e5` (ADR-121 proposal). The smoke confirms `PASS` for those real repo paths, injects `crates/cad-core/src/lib.rs` to confirm a true out-of-envelope `FAIL`, confirms Planner-authored `SCOPE_OVERRIDE` downgrades that injected violation to `WARN`, and confirms a legacy task with no envelope returns `UNCHECKED` instead of failing closed. ADR-121 D7 now points at this suite as the initial executable smoke record. **Explicit non-changes:** no historical handoff packets rewritten, no `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` integration, no packet-template change, no queue/loop/auto/guard/scheduler behavior change, no schema/workflow change, no claim/lock implementation, no blocking gate. **Verification:** focused `HandoffScopeHistoricalSmoke.Tests.ps1` Pester 3/3; full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 366/366; PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (ADR-121 claim/lock helper, standalone advisory tooling). The second ADR-121 mechanism lands as an unwired helper. New `Invoke-HandoffClaim.ps1` implements the protocol's claim/lock convention without changing any runner: live operational locks are atomically acquired by creating `.ai/handoff-claims/<DISPATCH_ID>/`, while durable append-only audit events are written under `ai_handoffs/claims/*.json`. Supported actions: `Status`, `Claim`, `Renew`, `Release`, and `Reclaim`. The helper validates dispatch IDs to prevent path escape, records actor/harness/branch/timestamp/TTL/pid in the live `claim.json`, writes JSON events for claim/renew/release/expire/reclaim, blocks other actors while a live claim exists, treats expired claims as `STALE` until an explicit `Reclaim`, allows renew/release only for the owning actor+harness, and exposes `-Blocking` for callers that want `BLOCKED`/`STALE` to exit 2. Branch autodetection is best-effort and silent outside a git repo to avoid the PowerShell 5.1 native-stderr trap. New `tools/dispatch-tests/HandoffClaim.Tests.ps1` covers available status, live record + event creation, duplicate actor blocking, same-owner `OWNED`, renew ownership and timestamp/TTL update, release ownership and live-lock removal, explicit stale reclaim with `expire` + `reclaim` events, and path-safety rejection. **Explicit non-changes:** no `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchGuard.ps1`, `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, packet-template, scheduler, schema/workflow, or blocking-gate integration. **Verification:** focused `HandoffClaim.Tests.ps1` Pester 8/8; full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 374/374; PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (dispatch task-brief stale-entry reconciliation, docs/task-brief only). `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` now marks stale completed entries #62-#72 as done so the autonomous selector does not re-file already landed work. Entry #72 is the PowerShell CI guardrails task already landed as ISSUE-223 commit `6a24f51`. Only the headings gained `[DONE ...]` markers; task bodies remain unchanged. **Explicit non-changes:** no dispatch loop, queue, auto driver, guard, verify gate, scheduler, packet protocol, workflow, Rust/Cargo, or test behavior changed. **Verification:** `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (ADR-121 advisory verify integration, non-blocking). ADR-121's advisory validator is now visible in the canonical verify log without becoming a gate. `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` invokes `Test-HandoffPacket.ps1` after the Rust/CI-parity checks as a non-counted advisory section. It discovers dispatch context from `RGE_AI_DISPATCH_ID` or an `ai-dispatch/<id>` branch, validates the latest matching `EXEC` packet when present (falling back to `TASK`), passes the matching `TASK` packet for optional scope validation, and passes matching `CORRECT` packets as Planner-owned override authorities. The hook prints `HANDOFF_ADVISORY: SKIP|PASS|WARN`; validator printed `WARN`/`FAIL` verdicts and validator non-zero exits are downgraded to advisory `WARN`, the hook never passes `-Blocking`, and `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` still exits according to the existing CI-parity steps only. `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` now sets/restores `RGE_AI_DISPATCH_ID` and `RGE_AI_DISPATCH_ROUND` around only the verification child process so standalone loop runs have explicit context. `Test-HandoffPacket.ps1` help text and ADR-121 are updated to describe the advisory integration state. New `tools/dispatch-tests/HandoffVerifyAdvisory.Tests.ps1` adds 7 focused tests covering dispatch-id resolution, no-context skip, packet selection, printed-`FAIL` with exit 0, and non-zero validator downgrade. **Explicit non-changes:** no blocking handoff enforcement, no queue/auto/guard/scheduler behavior change, no packet-template/schema change, no claim/lock runner integration, no Rust/Cargo/workflow behavior change. **Verification:** focused `HandoffVerifyAdvisory.Tests.ps1` Pester 7/7; full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 381/381; PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings; `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (ADR-121 optional TASK envelope guidance, advisory template slice). New TASK packets now have an optional marker-delimited scope envelope in `ai_handoffs/templates/TASK_PACKET.md`, while the existing human-readable scope sections remain authoritative for the queue scope guard. The template adds `### Advisory machine-readable scope envelope` with `<!-- handoff:envelope v1 -->`, `MAY_EDIT`, `MUST_NOT_EDIT`, `INCIDENTAL_OK: false`, and guidance to delete/leave empty for read-only or unsafe-to-express scope so validation reports `UNCHECKED`. `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`'s Planner prompt now instructs Codex to mirror `### MAY edit` / `### MAY add new files` into `MAY_EDIT`, mirror `### MUST NOT edit` / `### MUST NOT add new files` into `MUST_NOT_EDIT`, use raw repo-relative paths/globs without Markdown backticks, avoid brace expansion, and set `INCIDENTAL_OK` true only for explicitly accepted `Cargo.lock` / `*.meta.json` incidental outputs. ADR-121 records the template slice. `tools/dispatch-tests/Invoke-PlanFill-Prompt.Tests.ps1` adds static checks for the prompt guidance and template markers. **Explicit non-changes:** no queue scope-guard parser behavior change, no blocking handoff enforcement, no claim/lock runner integration, no scheduler/publish behavior, no Rust/Cargo/workflow behavior change. **Verification:** focused `Invoke-PlanFill-Prompt.Tests.ps1` Pester 10/10; full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 384/384; PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings; `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (ADR-121 claim-event queue scope allowance, staging-prep only). `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`'s pre-staging scope guard now allows only helper-generated ADR-121 claim event JSON for the active dispatch under `ai_handoffs/claims/`, using a tight `Test-HandoffClaimEventPath` matcher for `<DISPATCH_ID>_<TIMESTAMP>_<claim|renew|release|expire|reclaim>.json`. This prepares the queue to stage future claim events without treating all nested `ai_handoffs/**` paths as protocol artifacts. `tools/dispatch-tests/Issue237-DotfileScopeGuard.Tests.ps1` adds a temp-git-worktree pass case for an active-dispatch claim event and a fail-closed case for a non-claim nested handoff path. ADR-121 records the slice. **Explicit non-changes:** no `Invoke-HandoffClaim.ps1` behavior change, no loop/queue/auto claim acquisition, no runner renewal/release lifecycle, no blocking validator, no scheduler/publish behavior, no Rust/Cargo/workflow behavior change. **Verification:** focused `Issue237-DotfileScopeGuard.Tests.ps1` Pester 34/34; full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 386/386; PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings; `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (ADR-121 split live-lock/event roots, helper-topology prep). `Invoke-HandoffClaim.ps1` now accepts `-LiveRoot`, and the internal `Invoke-HandoffClaim` function accepts `LiveRepoRoot`, so callers can place the mutable live lock under one shared root while writing append-only claim events under another root. The default remains unchanged: if `-LiveRoot` / `LiveRepoRoot` is empty, the helper uses `-Root` / `RepoRoot` for both live locks and event JSON. This is the required topology for future queue/worktree integration: `.ai/handoff-claims/<DISPATCH_ID>/claim.json` can live in the primary checkout shared by concurrent queue/worktrees, while `ai_handoffs/claims/*.json` is written in the isolated dispatch worktree and can be staged by the queue scope guard from the prior slice. `tools/dispatch-tests/HandoffClaim.Tests.ps1` adds a split-root regression with two synthetic worktrees sharing one primary live root; the first claim writes its event under worktree A and the live record under primary, and the second worktree is blocked without writing an event. The same edit also fixes analyzer warnings in that test file by renaming the helper from `Get-ClaimEventFiles` to `Get-ClaimEventFile` and avoiding assignment to PowerShell's `$event` automatic variable. **Explicit non-changes:** no dispatch loop/queue/auto claim acquisition, no queue argument plumbing, no runner renewal/release lifecycle, no blocking validator, no scheduler/publish behavior, no Rust/Cargo/workflow behavior change. **Verification:** focused `HandoffClaim.Tests.ps1` Pester 9/9; full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 387/387; PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings; `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (ADR-121 queue handoff claim lifecycle, default queue integration). `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` now acquires the ADR-121 handoff claim in the live queue path after `git worktree add` and before `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` starts. The queue calls `Invoke-HandoffClaim.ps1` with the primary checkout as `-LiveRoot` so concurrent worktrees contend on one shared `.ai/handoff-claims/<DISPATCH_ID>/claim.json`, and with the isolated worktree as `-Root` so append-only `ai_handoffs/claims/*.json` events are staged with the dispatch output. Accepted statuses are `CLAIMED`, `RECLAIMED`, and `OWNED`; `STALE` is reclaimed before execution; any other live owner blocks the dispatch before loop start, removes the empty just-created worktree, deletes the fresh branch, and reports a queue failure. Successful runs release the claim after the loop exits and before dispatch-log generation/staging. `-SkipHandoffClaim` is added as an operator escape hatch, with default queue/auto/scheduler-invoked runs keeping claims enabled. `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md` and ADR-121 now document the real behavior; `Status.md` and `HANDOFF.md` carry forward snapshots. Tests add source-level dry-run coverage for claim argument shape, acquire ordering, and release ordering, and update the existing ISSUE-231/ISSUE-259 source invariants so the pre-loop claim-block cleanup is not mistaken for post-run worktree cleanup that needs run-dir copy-out. **Explicit non-changes:** no blocking packet validator, no `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` executor behavior change, no guard behavior change, no scheduler registration or publish-mode semantic change, no Rust/Cargo/workflow/schema behavior change. **Verification:** focused Pester (`Issue231-DispatchLogPathStableAfterCleanup`, `Issue259-RunEvidenceCopyOut`, `AutonomousCodexExecutorDryRun`) 29/29; full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 390/390; PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings; `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (ADR-121 queue claim TTL guard, follow-up). `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` now exposes `-HandoffClaimTtlSeconds` with a 43200-second (12-hour) default and threads that value through claim acquisition, stale reclaim, and release helper calls. This fixes the immediate TTL mismatch from the first queue-claim integration slice: the previous helper-argument default was 7200 seconds, which could expire before a legitimate long orchestrator run completed across model calls, verification, and correction rounds. The automation spec and ADR-121 now document the tradeoff explicitly: this is a long default TTL, not a background renewal loop; a crashed queue leaves the live claim stale only after the configured TTL, and operators can override the value per run. `tools/dispatch-tests/AutonomousCodexExecutorDryRun.Tests.ps1` pins the queue parameter default, the generated helper argument vector, and the live acquire/release call sites. **Explicit non-changes:** no renewal background job, no blocking validator, no scheduler/publish-mode behavior change, no Rust/Cargo/workflow/schema behavior change. **Verification:** focused `AutonomousCodexExecutorDryRun.Tests.ps1` Pester 12/12; full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 392/392; touched-file PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings (full-repo analyzer still reports the pre-existing `Install-Windows11-D-Standalone.ps1:94` warning); `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (Play menu passive shortcut hints). The editor main menu now surfaces the already-live playback keys as display-only hints without registering them as menu accelerators. `MenuEntry` gains `shortcut_hint: Option<Shortcut>` plus `with_shortcut_hint`; the resolver continues to register only executable `shortcut` values in the accelerator table and shortcut-command index. `default_editor_menu` assigns passive hints to Play (`Space`), Pause (`Space`), and Stop (`Escape`), leaving Step and View Reset Camera without hints. `editor-egui-host::project_main_menu` displays executable shortcuts first and falls back to passive hints, so the menu bar shows the playback keys while `editor-shell` keeps owning the actual plain-key Space/Escape route. Tests pin that Play displays Space/Escape, Play/View still carry no executable accelerator, and the global accelerator table remains the five File/Edit bindings. **Explicit non-changes:** no playback key routing change, no View Reset Camera binding, no dynamic labels, no menu-click FIFO replacement, no plugin-menu behavior, no scheduler/dispatch automation/Rust workspace architecture change. **Verification:** `cargo test -p rge-editor-ui --lib` 100/100; `cargo test -p rge-editor-egui-host --lib` 24/24; `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; architecture lints PASS; `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (Play menu dynamic Resume label). `MenuRegistry::resolve` can now apply a resolver-time `LabelOverride` to cloned resolved entries, leaving registered `MenuEntry` data unchanged. `MenuEntry` gains `LabelOverride` and `with_label_override`; the default menu uses it narrowly for `play.start`, changing the visible label from `Play` to `Resume` when `PredicateContext.play_state == "paused"`. The entry id, `Command::PlayStart`, enablement predicate, and passive `Space` hint stay unchanged; `editor-egui-host::project_main_menu` simply receives the resolved label and needs no host-side branch. Tests cover generic resolved-label override non-mutation, the default menu's paused `Resume` label, and the host projection's paused label + Space hint. **Explicit non-changes:** no Play/Pause/Stop state-machine behavior change, no keyboard routing change, no executable accelerator-table binding, no View label/binding, no menu-click FIFO replacement, no plugin-menu behavior, no scheduler/dispatch automation change. **Verification:** `cargo test -p rge-editor-ui --lib` 102/102; `cargo test -p rge-editor-egui-host --lib` 25/25; `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; architecture lints PASS; `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (View Reset Camera Home accelerator). `default_editor_menu` now gives `view.reset_camera` an executable `Shortcut::plain(Key::Home)`, so the View menu displays `Home` and the existing shell accelerator path routes a plain Home key to `Command::ResetCamera`. This uses the same canonical menu source of truth as File/Edit accelerators: `keycode_to_shortcut(KeyCode::Home, false, false)` resolves to `Shortcut::Home`, `enabled_command_for_shortcut` returns `ResetCamera`, and `route_menu_command` already calls `EditorShell::reset_camera`. The executable accelerator table grows from five to six entries; Play's Space/Escape remain passive display hints only and still do not bind executable menu shortcuts. Tests update the default-menu accelerator count, egui-host View projection, and shell accelerator parity. **Explicit non-changes:** no reset-camera implementation change, no Play shortcut routing change, no menu-click FIFO replacement, no plugin-menu behavior, no scheduler/dispatch automation change. **Verification:** `cargo test -p rge-editor-ui --lib` 102/102; `cargo test -p rge-editor-egui-host --lib` 25/25; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib lifecycle::accelerator` 5/5; `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; architecture lints PASS; `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (Menu shortcut baseline reconciliation). `plans/BASELINE.md` now records a forward-only 2026-06-06 reconciliation for the latest menu follow-ups: `f3931a7` passive Play Space/Escape hints, `67c140a` paused-context `Resume` label override, and `2df991c` View Reset Camera plain-`Home` accelerator. The section narrows the older "Play/View accelerator display + execution" and "View Reset Camera has no keystroke binding" open lines while preserving the historical 2026-06-05 and 2026-06-04 subsections below it. Remaining menu work is restated as conflict UI/surfacing, dynamic labels beyond the narrow Resume override, plugin menu entries, host->shell FIFO replacement, generalized execution beyond wired canonical accelerators, and unused visibility predicates. **Explicit non-changes:** no Rust source/test/Cargo change, no runtime menu behavior change, no scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `git diff --check` clean; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.

2026-06-06 (Menu shortcut conflict diagnostics). `editor-egui-host` now surfaces registry shortcut conflicts at the host boundary. `project_main_menu` returns a named `ProjectedMainMenu` carrying the File/Edit/Play/View entry lists plus projected conflict diagnostics, and `EguiHost::render` adds a `Shortcut Conflicts` top-bar menu only when conflicts exist. The default canonical menu remains conflict-free, so normal UI output is unchanged. A new host unit test registers a synthetic duplicate `Ctrl+S` File-menu entry and asserts the projected diagnostic reports `Ctrl+S` with `file.save` then `plugin.conflict.save`, proving the registry's existing conflict data is no longer ignored by the host. `plans/BASELINE.md` now narrows the conflict-surfacing open item. **Explicit non-changes:** no conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gate, no plugin menu UX, no menu-click FIFO replacement, no shell accelerator routing change, no Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo test -p rge-editor-egui-host --lib` 26/26; `cargo test -p rge-editor-ui --lib` 102/102; `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; architecture lints PASS; `git diff --check` clean. Full `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` was attempted: steps 1-4 passed, then step 5 failed at executable link because this shell has no MSVC `link.exe` / SDK libraries (`rge-editor` test binary link), so full verify did not complete.

2026-06-06 (PowerShell CI handoff-test hardening). Fixed the `powershell` workflow failure observed on pushed commit `3fc8b0c`. The workflow now uses `actions/checkout` with `fetch-depth: 0` because ADR-121's historical scope smoke intentionally calls `git diff-tree` on older commits (`5730f5c`, `36365e5`, etc.); the prior depth-1 checkout made those revisions unavailable in CI. `Test-HandoffPacket.ps1` now trims header/list values and accepts CRLF on `INCIDENTAL_OK: true`, so CRLF-authored packet fixtures and real packets validate the same as LF-authored packets. `tools/dispatch-tests/HandoffPacketValidator.Tests.ps1` now creates a fresh per-test OS temp directory, outside Pester's shared `TestDrive`, before writing synthetic packets, preventing fixed packet filenames and `.meta.json` sidecars from leaking across tests or repeated runs. **Explicit non-changes:** no queue/guard/auto behavior, no Rust/Cargo/menu behavior, no scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** focused Pester (`HandoffPacketValidator`, `HandoffScopeHistoricalSmoke`) 15/15; full `tools/dispatch-tests` Pester 392/392; touched-file PSScriptAnalyzer 0 findings; `git diff --check` clean. Full `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` remains locally blocked by the missing MSVC linker environment recorded in the prior menu-conflict snapshot.

2026-06-06 (Optional Plugins menu projection). Added the smallest plugin-menu surface on top of the canonical menu registry. `editor-ui::menus::default_editor_menu` now declares `editor.main_menu.plugins` through exported `plugins_menu_point()` with no default entries. `editor-egui-host::menu::ProjectedMainMenu` carries a `plugins` entry list, `project_main_menu` fills it from that extension point, and `EguiHost::render` shows a top-level `Plugins` menu only when the list is non-empty. The default menu bar is unchanged because the point starts empty. Host tests now register a synthetic `Command::Plugin` entry with `Ctrl+Shift+P`, assert the projected label/shortcut/command/enabled tuple, and assert the command drains from `MenuCommandHandoff` unchanged. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the previous "plugin menu entries" open line to the remaining plugin action execution/registration UX. **Explicit non-changes:** no plugin action execution/routing policy, no `editor-shell` routing, no plugin runtime, no command-palette integration, no host->shell FIFO replacement, no conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, no Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. Local linked Rust verification is blocked by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe`: `cargo test -p rge-editor-ui --lib`, `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui --tests`, and `cargo check -p rge-editor-egui-host --tests` abort while linking proc-macro/test artifacts before crate assertions can run.

2026-06-06 (View scene-aware camera label). Added scene-aware wording for the View camera action without changing routing. `PredicateContext` now carries `has_frameable_scene`; `EditorShell::predicate_context()` fills it from `current_scene_bounds().is_some()`, the same bounds source consumed by `EditorShell::reset_camera()`. `default_editor_menu` keeps the static View entry label as `Reset Camera`, but the resolver changes it to `Frame Scene` when frameable scene bounds exist. The entry still dispatches `Command::ResetCamera` and still binds the plain `Home` accelerator. Tests cover the default label, scene-aware override, unchanged command/shortcut, host projection, and shell-side context publication for prebuilt render meshes. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the previous camera-state-aware View dynamic-label item. **Explicit non-changes:** no `reset_camera` behavior change, no menu command routing change, no accelerator-table semantics change, no plugin runtime, no host->shell FIFO replacement, no conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, no Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. Local linked Rust verification remains blocked by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe`.

2026-06-06 (View camera zoom commands). Added bounded View menu zoom commands using existing `Command::ZoomIn` / `Command::ZoomOut` variants. `default_editor_menu` now registers Zoom In and Zoom Out after Reset Camera / Frame Scene, with executable plain `PageUp` / `PageDown` accelerators. The canonical executable menu accelerator set is now eight bindings: File Open/Save/Save-As, Edit Undo/Redo, View Reset Camera, View Zoom In, and View Zoom Out; Play remains passive Space/Escape display hints only. `EditorShell::route_menu_command` routes the new commands to infallible camera helpers that preserve target, view direction, up vector, FOV, and clip planes while scaling eye-target distance by inverse factors (`0.8` / `1.25`), with a finite fallback for degenerate eye-target vectors. Tests cover registry resolution, host projection/FIFO, keyboard PageUp/PageDown parity, direct zoom math, and shell menu routing. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the broader camera-state UI item. **Explicit non-changes:** no command palette, no plugin runtime/action execution, no host->shell FIFO replacement, no conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, no Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --lib` remains locally blocked before crate type-checking by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe`.

2026-06-06 (Edit Select All menu command). Added the smallest bounded Edit command beyond Undo/Redo. `default_editor_menu` now registers Edit -> Select All after Redo, with `Command::SelectAll` and executable `Ctrl+A`. `PredicateContext` gains `has_selectable_entities`; `EditorShell::predicate_context()` fills it from the live world entity count, and the Select All entry is enabled only when the shell is Editing and the world is non-empty. `EditorShell::route_menu_command` routes Select All to `select_all_entities()`, which replaces entity selection with the deterministic live `World::entities()` set while leaving world contents, CAD geometry, face selection, and the undo stack unchanged. Tests cover registry resolution, disabled accelerator behavior, host projection/FIFO/enablement, keyboard Ctrl+A parity, shell context publication, and shell routing. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the generalized menu-command execution item. **Explicit non-changes:** no Cut/Copy/Paste/Delete/Duplicate semantics, clipboard, plugin runtime/action execution, command palette, host->shell FIFO replacement, conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host -p rge-editor-shell --lib` remains locally blocked before crate type-checking by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe`.

2026-06-06 (Edit Delete selected entities). Added a bounded destructive Edit command after Select All. `default_editor_menu` now registers Edit -> Delete with `Command::Delete` and executable plain `Delete`, enabled only while the shell is Editing with a non-empty entity selection. `World::despawn` removes an entity from both the kernel world and the legacy blob view, including all legacy component blobs for that entity. `EditorShell::route_menu_command` routes Delete to `delete_selected_entities()`, which deletes selected wrapper-world entities, clears entity selection, and prunes face selections whose entity was deleted. Tests cover registry resolution, disabled accelerator behavior, host projection/FIFO/enablement, keyboard Delete parity, world despawn, and shell routing. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the generalized menu-command execution item. **Explicit non-changes:** no Cut/Copy/Paste/Duplicate semantics, clipboard, authoritative CAD graph/projection/render deletion, undo/redo or dirty-state integration for deletion, plugin runtime/action execution, command palette, host->shell FIFO replacement, conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host -p rge-editor-shell --lib` remains locally blocked before crate type-checking by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe` while compiling proc-macro crates.

2026-06-06 (Edit Duplicate selected entities). Added a bounded duplication Edit command after Delete. `default_editor_menu` now registers Edit -> Duplicate with `Command::Duplicate` and executable `Ctrl+D`, enabled only while the shell is Editing with a non-empty entity selection. `World::duplicate_entity_blobs` spawns a fresh entity and clones legacy component blobs from the selected source entity; typed kernel components are intentionally not cloned because there is no safe generic clone path for arbitrary typed ECS components. `EditorShell::route_menu_command` routes Duplicate to `duplicate_selected_entities()`, which duplicates selected wrapper-world entities, selects the new duplicates, and clears face selection because no authoritative face-ID remapping exists. Tests cover registry resolution, disabled accelerator behavior, host projection/FIFO/enablement, keyboard Ctrl+D parity, world duplicate, and shell routing. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the generalized menu-command execution item. **Explicit non-changes:** no Cut/Copy/Paste semantics, clipboard, authoritative CAD graph/projection/render duplication, undo/redo or dirty-state integration for duplication, plugin runtime/action execution, command palette, host->shell FIFO replacement, conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host -p rge-editor-shell --lib` remains locally blocked before crate type-checking by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe` while compiling proc-macro crates.

2026-06-06 (File New empty scene command). Added a bounded File menu reset command. `default_editor_menu` now registers File -> New before Open with `Command::NewFile` and executable `Ctrl+N`, enabled only while the shell is Editing. `EditorShell::route_menu_command` routes New to `handle_new_file_request()`, which reuses `replace_world(KernelWorld::new())` to reset to a fresh unsourced empty world. The reset clears the adopted save source, entity selection, render content, PIE snapshot, and command bus, and the fresh world gets default `TimeScale`. Tests cover registry resolution, disabled accelerator behavior, host projection/FIFO/enablement, keyboard Ctrl+N parity, and shell routing. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the File/menu execution surface. **Explicit non-changes:** no unsaved-changes prompt, disk/project/template creation, Save-As-new-project completion, Cut/Copy/Paste semantics, clipboard, authoritative CAD graph/projection/render deletion or duplication, undo/redo or dirty-state integration for Edit content mutations, plugin runtime/action execution, command palette, host->shell FIFO replacement, conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host -p rge-editor-shell --lib` remains locally blocked before crate type-checking by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe` while compiling proc-macro crates.

2026-06-06 (Edit Copy/Paste selected entities). Added bounded shell-local Edit Copy/Paste commands. `default_editor_menu` now registers Edit -> Copy and Edit -> Paste after Select All with `Command::Copy` / `Command::Paste` and executable `Ctrl+C` / `Ctrl+V`. Copy is enabled only while Editing with a non-empty entity selection; Paste is enabled only while Editing with a non-empty shell-local entity clipboard. `PredicateContext` now carries `has_clipboard_entities`, filled by `EditorShell::predicate_context()`. `World::clone_entity_blobs` and `World::spawn_with_component_blobs` provide the shared legacy-blob clone/spawn substrate, and `World::duplicate_entity_blobs` now reuses it. `EditorShell::route_menu_command` routes Copy to `copy_selected_entities()` and Paste to `paste_copied_entities()`: Copy stores cloned legacy component blobs in a shell-local clipboard; Paste spawns fresh wrapper-world entities, selects the pasted entities, and clears face selection because no authoritative face-ID remapping exists. `replace_world` clears the clipboard, so File New and scene Open drop stale copied entities. Tests cover registry resolution, disabled accelerator behavior, host projection/FIFO/enablement, keyboard Ctrl+C/Ctrl+V parity, world clone/spawn helpers, shell routing, and File New clipboard clearing. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the generalized Edit menu and clipboard surface. **Explicit non-changes:** no Cut semantics, OS/system clipboard integration, typed kernel component cloning, authoritative CAD graph/projection/render copy/paste, undo/redo or dirty-state integration for Copy/Paste, plugin runtime/action execution, command palette, host->shell FIFO replacement, conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host -p rge-editor-shell --lib` remains locally blocked before crate type-checking by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe` while compiling proc-macro crates.

2026-06-06 (Edit Cut selected entities). Added a bounded shell-local Edit Cut command. `default_editor_menu` now registers Edit -> Cut between Select All and Copy with `Command::Cut` and executable `Ctrl+X`, enabled only while the shell is Editing with a non-empty entity selection. `EditorShell::route_menu_command` routes Cut to `cut_selected_entities()`, which clones selected legacy component blobs into the shell-local entity clipboard and then delegates deletion to the existing bounded wrapper-world `delete_selected_entities()` path. Cut despawns selected wrapper-world entities, clears entity selection, prunes face selections for deleted entities, and leaves the clipboard available for Paste; a later Paste creates fresh wrapper-world entities from the copied blobs. Tests cover registry resolution, disabled accelerator behavior, host projection/FIFO/enablement, keyboard Ctrl+X parity, and shell routing including Paste-after-Cut. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the generalized Edit menu and clipboard surface. **Explicit non-changes:** no OS/system clipboard integration, typed kernel component cloning, authoritative CAD graph/projection/render cut/paste, undo/redo or dirty-state integration for Cut, plugin runtime/action execution, command palette, host->shell FIFO replacement, conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host -p rge-editor-shell --lib` remains locally blocked before crate type-checking by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe` while compiling proc-macro crates.

2026-06-06 (File Close current document). Added a bounded File menu close command. `default_editor_menu` now registers File -> Close after Save As New Project with `Command::Close` and executable `Ctrl+W`, enabled only while the shell is Editing. `EditorShell::route_menu_command` routes Close to `handle_close_file_request()`, which reuses `replace_world(KernelWorld::new())` to close the current document into a fresh unsourced empty world without exiting the application. The reset clears the adopted save source, entity selection, shell-local clipboard, render content, PIE snapshot, and command bus, and the fresh world gets default `TimeScale`. Tests cover registry resolution, disabled accelerator behavior, host projection/FIFO/enablement, keyboard Ctrl+W parity, and shell routing. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the File/menu execution surface. **Explicit non-changes:** no app quit/exit behavior, unsaved-changes prompt, disk/project/template creation, Save-As-new-project completion, OS/system clipboard integration, authoritative CAD graph/projection/render deletion or duplication, undo/redo or dirty-state integration for File Close, plugin runtime/action execution, command palette, host->shell FIFO replacement, conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host -p rge-editor-shell --lib` remains locally blocked before crate type-checking by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe` while compiling proc-macro crates.

2026-06-06 (File Quit app exit). Added a bounded File menu application-exit command. `default_editor_menu` now registers File -> Quit after Close with `Command::Quit` and executable `Ctrl+Q`. Quit has no enablement predicate and remains available while PIE is active; the document-mutating File commands still gate on Editing. `EditorShell::route_menu_command` routes Quit to `handle_quit_request()`, which records a one-shot app-exit request. `window_event` consumes that request at the event-loop boundary and calls the same `ActiveEventLoop::exit()` path used by `WindowEvent::CloseRequested`. Quit does not close/reset the current document and does not clear the adopted save source. Tests cover registry resolution, host projection/FIFO/enablement, keyboard Ctrl+Q parity, shell routing, and the stale unrouted-command fixture now uses `ToggleCommandPalette` instead of routed `Cut`. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the File/menu execution surface. **Explicit non-changes:** no unsaved-changes prompt/confirmation, no graceful shutdown save flow, no disk/project/template creation, no File Close reset semantics, no OS/system clipboard integration, no authoritative CAD graph/projection/render content mutation, no undo/redo or dirty-state integration, no plugin runtime/action execution, no command palette, no host->shell FIFO replacement, no conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, no Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-egui-host -p rge-editor-shell --lib` remains locally blocked before crate type-checking by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe` while compiling proc-macro crates.

2026-06-06 (Plugin menu registration hook). Added the host-side registration surface for the optional Plugins menu. `EguiHost::register_plugin_menu_entry(MenuEntry) -> Result<(), RegistryError>` now delegates into the host-owned `MenuRegistry` at the canonical `plugins_menu_point()`, so code holding the live host can add plugin entries without constructing a separate test registry. The existing render path still re-resolves that registry each frame; registered entries project into `ProjectedMainMenu::plugins`, the top-level Plugins menu remains hidden while empty, and activation still only enqueues the entry's `Command` through `MenuCommandHandoff`. Host tests now exercise the new helper path with a synthetic `Command::Plugin`, assert projection + FIFO round-trip, and assert duplicate plugin entry ids return `RegistryError::DuplicateEntryId`. `host_scaffolding_smoke` pins the public method signature without constructing a GPU-backed host. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the remaining plugin registration UX gap. **Explicit non-changes:** no plugin action execution/routing policy, no `editor-shell` route for `Command::Plugin`, no plugin runtime/discovery/loading, no command-palette integration, no host->shell FIFO replacement, no generalized registry execution beyond the menu command queue, no conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating, no Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-egui-host --tests` remains locally blocked before crate assertions by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe` while compiling proc-macro crates.

Widened the host-side menu registration surface from Plugins-only convenience to any declared menu extension point. `EguiHost::register_menu_entry(&ExtensionPoint, MenuEntry) -> Result<(), RegistryError>` now delegates into the host-owned `MenuRegistry`, and `register_plugin_menu_entry` is a wrapper using `plugins_menu_point()`. The render path is unchanged: the host still re-resolves the registry each frame, projects entries into the appropriate main-menu vector, and activation only pushes the entry's `Command` into `MenuCommandHandoff`. 2026-06-06 (Extension menu command capture). Added the shell-side capture substrate for extension menu activations. `EditorShell` now owns a shell-local FIFO for `Command::Custom` and `Command::Plugin`; `route_menu_command` captures those commands into the FIFO and logs their stable `diagnostic_id()` instead of dropping them as generic unrouted commands. `EditorShell::drain_extension_menu_commands()` exposes a one-shot FIFO drain for a future plugin/action executor. 2026-06-06 (Command palette toggle request). Added the shell-side request latch for the existing core `Command::ToggleCommandPalette`. `EditorShell` now owns a pending command-palette toggle flag, `route_menu_command` routes the command to `handle_command_palette_toggle_request()`, and `take_command_palette_toggle_request()` consumes it once for a future palette surface. The old test that treated `ToggleCommandPalette` as an unrouted no-op now proves the request is set and single-consume, does not run document handlers, and is not captured in the extension-command FIFO. `plans/BASELINE.md` narrows the command-palette gap accordingly. **Explicit non-changes:** no command-palette UI, search model, command list, default menu entry, shortcut binding, plugin runtime/action execution, host projection or FIFO replacement, generalized command execution beyond the existing menu command router, keybinding editor, Cargo/scheduler/dispatch automation change, no task arming. **Verification:** `cargo +nightly fmt --all` applied clean formatting; `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean. `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --tests` remains locally blocked before crate assertions by this machine's missing MSVC `link.exe` while compiling proc-macro crates.

2026-06-06 (Menu-backed command palette window). Turned the command-palette request latch into a minimal real egui surface. `EguiHost` now owns `command_palette_open` plus `toggle_command_palette()` / `is_command_palette_open()`. `EditorShell` consumes the one-shot `Command::ToggleCommandPalette` request immediately before `EguiHost::render` in both cuboid and egui-only render paths. 2026-06-07 (compile timing harness). Added `tools/compile-timing.ps1` as the repeatable Phase 9 warm-cache compile timing harness named by the earlier §13.3 baseline deferral. The script measures workspace `cargo check` and/or `cargo build` wall time, uses `A:\RustCache\cargo`, `A:\RustCache\rustup`, and `A:\RustCache\target` when present and unset, captures stdout/stderr cleanly on Windows PowerShell 5.1, supports `-Mode check|build|both`, `-Iterations`, `-AllTargets`, `-Release`, `-TimeoutSeconds`, `-JsonPath`, and `-NoDefaultRustCache`, and intentionally exposes no target-deletion or `cargo clean` path. Recorded current shared-cache warm no-op measurements: `cargo check --workspace` wall 0.896s / cargo 0.73s; `cargo build --workspace` wall 1.024s / cargo 0.84s. Both runs emitted the pre-existing `rge-ui-theme` missing-docs warnings and exited 0. Updated `plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to keep the destructive clean-build and 1-line incremental p95 measurements open as separate explicitly authorized tasks. **Explicit non-changes:** no source, tests, Cargo, workflow, scheduler, dispatch automation behavior, or target-cache deletion. **Verification:** `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\compile-timing.ps1 -Mode check -Iterations 1 -TimeoutSeconds 30` passed; `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\compile-timing.ps1 -Mode build -Iterations 1 -TimeoutSeconds 120` passed; `git diff --check` clean.

2026-06-07 (clean release compile measurement).
