2026-06-07 (guarded multi-tick auto selection). Added finite multi-tick full-automation support to `Invoke-AiDispatchGuard.ps1`: new `-DriverTicks` defaults to `1` for unchanged behavior, and values above 1 supervise sequential fresh invocations of `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` under the same guard. This makes the guarded automation re-run Codex task selection after each successful tick, using current task-brief and GitHub issue state, while preserving the one issue / one branch / one dispatch / one publish-decision queue boundary. The guarded batch stops early on no selectable task, cap reached, ambiguous queue state, dry-run, halt sentinel, failed autonomous issue, or another active Auto tick. `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` selector prompt now explicitly says "best next task" and preserves brief order, dependency ordering, already-filed issue avoidance, and DONE / DONE-SUPERSEDED exclusion. Updated `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`. **Explicit non-changes:** no scheduler registration, no default `PublishMode` change, no standing `PublishMode main` authorization, no queue publish behavior change, no verification-gate change, and no Rust/Cargo/workflow changes. **Verification:** PowerShell parser validation for `Invoke-AiDispatchGuard.ps1` and `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` passed; focused `Invoke-Pester -Path .\tools\dispatch-tests\GuardSafetyMonitor.Tests.ps1 -Output Detailed` passed 43/43; full `Invoke-Pester -Path .\tools\dispatch-tests -Output Normal` passed 399/399; `git diff --check` clean.

2026-06-07 (one-line incremental p95 build measurement — ISSUE-321). Measured the still-open §13.3 one-line incremental `cargo build --workspace` p95 budget using the existing `tools/compile-timing.ps1` harness on the warm shared `A:\RustCache\target` cache, leaving no source edit in the final diff. Method: one uncounted warm-up build (`-Mode build -Iterations 1 -TimeoutSeconds 120`, 13.788s wall) resynced this worktree's fingerprints against the shared cache; then 24 counted samples, each preceded by toggling exactly one harmless trailing comment line in the leaf binary `runtime/runtime-headless/src/main.rs` (a true leaf — nothing depends on it, so each touch recompiles only `rge-runtime-headless` plus its relink), each run via `-Mode build -Iterations 1 -TimeoutSeconds 120 -JsonPath .ai\dispatch-ISSUE-321\compile-timing-incremental-NN.json`. All 24 counted samples exited 0, none timed out, none were no-ops (each cargo `Finished` exceeded the ~1.0s warm no-op floor). Nearest-rank p95 over `wall_seconds` ascending (rank `ceil(0.95×24)=23`) = **1.507s wall → PASS** vs the ≤10s incremental budget (min 1.351 / mean 1.452 / p50 1.451 / max 1.511 s). The temporary source touch was restored via `git checkout -- runtime/runtime-headless/src/main.rs` (`git diff` empty; no `.rs` in the tracked diff); all 25 timing JSON payloads stay under the gitignored `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-321/`. Updated `plans/BASELINE.md` (new §13.3 measurement entry + follow-up #3 marked measured), `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` (task 85 marked DONE). The §13.3 *clean*-build sub-gate remains a separate measured MISS (156.591s) for later remediation. **Explicit non-changes:** no final source, tests, Cargo manifests/lock, tooling/harness, architecture lints, workflows, scheduler config, dispatch automation behavior, or production behavior; no `cargo clean`; no deletion or wipe of `A:\RustCache\target`. **Verification:** warm-up build exit 0; all 24 incremental samples exit 0; `git diff -- runtime/runtime-headless/src/main.rs` empty after restore; `git diff --check` clean; `git status --short --untracked-files=no` shows only the intended docs/task-record changes; `git diff --name-only` over source/Cargo/workflow/tooling paths empty.

2026-06-07 (clean release build hotspot attribution — ISSUE-322). Attributed the still-open §13.3 clean release build MISS using Cargo's built-in `--timings` per-unit profiler, without touching source or the shared cache. Method: pointed `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` at a fresh empty isolated target `B:\sdk\rge-clean-hotspots-ISSUE-322-20260607-234243` (reusing `CARGO_HOME=A:\RustCache\cargo` / `RUSTUP_HOME=A:\RustCache\rustup` for the already-downloaded registry + toolchain so the empty target still forces a full from-scratch recompile), then ran `cargo build --workspace --release --timings`. `tools/compile-timing.ps1` was not used for this row because it intentionally exposes no `--timings`/per-unit path. The build exited 0 at wall **147.82s / cargo `Finished` 2m 27s** (`--timings` Total 147.7s) with the pre-existing `rge-ui-theme` missing-docs warnings — still a **MISS** against ≤120s (≈23% over). This fresh isolated-target run **supersedes the earlier 156.591s** as the current clean-release figure (the ~8.8s delta is ordinary run-to-run variance; both runs confirm the MISS); the historical 156.591s rows in `plans/BASELINE.md`/`Status.md`/`HANDOFF.md` were left intact (forward-only). **Finding:** the clean build is bounded by a single serial unit, not by parallelism — `cranelift-codegen` 0.131.1 compiles as one 125.64s rustc unit (85% of the 147.66s critical wall), on the critical path from 22.02s to the build end, while max concurrency was already 16 jobs on 16 cores (total CPU compile-work 1517.6s over 685 units). `cargo tree -i` confirms the critical-path attribution: `cranelift-codegen` ← `cranelift-frontend`/`cranelift-native` ← `wasmtime-internal-cranelift` ← `wasmtime` 44.0.1, reached via `rge-expr-wasm` + `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine` (scripting/plugin WASM runtime). That is the attribution path that owns the critical-path unit, not the full direct-dependency set: a direct manifest scan shows four workspace crates declare `wasmtime` directly — `rge-expr-wasm`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, `rge-script-bench` — and any dependency-scope/Pulley/feature-gate remediation must cover all four (corrected in the ISSUE-322 correction round, below). The wasm/wasmtime/cranelift family totals 340.8s CPU across 54 units. Estimated post-remediation floor ~110s (next-latest tail = `rge-editor` and `rge-tool-architecture-lints` binary links at ~109.9s), so cutting the Cranelift pole alone would likely cross under the 120s budget before other drivers matter. Recorded a ranked cost-driver table and three intentionally-small remediation candidates with risk + automation suitability in `plans/BASELINE.md` §13.3 (A: lower release `opt-level` for `cranelift-codegen` via a `[profile.release.package]` override — only after a separate task; B: wasmtime Pulley interpreter — not automatable, high runtime risk; C: feature-gate the wasm scripting stack out of the default build — not automatable). Updated `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and marked `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` task 86 DONE. The gate is **not** claimed closed (no fresh ≤120s isolated-target result). **Explicit non-changes:** no source, tests, Cargo manifests/lock, architecture lints, tooling/harness, workflows, scheduler config, or dispatch automation behavior; no remediation implemented; no `cargo clean`; no deletion/wipe of `A:\RustCache\target`. **Verification:** `cargo build --workspace --release --timings` exit 0; `cargo tree -i cranelift-codegen` / `cargo tree -i wasmtime` exit 0; scratch target verified to resolve under `B:\sdk\rge-clean-hotspots-ISSUE-322-*` and removed (2.28 GB freed); attribution artifacts (UNIT_DATA JSON, cargo-timing.html, build log) retained under gitignored `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-322/`; `git diff --check` clean; `git diff --name-only` confined to `plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, and this dispatch's protocol/audit artifacts.

2026-06-08 (clean release attribution correction — ISSUE-322 correction round 0). Codex control review returned `needs_changes` on the ISSUE-322 attribution docs with exactly two approved fixes; this correction applies only those, documentation/audit-only. **Fix 1 — false `wasmtime` provenance claim.** The attribution docs/EXEC claimed `wasmtime` is pulled by precisely two workspace crates, but a direct manifest scan (`Select-String -Path crates/*/Cargo.toml -Pattern wasmtime`, exit 0) shows four workspace crates declare `wasmtime` as a *direct* dependency: `rge-expr-wasm` (`crates/expr-wasm/Cargo.toml:16`), `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine` (`crates/runtime-wasmtime-engine/Cargo.toml:33`, behind default-on `engine_wasmtime`), `rge-script-host` (`crates/script-host/Cargo.toml:20`), and `rge-script-bench` (`crates/script-bench/Cargo.toml:39`, `winch` feature). The two-crate wording was only the **critical-path attribution path** that `cargo tree -i wasmtime` surfaces, not the complete direct-dependency set. Reworded `plans/BASELINE.md` §13.3, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and this `change.md` entry to distinguish the attribution path from the four direct dependents, and updated the Pulley/feature-gate remediation candidates so they are scoped to all four (`script-host`/`script-bench` would otherwise keep pulling cranelift/winch). **Fix 2 — undocumented local parsing command for `unit-data.json`.** The original ad-hoc extraction command that produced `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-322/unit-data.json` was not captured at measurement time; this is stated explicitly. An equivalent local extraction was re-run against the preserved `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-322/cargo-timing-clean-release.html` (the `const UNIT_DATA = [ … ];` array, lines 6414–16140): `$h = Get-Content .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-322/cargo-timing-clean-release.html -Raw; $s = $h.IndexOf('const UNIT_DATA = [') + 18; $e = $h.IndexOf("`n];", $s); ($h.Substring($s, $e - $s + 2) | ConvertFrom-Json) | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 6 | Set-Content .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-322/unit-data.regen.json -Encoding UTF8` → exit 0 (pure-PowerShell pipeline, `$?` = True). Output artifact `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-322/unit-data.regen.json` corroborated the original exactly: 685 units in both, `cranelift-codegen` 0.131.1 = 125.64s. The parsing-command provenance is now recorded in `plans/BASELINE.md` §13.3 notes and the correction EXEC packet. **Explicit non-changes:** no source, tests, benches, Cargo manifests/lock, tooling, workflows, schemas, scheduler config, or dispatch automation; no remediation implemented; no dependency-graph edit; no `cargo clean`; no deletion/wipe of `A:\RustCache\target`. **Re-verification:** `Select-String -Path crates/*/Cargo.toml -Pattern wasmtime` exit 0 (four direct deps shown); the two-crate-claim grep over the four docs (`Select-String` for the prior unqualified two-crate provenance wording across `plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`) → no matches; local UNIT_DATA extraction exit 0; `git diff --check` clean; tracked diff confined to `plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, and this dispatch's protocol/audit artifacts.

The direct `wasmtime` manifest scan exited 0 and confirmed exactly one workspace slot plus four direct crate declarations: `rge-expr-wasm`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, and `rge-script-bench`. 2026-06-08 (guard monitor parser hotfix after ISSUE-335 abort). The first guarded retry for task 93 filed ISSUE-335 and scaffolded only a template TASK packet before the guard killed the child during the Codex plan gate on another monitor parse false-abort: `Invalid JSON primitive: ok`. The parser already recovered exact quoted `"verdict"` keys with bare `ok`/`abort` values; this hotfix also accepts exact object-like bare `verdict` keys such as `{verdict:ok,reason:"healthy"}` and `{verdict:"abort",reason:"scope creep"}`. 2026-06-08 (ISSUE-345 command-palette recent ordering). Implemented task 100 in `editor-egui-host`. `EguiHost` now owns a host-local, in-memory most-recent-first list of successful command-palette activation ids from `Command::diagnostic_id()`, capped at 16 and de-duplicated by moving existing ids to the front. Palette activations record at the existing `command_palette_window` return -> `MenuCommandHandoff` enqueue point; main-menu activations do not update recents. Stale ids are ignored, disabled recent rows stay only in the remainder, and non-blank task-98 fuzzy score ordering is unchanged.
